.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit 4402987 (run: git show 4402987). It adds a LOCAL benchmark harness (scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh + scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1) and its evidence (docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/), comparing blit against robocopy for a local D: -> E: copy on a Windows box (two separate identical NVMe drives). No network is involved.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:17:  large  1 x 1 GiB           blit  539ms  robocopy  541ms  ratio 0.996 (parity)
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:18:  mixed  512MiB + 5000 files blit  934ms  robocopy  501ms  ratio 1.863
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:19:  small  10,000 x 4 KiB      blit 1388ms  robocopy  697ms  ratio 1.991
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:23:**Is this an apples-to-apples comparison, or is blit simply DOING MORE WORK than robocopy?** A 2x cost is not a defect if blit is doing strictly more. Read blit's actual local copy path in the tree (it rides the unified transfer_session since otp-11 — see crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/, the planner, and the local apply carrier / FsTransferSink) and determine what work a `blit copy <src> <dst> --yes` performs per file that `robocopy <src> <dst> /E /MT:8 /R:2 /W:2` does NOT — and vice versa. Consider specifically: content hashing/checksums or verification passes; manifest/need negotiation; metadata (mtime/permissions/ACLs/alternate data streams) preservation; per-file fsync or flush; directory pre-creation; enumeration/stat passes (one vs several); thread/parallelism defaults (robocopy /MT:8 vs whatever blit chooses); and whether blit's local path still frames records through a data-plane/tar-shard encoder rather than doing a direct file copy. Name the concrete asymmetries with file:line. If blit is doing materially more work, say so plainly — the finding as written would then be misleading.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:26:1. scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1 — the timed window. Is anything inside the Stopwatch that should be outside it (or vice versa) for one arm but not the other? Note blit is invoked as `& $BlitExe copy $Src $DestRoot` (it nests the source basename under the destination) while robocopy is pointed at `$DestRoot\<leaf>` directly (it copies contents). Do BOTH arms therefore land an identical tree and do identical work? Is the container precreation fair to both?
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:27:2. Durability: `Write-VolumeCache -DriveLetter E` is self-timed and added to the wall time for BOTH arms. Is that keyed by DESTINATION (correct) and symmetric? Could one tool leave more dirty data for the flush to absorb, and is that legitimate to charge it?
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:29:4. Validity gates: robocopy exit <8 = success, blit exit 0; landed file count verified per run; failed flush voids. Anything a broken run could slip through?
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:30:5. The recorded cold-allocation outlier: blit's warm-up (2253ms) AND first timed run (2175ms) in the `small` cell are slow, then it settles at 1386-1430; robocopy shows the same far more weakly (742 -> ~690). The README claims blit is "disproportionately sensitive to cold NTFS destination-allocation state". Is that supported, or is there a harness explanation?
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:37:I’ll review this read-only, starting with the actual `blit copy` local data path, then audit the timing/validity logic and benchmark claims against the recorded evidence and OTP12 findings. I’ll cite concrete lines from commit `4402987`.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:99:The core asymmetry is now clear: default blit does not hash or fsync each file, but it does run every entry through source manifest/readability checks, destination need-diff bookkeeping, and—in these small-file cells—an in-memory tar encode/validate/decode path. I’m checking whether that extra machinery represents stronger output semantics or merely implementation overhead, and whether robocopy is actually preserving more Windows metadata.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:101:web search: site:learn.microsoft.com robocopy /MT default 8 /COPY:DAT /DCOPY:DA alternate data streams ...
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:135:- **High — Small-file execution is radically different, including parallelism.** These files are encoded into in-memory tar shards, reparsed and copied into new buffers, then written with a `create_dir_all` check per member ([payload.rs:254](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/payload.rs:254), [tar_safety.rs:104](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs:104), [sink.rs:542](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:542)). Normal local blit uses one payload worker; only writes within the current shard use Rayon ([local.rs:602](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/local.rs:602), [sink.rs:592](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:592)). Robocopy is explicitly given `/MT:8` ([local-ab-run.ps1:60](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1:60)); that is selected eight-thread execution, not equal default parallelism. This and tar processing are plausible causes, not proven attribution.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:137:- **High — Blit is not a semantic superset of robocopy.** Blit’s Windows tar path carries only mtime—not Windows permissions/attributes, ACLs, or ADS—and the manifest omits directory entries, so empty directories and directory attributes are lost ([source.rs:223](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/source.rs:223), [sink.rs:583](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:583), [source.rs:137](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/source.rs:137)). Robocopy defaults to `/COPY:DAT` and `/DCOPY:DA`, copies ADS unless `X` is requested, and `/E` includes empty directories; neither command requests ACLs. [Microsoft’s robocopy reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/robocopy). Large blit files do go directly through `CopyFileExW` ([windows.rs:340](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:340)), which preserves attributes and ADS. [Microsoft’s CopyFileEx reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/winbase/nf-winbase-copyfileexa).
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:139:- **Medium — “No carrier” and the H7 reproduction claim are inaccurate.** Local payload bytes bypass TCP/protobuf, but manifest control frames still traverse bounded in-process channels, and small data still takes the tar path ([transport.rs:61](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/transport.rs:61), [transfer_session/local.rs:4](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/local.rs:4)). More importantly, local apply sends no per-file `NeedBatch`; it plans/applies directly and sends only `NeedComplete` ([mod.rs:3353](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:3353)). Thus [README.md:91](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/README.md:91) exercises H7’s manifest-map insertion, but not its cited per-need channel hop. It is a supplemental lead, not an H7 reproduction or pf-1 substitute.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:143:- **Medium — Validity proves counts, not bytes or tree equivalence.** Source preflight and destination validation check only file counts ([bench_win_local_ab.sh:70](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:70), [local-ab-run.ps1:78](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1:78)). Correct-count zero-length, truncated, wrong-content, or structurally different trees can pass, so [README.md:43](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/README.md:43) should say throughput parity, not “byte-for-byte.” Robocopy mismatch codes below 8 also pass, and an incomplete matrix merely logs then exits successfully ([bench_win_local_ab.sh:119](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:119), [bench_win_local_ab.sh:164](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:164)). Recorded runs were all robocopy rc=1 and n=8, so no committed run is shown broken.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:145:- **Medium — The cold-NTFS attribution is unsupported.** Warm-up is always blit, and the first timed arm is again blit; robocopy never observes the same cell-start state ([bench_win_local_ab.sh:135](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:135), [bench_win_local_ab.sh:143](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:143), [bench_win_local_ab.sh:148](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:148)). This confounds tool with order/allocation/cleanup state. Robocopy run 8 is also 743 ms versus run 1’s 742 ms ([runs.csv:19](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/runs.csv:19), [runs.csv:33](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/runs.csv:33)). The safe conclusion is “unexplained first-blit outlier”; the RUNS=8 median remains robust.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:147:- **High — “Not an otp-11 regression” is too categorical.** The cited gate was RUNS=3 on macOS/APFS, same-volume, old-vs-new blit—not Windows/NTFS or blit-vs-robocopy ([otp11 README.md:6](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp11-local-2026-07-11/README.md:6), [otp11 README.md:11](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp11-local-2026-07-11/README.md:11), [otp11 README.md:22](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp11-local-2026-07-11/README.md:22)). [Windows README.md:66](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/README.md:66) honestly admits Windows old-vs-new is unmeasured, contradicting the categorical wording at lines 52–57. The evidence proves only “no material otp-11 regression on the Mac gate.” The local cell is indeed not P2 because P2 is old-vs-new TCP push ([OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:241](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:241)); “old blit carries this Windows cost” is not established.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:149:No timed-window/tree/flush defect was found: both arms target the same precreated `$DestRoot\$leaf`, and E: flush is symmetric and properly charged ([local-ab-run.ps1:44](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1:44), [resolution.rs:141](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/resolution.rs:141), [local-ab-run.ps1:68](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1:68)). Removing flush actually enlarges the mixed/small ratios to about 2.37×/2.35×. `_Total` is conservative—unrelated disks can cause false timeouts, not conceal E: writing above the threshold—though a per-E-disk queue/rate check would be more specific. The P1 disclaimer is correct.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:151:**VERDICT:** Valid user-level plain-file shipping comparison, but not equal work. Blit does more internal bookkeeping/safety/tar work while robocopy uses more copy threads and preserves more Windows tree metadata. The ~2× median stands as genuine blit overhead; the README must narrow its H7, cold-allocation, and Windows otp-11/P2 claims.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:158:- **High — Small-file execution is radically different, including parallelism.** These files are encoded into in-memory tar shards, reparsed and copied into new buffers, then written with a `create_dir_all` check per member ([payload.rs:254](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/payload.rs:254), [tar_safety.rs:104](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/tar_safety.rs:104), [sink.rs:542](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:542)). Normal local blit uses one payload worker; only writes within the current shard use Rayon ([local.rs:602](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/local.rs:602), [sink.rs:592](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:592)). Robocopy is explicitly given `/MT:8` ([local-ab-run.ps1:60](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1:60)); that is selected eight-thread execution, not equal default parallelism. This and tar processing are plausible causes, not proven attribution.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:160:- **High — Blit is not a semantic superset of robocopy.** Blit’s Windows tar path carries only mtime—not Windows permissions/attributes, ACLs, or ADS—and the manifest omits directory entries, so empty directories and directory attributes are lost ([source.rs:223](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/source.rs:223), [sink.rs:583](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/sink.rs:583), [source.rs:137](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/source.rs:137)). Robocopy defaults to `/COPY:DAT` and `/DCOPY:DA`, copies ADS unless `X` is requested, and `/E` includes empty directories; neither command requests ACLs. [Microsoft’s robocopy reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/robocopy). Large blit files do go directly through `CopyFileExW` ([windows.rs:340](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/copy/windows.rs:340)), which preserves attributes and ADS. [Microsoft’s CopyFileEx reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/winbase/nf-winbase-copyfileexa).
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:162:- **Medium — “No carrier” and the H7 reproduction claim are inaccurate.** Local payload bytes bypass TCP/protobuf, but manifest control frames still traverse bounded in-process channels, and small data still takes the tar path ([transport.rs:61](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/transport.rs:61), [transfer_session/local.rs:4](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/local.rs:4)). More importantly, local apply sends no per-file `NeedBatch`; it plans/applies directly and sends only `NeedComplete` ([mod.rs:3353](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:3353)). Thus [README.md:91](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/README.md:91) exercises H7’s manifest-map insertion, but not its cited per-need channel hop. It is a supplemental lead, not an H7 reproduction or pf-1 substitute.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:166:- **Medium — Validity proves counts, not bytes or tree equivalence.** Source preflight and destination validation check only file counts ([bench_win_local_ab.sh:70](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:70), [local-ab-run.ps1:78](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1:78)). Correct-count zero-length, truncated, wrong-content, or structurally different trees can pass, so [README.md:43](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/README.md:43) should say throughput parity, not “byte-for-byte.” Robocopy mismatch codes below 8 also pass, and an incomplete matrix merely logs then exits successfully ([bench_win_local_ab.sh:119](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:119), [bench_win_local_ab.sh:164](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:164)). Recorded runs were all robocopy rc=1 and n=8, so no committed run is shown broken.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:168:- **Medium — The cold-NTFS attribution is unsupported.** Warm-up is always blit, and the first timed arm is again blit; robocopy never observes the same cell-start state ([bench_win_local_ab.sh:135](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:135), [bench_win_local_ab.sh:143](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:143), [bench_win_local_ab.sh:148](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh:148)). This confounds tool with order/allocation/cleanup state. Robocopy run 8 is also 743 ms versus run 1’s 742 ms ([runs.csv:19](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/runs.csv:19), [runs.csv:33](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/runs.csv:33)). The safe conclusion is “unexplained first-blit outlier”; the RUNS=8 median remains robust.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:170:- **High — “Not an otp-11 regression” is too categorical.** The cited gate was RUNS=3 on macOS/APFS, same-volume, old-vs-new blit—not Windows/NTFS or blit-vs-robocopy ([otp11 README.md:6](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp11-local-2026-07-11/README.md:6), [otp11 README.md:11](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp11-local-2026-07-11/README.md:11), [otp11 README.md:22](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp11-local-2026-07-11/README.md:22)). [Windows README.md:66](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/README.md:66) honestly admits Windows old-vs-new is unmeasured, contradicting the categorical wording at lines 52–57. The evidence proves only “no material otp-11 regression on the Mac gate.” The local cell is indeed not P2 because P2 is old-vs-new TCP push ([OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:241](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:241)); “old blit carries this Windows cost” is not established.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:172:No timed-window/tree/flush defect was found: both arms target the same precreated `$DestRoot\$leaf`, and E: flush is symmetric and properly charged ([local-ab-run.ps1:44](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1:44), [resolution.rs:141](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/resolution.rs:141), [local-ab-run.ps1:68](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1:68)). Removing flush actually enlarges the mixed/small ratios to about 2.37×/2.35×. `_Total` is conservative—unrelated disks can cause false timeouts, not conceal E: writing above the threshold—though a per-E-disk queue/rate check would be more specific. The P1 disclaimer is correct.
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:174:**VERDICT:** Valid user-level plain-file shipping comparison, but not equal work. Blit does more internal bookkeeping/safety/tar work while robocopy uses more copy threads and preserves more Windows tree metadata. The ~2× median stands as genuine blit overhead; the README must narrow its H7, cold-allocation, and Windows otp-11/P2 claims.
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:508:> On benchmark methodology: "tmp on one side, spinning rust on the
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:559:- **Converge up, not down**: per benchmark cell, the unified session
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:4195:docs/audit/2026-05-04_roadmap_audit.md:559:| Phase 4 production hardening | `MASTER_WORKFLOW.md:33` "✅ Complete" | `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md:5` "In progress. Repo-level production hardening review saved at `docs/reviews/codebase_review_2026-05-01.md`; packaging, benchmark capture, and hardening follow-ups remain." | PARTIAL. The newer review doc and DEVLOG support "in progress" framing. |
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:4766:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1148:DEVLOG.md:108:**2026-05-13 18:30:00Z** - **FEAT + SCOPE**: §3.1 / §3.3 / §5.2 / §5.4 closed (D5 modified, D6 default taken, BlitAuth + AI telemetry removed). Three owner decisions landed in one commit set: **(1) §3.1 D5 modified.** Rather than ship `--metrics` as dormant scaffolding, the daemon now emits a one-line stderr summary at end of each push/pull/pull_sync RPC when `--metrics` is on. Format: `[metrics] push ok in 1.23s (push_ops=N pull_ops=N purge_ops=N active=N errors=N)` — rsync/rclone-style operator visibility under systemd / foreground without needing the TUI. `crates/blit-daemon/src/metrics.rs` gains `log_completion(op_kind, duration, ok)` (no-op when disabled — atomics never loaded) and `format_completion_line` (extracted so the format contract is unit-testable without stderr capture). 3 new unit tests in `metrics::tests` pin the line format and error tagging. Counters themselves stay in place and feed future TUI work; the change just gives `--metrics` a real consumer for 0.1.0. **(2) §3.3 D6 default taken.** Phase 4.8 daemon FS-capability work (4.8.2 startup/idle daemon probes + 4.8.3 `blit diagnostics profile` capability probes) explicitly deferred to 0.2.0. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.8 now carries a scope-note callout marking 4.8.2 and 4.8.3 as deferred; 4.8.1 client-side `fs_capability` cache ships in 0.1.0 as today. **(3) §5.2 BlitAuth removed.** `service BlitAuth { Authenticate(AuthRequest) returns (AuthResponse) }` plus the two messages stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` (forward-compat auth passthrough hook for delegated pull) also removed — field 10 reserved against future reuse. `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` no longer sets the field. `DAEMON_CONFIG.md` Trust Model section rewritten to say "no auth, intentional, out of scope — terminate auth at the network edge" (was "no auth in 0.1.0; passthrough field exists for forward-compat"). `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` got a top-of-file auth note flagging body references to BlitAuth/`delegated_credential` as obsolete (body retained as historical design record). **(4) §5.4 AI telemetry removed.** `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` deleted entirely. References in `docs/plan/README.md`, `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` dropped. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.9 marked as removed from scope. Predictor (§2.8) is unaffected — performance history is still collected; no "analyze my history" feature is planned. **Release-plan tracker:** §3.1 / §3.2 / §3.3 all ✅ closed. §5.2 / §5.4 changed from "deferred" to "removed." `--metrics` decision (D5) modified from default; all other D-decisions taken at their plan defaults. **Net release-blocker count:** §2.6 (hardware-bound 10 GbE benchmark capture) is the last open P0. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+3 metrics tests); `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check` clean.
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:4768:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1150:DEVLOG.md:116:**2026-05-07 14:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup #2 catching review of `8d43e4d` — the RELEASE_PLAN_v2 itself was still presenting closed P0s as open work, which was a serious risk because `docs/plan/README.md` points new agents at it as the live source of truth for what remains before shipping. **Medium**: added a top-of-file "Closure tracker (last updated 2026-05-07)" block with a one-row-per-section table showing closing commits for §2.1/§2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.7/§2.8 and §4, plus pending markers for §2.6 (hardware-bound benchmark — last open P0) and §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 (P1, doc-only). Each P0 section now has its own per-section `**Status (2026-05-07):** ✅ Closed <commit>` block at the top so a reader landing on a specific section sees current state without scrolling back to the top. §2.5 line 265 also still showed the old `blit completions remote <prefix>` syntax in the recommendation paragraph — corrected to the actual `<REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` form, matching the existing line-21 manpage. §6 commit-sequence rewritten as a status table with closing commits per row (P0 done except step 2 §2.6; P1 deferrable). §7 decisions table flipped from "Default recommendation" / "Owner" columns to "Default" / "Outcome" with closing commits where taken — D1/D2/D3/D7/D8/D9 ✅ taken, D4/D5/D6 still open. Test totals updated from the 2026-05-04 baseline (383) to current (407, after R41-R45 review-fix coverage). Footer "Last updated" + "Next review" rewritten. **Low**: `docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md:83` Success Criteria still said `blit-cli mirror -p -v local remote`; updated to `blit mirror`. Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:4868:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3200:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1883:**2026-05-13 18:30:00Z** - **FEAT + SCOPE**: §3.1 / §3.3 / §5.2 / §5.4 closed (D5 modified, D6 default taken, BlitAuth + AI telemetry removed). References in `docs/plan/README.md`, `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` dropped. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.9 marked as removed from scope. .review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:4891:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3239:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2113:**2026-05-07 14:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup #2 catching review of `8d43e4d` — the RELEASE_PLAN_v2 itself was still presenting closed P0s as open work, which was a serious risk because `docs/plan/README.md` points new agents at it as the live source of truth for what remains before shipping. **Medium**: added a top-of-file "Closure tracker (last updated 2026-05-07)" block with a one-row-per-section table showing closing commits for §2.1/§2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.7/§2.8 and §4, plus pending markers for §2.6 (hardware-bound benchmark — last open P0) and §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 (P1, doc-only). Each P0 section now has its own per-section `**Status (2026-05-07):** ✅ Closed <commit>` block at the top so a reader landing on a specific section sees current state without scrolling back to the top. §2.5 line 265 also still showed the old `blit completions remote <prefix>` syntax in the recommendation paragraph — corrected to the actual `<REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` form, matching the existing line-21 manpage. §6 commit-sequence rewritten as a status table with closing commits per row (P0 done except step 2 §2.6; P1 deferrable). §7 decisions table flipped from "Default recommendation" / "Owner" columns to "Default" / "Outcome" with closing commits where taken — D1/D2/D3/D7/D8/D9 ✅ taken, D4/D5/D6 still open. Test totals updated from the 2026-05-04 baseline (383) to current (407, after R41-R45 review-fix coverage). Footer "Last updated" + "Next review" rewritten. **Low**: `docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md:83` Success Criteria still said `blit-cli mirror -p -v local remote`; updated to `blit mirror`. Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:5110:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1883:**2026-05-13 18:30:00Z** - **FEAT + SCOPE**: §3.1 / §3.3 / §5.2 / §5.4 closed (D5 modified, D6 default taken, BlitAuth + AI telemetry removed). Three owner decisions landed in one commit set: **(1) §3.1 D5 modified.** Rather than ship `--metrics` as dormant scaffolding, the daemon now emits a one-line stderr summary at end of each push/pull/pull_sync RPC when `--metrics` is on. Format: `[metrics] push ok in 1.23s (push_ops=N pull_ops=N purge_ops=N active=N errors=N)` — rsync/rclone-style operator visibility under systemd / foreground without needing the TUI. `crates/blit-daemon/src/metrics.rs` gains `log_completion(op_kind, duration, ok)` (no-op when disabled — atomics never loaded) and `format_completion_line` (extracted so the format contract is unit-testable without stderr capture). 3 new unit tests in `metrics::tests` pin the line format and error tagging. Counters themselves stay in place and feed future TUI work; the change just gives `--metrics` a real consumer for 0.1.0. **(2) §3.3 D6 default taken.** Phase 4.8 daemon FS-capability work (4.8.2 startup/idle daemon probes + 4.8.3 `blit diagnostics profile` capability probes) explicitly deferred to 0.2.0. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.8 now carries a scope-note callout marking 4.8.2 and 4.8.3 as deferred; 4.8.1 client-side `fs_capability` cache ships in 0.1.0 as today. **(3) §5.2 BlitAuth removed.** `service BlitAuth { Authenticate(AuthRequest) returns (AuthResponse) }` plus the two messages stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` (forward-compat auth passthrough hook for delegated pull) also removed — field 10 reserved against future reuse. `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` no longer sets the field. `DAEMON_CONFIG.md` Trust Model section rewritten to say "no auth, intentional, out of scope — terminate auth at the network edge" (was "no auth in 0.1.0; passthrough field exists for forward-compat"). `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` got a top-of-file auth note flagging body references to BlitAuth/`delegated_credential` as obsolete (body retained as historical design record). **(4) §5.4 AI telemetry removed.** `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` deleted entirely. References in `docs/plan/README.md`, `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` dropped. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.9 marked as removed from scope. Predictor (§2.8) is unaffected — performance history is still collected; no "analyze my history" feature is planned. **Release-plan tracker:** §3.1 / §3.2 / §3.3 all ✅ closed. §5.2 / §5.4 changed from "deferred" to "removed." `--metrics` decision (D5) modified from default; all other D-decisions taken at their plan defaults. **Net release-blocker count:** §2.6 (hardware-bound 10 GbE benchmark capture) is the last open P0. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+3 metrics tests); `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check` clean.
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:5112:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1891:**2026-05-07 14:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup #2 catching review of `8d43e4d` — the RELEASE_PLAN_v2 itself was still presenting closed P0s as open work, which was a serious risk because `docs/plan/README.md` points new agents at it as the live source of truth for what remains before shipping. **Medium**: added a top-of-file "Closure tracker (last updated 2026-05-07)" block with a one-row-per-section table showing closing commits for §2.1/§2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.7/§2.8 and §4, plus pending markers for §2.6 (hardware-bound benchmark — last open P0) and §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 (P1, doc-only). Test totals updated from the 2026-05-04 baseline (383) to current (407, after R41-R45 review-fix coverage). Footer "Last updated" + "Next review" rewritten. **Low**: `docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md:83` Success Criteria still said `blit-cli mirror -p -v local remote`; updated to `blit mirror`. Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:5113:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1893:**2026-05-05 22:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup to the §2.2+§4 sweep — three doc misses caught by review of commit `aac13bf`. **Medium**: `docs/plan/BENCHMARK_10GBE_PLAN.md` lines 36 + 99-100 still pointed users at `./target/release/blit-cli` and `/tmp/blit-cli` for the active 10 GbE benchmark playbook (the plan that's currently driving the §2.6 capture). Replaced with `blit` paths. **Medium**: `docs/WHITEPAPER.md:738-739` build/run example used the same stale `target/release/blit-cli`; rewritten. WHITEPAPER:7 also still described `blit-cli` as the "user binary" without noting the `[[bin]] name = "blit"` rename + admin-verb merge — corrected. **Low**: `README.md:149` documented the daemon-backed completion fallback as `blit completions remote <prefix>`, but the actual CLI signature is `blit completions remote <REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` (matching `docs/cli/blit.1.md:21`). Rewrote the README line to show the correct positional `<REMOTE>` plus `--prefix` flag form. Two intentionally-left-alone files: `docs/plan/POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md:85` (real-world incident report dated 2026-05-01 capturing the exact pre-rename command that triggered the bug — historical evidence) and `docs/bugs/{single-file-source-silent-noop,copy-destination-semantics}.md` (bug reports also dated against the pre-rename binary). Verified by `grep -rn 'release/blit-cli\|/tmp/blit-cli\|--bin blit-cli'` across `docs/`, `scripts/`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` — only the three intentional historical references remain. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:5128:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1923:**2026-05-03 01:25:00Z** - **DOCS/TOOLS**: Completed Phase 3 cleanup/harness items from `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` except live benchmark execution. Documented `RemoteTransferSource` as the explicit legacy `--relay-via-cli` primitive now that default remote→remote uses destination-side `DelegatedPull`. Added `scripts/bench_remote_remote.sh` to compare default direct delegation against explicit relay using identical remote payloads and the existing `BLIT_TEST_COUNTER_FILE` byte-path counter. Added `docs/perf/remote_remote_benchmarks.md` as the results capture template. Updated the plan status/checklist; remaining unchecked item is running the benchmark on the target network and filling real numbers. **Validation**: `cargo fmt -- --check`; `cargo test -p blit-cli remote_remote_direct`; `bash -n scripts/bench_remote_remote.sh`.

.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:10873:5113:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1893:**2026-05-05 22:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup to the §2.2+§4 sweep — three doc misses caught by review of commit `aac13bf`. **Medium**: `docs/plan/BENCHMARK_10GBE_PLAN.md` lines 36 + 99-100 still pointed users at `./target/release/blit-cli` and `/tmp/blit-cli` for the active 10 GbE benchmark playbook (the plan that's currently driving the §2.6 capture). WHITEPAPER:7 also still described `blit-cli` as the "user binary" without noting the `[[bin]] name = "blit"` rename + admin-verb merge — corrected. **Low**: `README.md:149` documented the daemon-backed completion fallback as `blit completions remote <prefix>`, but the actual CLI signature is `blit completions remote <REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` (matching `docs/cli/blit.1.md:21`). Verified by `grep -rn 'release/blit-cli\|/tmp/blit-cli\|--bin blit-cli'` across `docs/`, `scripts/`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` — only the three intentional historical references remain. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).

.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:11193:  the better direction per cell ±10%); benchmark verdict cells must
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:11214:- **10 GbE benchmark session DONE (2026-07-04/05)** — REV4 sign-off
.review/results/otp-3-transfer-session-core.codex.md:2681:- Why: the dead draft busy-waits on EAGAIN (would be rewritten, not revived); wiring needs a raw-fd special case beside a permanent buffered fallback; the CPU saving is a fraction of one core, Linux-only, and unmeasured. Revisit gate: 10 GbE benchmarks showing receive-side CPU saturation — design notes preserved in the eval doc.
.review/results/otp-3-transfer-session-core.codex.md:2691:- Decision: `docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` is **Active**. Owner approved with four parameters that bind the design: (q1) **no probe-then-go phase** — the engine starts moving within ~1s at conservative defaults bounded by the receiver ceiling and the tuner adjusts dials live from the first byte; the "small-transfer threshold" is obviated (no probe to skip), and the **planner** carries the workload-shape judgment (file count vs bytes) that the old size gate proxied. (q2) the receiver advertises a **rich capacity profile** (CPU cores, disk class, load, max streams, drain estimate) — "more data serves the ubergoal"; do not minimize the negotiation payload. (q3) engine type **deferred to the agent**, who recommends a new src/dst-agnostic `TransferEngine` + a local adapter over renaming `TransferOrchestrator` in place — ratified at `ue-1c`. (q4) `ue-2` (mid-transfer stream add/drop via PR3's resize proto) is **in scope at Active**, sequenced last; 11 months of owner benchmarking is the justification, the 10 GbE rig is sign-off not a gate.
.review/results/otp-3-transfer-session-core.codex.md:2747:- Decision: The D-2026-06-12-1 revisit gate ("receive-side CPU saturation") is **declared met by the owner** (2026-07-05): a UniFi UNAS 8 Pro daemon target whose CPU cannot saturate 10 GbE even from SSD cache. Zero-copy receive is unparked as sanctioned FAST work. Two clarifications: (a) the dead `zero_copy.rs` module still gets deleted as ratified — its EAGAIN busy-wait draft is a rewrite, not a revival (eval doc); (b) the capability returns the one-path way (owner exchange 2026-07-05): a **runtime-selected write strategy inside the unified receive sink** — the eval doc's revisit design (`AsyncFd`-readiness splice loop beside the buffered relay, selected when the reader is a raw TcpStream and the payload is a file record, buffered relay as universal fallback), capability-gated by kernel/fs support, identical in both roles — never a side path. Sequenced after ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover (otp-10) as its own slice set; the UNAS is the measurement rig and the symmetric-endpoint benchmark rule (D-2026-07-05-2 era methodology) applies to its cells.
.review/results/otp-3-transfer-session-core.codex.md:2749:- Supersedes: the STATE Blocked "zero-copy option a/b/c" question and the "zero-copy revisit verdict" item among the four 10 GbE owner declarations (both resolved by this entry); amends D-2026-06-12-1's revisit-gate framing from "10 GbE benchmarks showing receive-side CPU saturation" to "a CPU-bound receiver exists" (annotated in the eval doc). D-2026-06-12-1's deletion of the dead module stands.

.review/results/otp-3-transfer-session-core.codex.md:3439:- **Converge up, not down**: per benchmark cell, the unified session

.review/results/otp12-perf-findings-r3.codex.md:122:- **INFO — [OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:37](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:37): all other cited benchmark figures reconcile.** This includes both P1 tables and spreads, 1.313/1.247 corroboration, P2’s 1.105/1.149/1.201, the 1.044 control, and the 11/12 range. D2 is read correctly: every relevant P1 spread is below 25%, so no registered escalation triggers. The necessary/sufficient wording, new-arm final-build wording, and signed H5 event definition are genuinely repaired; H1/H4/H5/H6 exist, and H2 is correctly contradicted.

.review/results/w9-3-test-harness-builder.codex.md:7114:docs/audit/DESIGN_FINDINGS_2026-06-11_PHASE_B.md:1191:Verified-clean areas (Phase C input): (1) docs/ARCHITECTURE.md blit-core module table (lines 44-64) — every listed module (remote::transfer::{pipeline,source,sink,payload}, orchestrator, mirror_planner, enumeration, copy, checksum, change_journal, tar_stream, auto_tune, perf_predictor, perf_history, fs_capability) exists in crates/blit-core/src; its blit-cli structure section matches the actual files; no ghosts found. (2) AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md infrastructure claims all check out: .claude/commands/ has all six slash commands, scripts/agent/{context,check-docs,precompact,catchup}.sh and scripts/windows/run-blit-tests.ps1 exist, .agents/ layout (state.md/decisions.md pointer stubs, repo-map.json, skills/{catchup,handoff}) matches §0/§3. (3) payload.rs:67-76 doc comment is the accurate counter-model for the FileStream design and is correct. (4) Plan-doc status hygiene is mostly good: UNIFIED_RECEIVE_PIPELINE.md, LOCAL_TRANSFER_HEURISTICS.md, PIPELINE_UNIFICATION.md, BENCHMARK_10GBE_PLAN.md all correctly marked Historical. (5) README.md, docs/API.md ghost-scans clean (no transfer_engine/TransferFacade/FileStream/heartbeat claims). (6) STATE.md itself is internally consistent and current. Partially refuted map claims corrected in findings: TransferOrchestrator exists (drift-agents-md finding includes the map erratum); WORKFLOW_PHASE_2's fast-path-routing row and tests/integration/local_transfers.rs are real, so only the streaming-planner rows are ghosts. Not covered / deliberately skipped: grpc_fallback.rs:68-71 'handled by HTTP/2 keepalive + cancel-on-disconnect' coverage claim (substantively owned by queued slice-2 watchdog re-scope; only the comment side would be drift and it will be rewritten by that slice); blit-tui internals (light-pass rule — only a perf_history identifier scan done); docs/WHITEPAPER.md, docs/DAEMON_CONFIG.md, docs/perf/remote_remote_benchmarks.md not audited beyond identifier grep; greenfield_plan_v6.md:193 mentions transfer_engine in its Phase 0 port directive (Active plan, intent-language — noted but not filed; fix it alongside the AGENTS.md slice if desired); docs/audit/findings/drift-phases.md (2026-06-04) already documents the WORKFLOW_PHASE_2 streaming-planner gap — my finding 1 confirms it independently and adds the still-unfixed Shipped header + manpage propagation.
.review/results/w9-3-test-harness-builder.codex.md:7712:  1154	- **drift-shipped-docs-cite-gitignored-absent-benchmark-logs** — Shipped gate docs (WORKFLOW_PHASE_2.5, WORKFLOW_PHASE_3) cite specific logs/ artifacts as acceptance evidence, but logs/ is gitignored and absent
.review/results/otp-2.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit e757dcc (run: git show e757dcc). It implements ONE_TRANSFER_PATH slice otp-2 (docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md slice 2: 'symmetric baseline — harness + rig, no production code'): scripts/bench_otp2_baseline.sh (the corrected benchmark harness) plus recorded evidence in docs/bench/otp2-baseline-2026-07-10/ (README with rig facts, 12 verdict-cell medians, methodology findings from two committed probe runs) and a finding doc .review/findings/otp-2-symmetric-baseline.md. Check: (1) the harness script's correctness as bash — quoting, error handling, the drain/purge/sync ordering, whether the timed window actually measures what the README claims, portability hazards on macOS bash 3.2; (2) whether the methodology matches the plan's otp-2 requirements (same-fs disk-to-disk verdict cells, cold caches, tmpfs rows re-labeled wire-reference only, per-cell per-direction OLD-path baseline) and D-2026-07-04-4 (no competitor-relative rows); (3) internal coherence of the README's numbers vs runs.csv/summary.csv (medians correct, claims supported by the probe CSVs); (4) the open question routed to the owner (cross-direction acceptance bar vs hardware-asymmetric rig) — is it stated fairly, does anything in the commit silently pre-adjudicate it; (5) no contradiction with docs/DECISIONS.md or docs/STATE.md; (6) no production code touched. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

It adds docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md (Status: Draft, plan-doc only, no code) plus a STATE.md queue entry. This is a PLAN review per docs/agent/GPT_REVIEW_LOOP.md: check internal coherence (goal vs acceptance criteria vs slices vs constraints), no contradiction with docs/DECISIONS.md (esp. D-2026-06-20-1 bounded-unilateral dial contract, D-2026-07-04-1 review loop, D-2026-06-12-1 zero-copy) or docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE_REV4.md, and that the measured claims match the repo's benchmark records (DEVLOG 2026-07-05 entries; logs/ CSVs are gitignored — the DEVLOG digest is the in-repo record). Key principle the doc must consistently embody (owner, 2026-07-05): goals are hardware-ceiling-driven, never competitor-relative; other tools are tripwires only. Flag any residual competitor-relative framing, any slice not sized for one review-loop pass, any acceptance criterion that is not observable/testable, and any invariant risk (SIMPLE: dial stays single tuning owner; RELIABLE: REV4 invariants; wire-compat gate on sf-6). Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:792:- Decision: `docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` is **Active**. Owner approved with four parameters that bind the design: (q1) **no probe-then-go phase** — the engine starts moving within ~1s at conservative defaults bounded by the receiver ceiling and the tuner adjusts dials live from the first byte; the "small-transfer threshold" is obviated (no probe to skip), and the **planner** carries the workload-shape judgment (file count vs bytes) that the old size gate proxied. (q2) the receiver advertises a **rich capacity profile** (CPU cores, disk class, load, max streams, drain estimate) — "more data serves the ubergoal"; do not minimize the negotiation payload. (q3) engine type **deferred to the agent**, who recommends a new src/dst-agnostic `TransferEngine` + a local adapter over renaming `TransferOrchestrator` in place — ratified at `ue-1c`. (q4) `ue-2` (mid-transfer stream add/drop via PR3's resize proto) is **in scope at Active**, sequenced last; 11 months of owner benchmarking is the justification, the 10 GbE rig is sign-off not a gate.

It adds docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md (Status: Draft, plan-doc only, no code) plus a STATE.md queue entry. This is a PLAN review per docs/agent/GPT_REVIEW_LOOP.md: check internal coherence (goal vs acceptance criteria vs slices vs constraints), no contradiction with docs/DECISIONS.md (esp. D-2026-06-20-1 bounded-unilateral dial contract, D-2026-07-04-1 review loop, D-2026-06-12-1 zero-copy) or docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE_REV4.md, and that the measured claims match the repo's benchmark records (DEVLOG 2026-07-05 entries; logs/ CSVs are gitignored — the DEVLOG digest is the in-repo record). Key principle the doc must consistently embody (owner, 2026-07-05): goals are hardware-ceiling-driven, never competitor-relative; other tools are tripwires only. Flag any residual competitor-relative framing, any slice not sized for one review-loop pass, any acceptance criterion that is not observable/testable, and any invariant risk (SIMPLE: dial stays single tuning owner; RELIABLE: REV4 invariants; wire-compat gate on sf-6). Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1797:**2026-07-05 00:34:30Z** - **BENCHMARK SESSION (10 GbE, TrueNAS↔Arch, claude + owner)**: The owner-called REV4 sign-off session ran end-to-end on the recorded pair (skippy = TrueNAS SCALE, glibc 2.36, binaries exec'd from the pool because /tmp and /home are noexec; client = netwatch-01 Arch, enp6s0 10 GbE). Env: **MTU 9000 both ends** (client NM profile was silently 1500 — set to 9000 via profile edit + full `connection up` reactivation after `device reapply` refused on immutable fields; 8972-byte DF probe green), **iperf3 baseline 9.88 Gbit/s single-stream / 9.91 both directions** — the parity reference (~1.24 GB/s). **Results (validated matrix, avg of 3 true cold runs each)**: TCP push 1 GiB **907 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** (first payload 14.5 ms), TCP pull 1 GiB **904 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** — both at the wire ceiling; gRPC-fallback push 1 GiB 1070 ms ≈ 8.0 Gbit/s (first payload 1.0–2.2 s scaling with manifest size — the design-4 no-mid-manifest-negotiation property, deliberate); gRPC pull ≈ TCP pull on large (880 vs 904 ms — 1 MiB-chunked fallback is wire-competitive on clean LAN), TCP 31% faster on 10k-small pulls; push 10k×4 KiB 2.29 s (~4.4k files/s) vs pull 433 ms (~23k files/s — push side per-file daemon work is the gap, w4-4/w9 territory); local blit beats rsync 2.3× on 1 GiB (157 vs 359 ms), no-op mirror 3 ms vs rsync 42 ms. **ue-1 loopback parity band: HOLDS** — local/push/pull per workload: large 195/178/162 ms, small 83/147/116, mixed 119/163/104; worst spread 1.8×, no 10×/2× gap (record: logs/bench_10gbe_20260704T201804/loopback_parity_band.csv). **Reverse direction validated** (daemon on client, skippy as pusher/puller through ufw): push 7.25 Gbit/s data-plane (first payload 3.9 ms), pull 9.75 Gbit/s. **ue-2 observation**: no organic mid-transfer resize triggered anywhere — a single stream saturates 10 GbE (daemon log: 1 GiB stream at 9.69 Gbit/s; negotiated extra streams close cleanly at 0 bytes), including under deliberate 2-concurrent-push contention (8.30+3.21 Gbit/s, clean completion); organic resize needs a constrained/variable wire — owner call whether the ue-r2-2 deterministic suite + this no-wedge evidence satisfies the gate. **Zero-copy datapoint** (D-2026-06-12-1 revisit): every transfer reported `zero-copy 0 bytes` yet the wire is saturated — splice wiring buys nothing at 10 GbE; CPU is not the bottleneck. **Methodology recorded**: engine-vs-wire isolation by design — tmpfs local ends, ZFS absorbing writes async, ARC-warm re-reads, no sync/ARC-eviction between runs (owner asked; disk-path variants — post-push `zpool sync` timing, cold-ARC pulls via `primarycache` — deferred as owner-gated follow-ups). **Session findings fixed en route**: bench_10gbe.sh had never survived past its local phase — stale endpoint grammar (missing load-bearing trailing slash) + daemon-only `--force-grpc-data` flag on the client, both silent aborts under run_timed's `2>/dev/null` + `set -e` (`b9befb8`); codex review of that fix returned 2 High (run_timed re-runs measure no-ops; shared module root lets gRPC pushes no-op and de-isolates pull labels) — both accepted, fixed `92d6326` (run_timed_fresh / per-run push subdirs / per-workload subpath pulls), validated by a full re-run (avg≈best every row). Verdicts: `.review/results/bench-script-fix.{codex,gpt-verdict}.md`. **New foot-gun filed**: `blit copy src_large dst` with an existing local dir named without `./` parses as an mDNS discovery endpoint and errors "remote source must include a module or root" (blit-app endpoints.rs) — STATE open question. Benchmark payload cleaned off the pool; binaries + bench.toml left at /mnt/generic-pool/video/blit-bin/ for future sessions. **Owner calls now pending**: ue-1 declaration (evidence says band holds), ue-2 declaration, zero-copy revisit verdict, REV4 → Shipped flip, and the GitHub push (w9-3 Windows CI check still queued behind it).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1801:**2026-07-04 22:09:54Z** - **OWNER Q&A + PUSH (benchmark host plan, claude)**: Owner pushed `master` → `github` at `10d89e0` (first push carrying the w9-1/w9-4 ungated suites since the Windows triage — windows-latest CI on it is the "meaningfully green" check; `origin` gitea mirror not pushed, lags). Benchmark host question settled in Q&A: the 10 GbE **sign-off pair is TrueNAS (skippy) ↔ Arch client, all-Linux** — the zero-copy/splice revisit gate (D-2026-06-12-1) needs a Linux consumer, and ue-1's parity band should measure the engine without Windows I/O confounders (win_fs, TCP autotuning, filter drivers). Key facts recorded: the client box **dual-boots Win 11/Arch on identical hardware** (so a later TrueNAS→Win 11 bare-metal pull in the same hardware window gives a clean platform delta as a deployment-parity datapoint, not a gate), and a **Windows VM on the Arch install** exists for Windows-specific functional checks — explicitly never for perf numbers (virtio/NAT skews throughput). iperf3 baseline per pair before any Blit numbers. Plan recorded in STATE.md Queue item 2. Handoff commit follows; push of the handoff itself pending the owner's ref-listing go per push policy.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1817:**2026-07-04 13:53:22Z** - **DECISIONS (owner Q&A, claude)**: The owner asked for the four standing questions "one at a time, no idea what these refer to" — each was presented in plain English with options and answered: (1) **commit erratum → leave as-is** (D-2026-07-04-2; mirrors the D-2026-06-07-1 no-rewrite calculus — two bisect-skippable commits beat force-pushing shared history); (2) **10 GbE session → "soon, but keep coding first"** (STATE Blocked reworded: not a daily blocker; owner will call "benchmark"); (3) **D-2026-06-20-1 stale warmup/size-gate wording → "follow the existing pattern"** — the ledger's own precedent (D-2026-06-20-2's veto annotation, D-2026-06-20-6's struck scope clause) IS edit-in-place-with-annotation, so the superseded framings are struck with pointers to -2 q1 and REV4/-5 (bounded-unilateral untouched — still true), and -5's "remains an open question" note resolved; (4) **supports_cancellation → flip it** (D-2026-07-04-3): CancelJob + TUI F2 will work on attached Push/PullSync transfers; policy-only after w4-3's race wiring; contract change (exit 2→0) recorded; implementation queued as **w4-5-supports-cancellation-flip**, now the topmost open REVIEW.md row. Batch `2a21d6f` through the codex loop per D-2026-07-04-1: **NEEDS FIXES (1 Medium + 1 Low, both STATE.md coherence)** — the Now bullet still called the erratum an open owner call, and the queue rewrite dropped the coder's-pick clause (design-3-vs-w2-2 ordering contradiction); both accepted, fixed `a928193`. The decision content itself passed all cross-checks (ledger consistency, w4-3 scope-note agreement, strike precision). check-docs.sh green. All on master, unpushed.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1883:**2026-05-13 18:30:00Z** - **FEAT + SCOPE**: §3.1 / §3.3 / §5.2 / §5.4 closed (D5 modified, D6 default taken, BlitAuth + AI telemetry removed). Three owner decisions landed in one commit set: **(1) §3.1 D5 modified.** Rather than ship `--metrics` as dormant scaffolding, the daemon now emits a one-line stderr summary at end of each push/pull/pull_sync RPC when `--metrics` is on. Format: `[metrics] push ok in 1.23s (push_ops=N pull_ops=N purge_ops=N active=N errors=N)` — rsync/rclone-style operator visibility under systemd / foreground without needing the TUI. `crates/blit-daemon/src/metrics.rs` gains `log_completion(op_kind, duration, ok)` (no-op when disabled — atomics never loaded) and `format_completion_line` (extracted so the format contract is unit-testable without stderr capture). 3 new unit tests in `metrics::tests` pin the line format and error tagging. Counters themselves stay in place and feed future TUI work; the change just gives `--metrics` a real consumer for 0.1.0. **(2) §3.3 D6 default taken.** Phase 4.8 daemon FS-capability work (4.8.2 startup/idle daemon probes + 4.8.3 `blit diagnostics profile` capability probes) explicitly deferred to 0.2.0. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.8 now carries a scope-note callout marking 4.8.2 and 4.8.3 as deferred; 4.8.1 client-side `fs_capability` cache ships in 0.1.0 as today. **(3) §5.2 BlitAuth removed.** `service BlitAuth { Authenticate(AuthRequest) returns (AuthResponse) }` plus the two messages stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` (forward-compat auth passthrough hook for delegated pull) also removed — field 10 reserved against future reuse. `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` no longer sets the field. `DAEMON_CONFIG.md` Trust Model section rewritten to say "no auth, intentional, out of scope — terminate auth at the network edge" (was "no auth in 0.1.0; passthrough field exists for forward-compat"). `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` got a top-of-file auth note flagging body references to BlitAuth/`delegated_credential` as obsolete (body retained as historical design record). **(4) §5.4 AI telemetry removed.** `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` deleted entirely. References in `docs/plan/README.md`, `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` dropped. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.9 marked as removed from scope. Predictor (§2.8) is unaffected — performance history is still collected; no "analyze my history" feature is planned. **Release-plan tracker:** §3.1 / §3.2 / §3.3 all ✅ closed. §5.2 / §5.4 changed from "deferred" to "removed." `--metrics` decision (D5) modified from default; all other D-decisions taken at their plan defaults. **Net release-blocker count:** §2.6 (hardware-bound 10 GbE benchmark capture) is the last open P0. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+3 metrics tests); `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check` clean.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1891:**2026-05-07 14:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup #2 catching review of `8d43e4d` — the RELEASE_PLAN_v2 itself was still presenting closed P0s as open work, which was a serious risk because `docs/plan/README.md` points new agents at it as the live source of truth for what remains before shipping. **Medium**: added a top-of-file "Closure tracker (last updated 2026-05-07)" block with a one-row-per-section table showing closing commits for §2.1/§2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.7/§2.8 and §4, plus pending markers for §2.6 (hardware-bound benchmark — last open P0) and §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 (P1, doc-only). Each P0 section now has its own per-section `**Status (2026-05-07):** ✅ Closed <commit>` block at the top so a reader landing on a specific section sees current state without scrolling back to the top. §2.5 line 265 also still showed the old `blit completions remote <prefix>` syntax in the recommendation paragraph — corrected to the actual `<REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` form, matching the existing line-21 manpage. §6 commit-sequence rewritten as a status table with closing commits per row (P0 done except step 2 §2.6; P1 deferrable). §7 decisions table flipped from "Default recommendation" / "Owner" columns to "Default" / "Outcome" with closing commits where taken — D1/D2/D3/D7/D8/D9 ✅ taken, D4/D5/D6 still open. Test totals updated from the 2026-05-04 baseline (383) to current (407, after R41-R45 review-fix coverage). Footer "Last updated" + "Next review" rewritten. **Low**: `docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md:83` Success Criteria still said `blit-cli mirror -p -v local remote`; updated to `blit mirror`. Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1893:**2026-05-05 22:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup to the §2.2+§4 sweep — three doc misses caught by review of commit `aac13bf`. **Medium**: `docs/plan/BENCHMARK_10GBE_PLAN.md` lines 36 + 99-100 still pointed users at `./target/release/blit-cli` and `/tmp/blit-cli` for the active 10 GbE benchmark playbook (the plan that's currently driving the §2.6 capture). Replaced with `blit` paths. **Medium**: `docs/WHITEPAPER.md:738-739` build/run example used the same stale `target/release/blit-cli`; rewritten. WHITEPAPER:7 also still described `blit-cli` as the "user binary" without noting the `[[bin]] name = "blit"` rename + admin-verb merge — corrected. **Low**: `README.md:149` documented the daemon-backed completion fallback as `blit completions remote <prefix>`, but the actual CLI signature is `blit completions remote <REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` (matching `docs/cli/blit.1.md:21`). Rewrote the README line to show the correct positional `<REMOTE>` plus `--prefix` flag form. Two intentionally-left-alone files: `docs/plan/POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md:85` (real-world incident report dated 2026-05-01 capturing the exact pre-rename command that triggered the bug — historical evidence) and `docs/bugs/{single-file-source-silent-noop,copy-destination-semantics}.md` (bug reports also dated against the pre-rename binary). Verified by `grep -rn 'release/blit-cli\|/tmp/blit-cli\|--bin blit-cli'` across `docs/`, `scripts/`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` — only the three intentional historical references remain. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1923:**2026-05-03 01:25:00Z** - **DOCS/TOOLS**: Completed Phase 3 cleanup/harness items from `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` except live benchmark execution. Documented `RemoteTransferSource` as the explicit legacy `--relay-via-cli` primitive now that default remote→remote uses destination-side `DelegatedPull`. Added `scripts/bench_remote_remote.sh` to compare default direct delegation against explicit relay using identical remote payloads and the existing `BLIT_TEST_COUNTER_FILE` byte-path counter. Added `docs/perf/remote_remote_benchmarks.md` as the results capture template. Updated the plan status/checklist; remaining unchecked item is running the benchmark on the target network and filling real numbers. **Validation**: `cargo fmt -- --check`; `cargo test -p blit-cli remote_remote_direct`; `bash -n scripts/bench_remote_remote.sh`.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1983:**2026-04-14 20:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Completed unified-pipeline remediation across all transfer paths. Previously only local→local and gRPC-fallback push routed through `execute_sink_pipeline`; multi-stream TCP push and both daemon pull paths had bespoke dispatch code. Added `execute_sink_pipeline_streaming(source, sinks, payload_rx, prefetch, progress)` in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs` and made the one-shot form a thin wrapper. Rewrote `MultiStreamSender` in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs` as a lightweight `payload_tx` + `JoinHandle` shell — deleted `data_plane_sink_worker`, batch-splitting helpers, and `StreamStats` (~130 lines). Converted both daemon pull paths in `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs` to use the pipeline; deleted `handle_pull_stream` and `handle_pull_stream_streaming` (~125 lines). Deleted dead modules: `transfer_engine.rs` (WorkerFactory trait + task-channel infra), `transfer_facade/` (TaskAggregator, PlannerEvent, build_local_plan, stream_local_plan), and `RetryableTask` from `transfer_plan.rs` — 1139 lines total. Updated `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` with the unified-pipeline diagram and source/sink matrix. Added `blit(1)` command-summary hints and expanded `TransferArgs` doc-comments for `--help`. **Tests**: 152 passed (87 core + 65 integration), clippy clean. **Remaining**: end-to-end 10 GbE benchmark runs.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1985:**2026-04-07 18:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Completed all Phase 4 production hardening items that don't require hardware. Created `CHANGELOG.md` with full 0.1.0 feature inventory. Added `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` scoping statistical anomaly detection, tuning recommendations, and diagnostics (all local, no ML, opt-in). Created `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with cargo fmt/clippy, tri-platform tests, and release artifact builds. Added `scripts/build-release.sh` and `scripts/windows/build-release.ps1`. Updated TODO to mark packaging, CI, telemetry, logging review, install docs, and release notes complete. **Remaining**: benchmark runs (need hardware), RDMA (post-release), ReFS privilege investigation (Windows).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2019:**2026-07-05 00:34:30Z** - **BENCHMARK SESSION (10 GbE, TrueNAS↔Arch, claude + owner)**: The owner-called REV4 sign-off session ran end-to-end on the recorded pair (skippy = TrueNAS SCALE, glibc 2.36, binaries exec'd from the pool because /tmp and /home are noexec; client = netwatch-01 Arch, enp6s0 10 GbE). Env: **MTU 9000 both ends** (client NM profile was silently 1500 — set to 9000 via profile edit + full `connection up` reactivation after `device reapply` refused on immutable fields; 8972-byte DF probe green), **iperf3 baseline 9.88 Gbit/s single-stream / 9.91 both directions** — the parity reference (~1.24 GB/s). **Results (validated matrix, avg of 3 true cold runs each)**: TCP push 1 GiB **907 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** (first payload 14.5 ms), TCP pull 1 GiB **904 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** — both at the wire ceiling; gRPC-fallback push 1 GiB 1070 ms ≈ 8.0 Gbit/s (first payload 1.0–2.2 s scaling with manifest size — the design-4 no-mid-manifest-negotiation property, deliberate); gRPC pull ≈ TCP pull on large (880 vs 904 ms — 1 MiB-chunked fallback is wire-competitive on clean LAN), TCP 31% faster on 10k-small pulls; push 10k×4 KiB 2.29 s (~4.4k files/s) vs pull 433 ms (~23k files/s — push side per-file daemon work is the gap, w4-4/w9 territory); local blit beats rsync 2.3× on 1 GiB (157 vs 359 ms), no-op mirror 3 ms vs rsync 42 ms. **ue-1 loopback parity band: HOLDS** — local/push/pull per workload: large 195/178/162 ms, small 83/147/116, mixed 119/163/104; worst spread 1.8×, no 10×/2× gap (record: logs/bench_10gbe_20260704T201804/loopback_parity_band.csv). **Reverse direction validated** (daemon on client, skippy as pusher/puller through ufw): push 7.25 Gbit/s data-plane (first payload 3.9 ms), pull 9.75 Gbit/s. **ue-2 observation**: no organic mid-transfer resize triggered anywhere — a single stream saturates 10 GbE (daemon log: 1 GiB stream at 9.69 Gbit/s; negotiated extra streams close cleanly at 0 bytes), including under deliberate 2-concurrent-push contention (8.30+3.21 Gbit/s, clean completion); organic resize needs a constrained/variable wire — owner call whether the ue-r2-2 deterministic suite + this no-wedge evidence satisfies the gate. **Zero-copy datapoint** (D-2026-06-12-1 revisit): every transfer reported `zero-copy 0 bytes` yet the wire is saturated — splice wiring buys nothing at 10 GbE; CPU is not the bottleneck. **Methodology recorded**: engine-vs-wire isolation by design — tmpfs local ends, ZFS absorbing writes async, ARC-warm re-reads, no sync/ARC-eviction between runs (owner asked; disk-path variants — post-push `zpool sync` timing, cold-ARC pulls via `primarycache` — deferred as owner-gated follow-ups). **Session findings fixed en route**: bench_10gbe.sh had never survived past its local phase — stale endpoint grammar (missing load-bearing trailing slash) + daemon-only `--force-grpc-data` flag on the client, both silent aborts under run_timed's `2>/dev/null` + `set -e` (`b9befb8`); codex review of that fix returned 2 High (run_timed re-runs measure no-ops; shared module root lets gRPC pushes no-op and de-isolates pull labels) — both accepted, fixed `92d6326` (run_timed_fresh / per-run push subdirs / per-workload subpath pulls), validated by a full re-run (avg≈best every row). Verdicts: `.review/results/bench-script-fix.{codex,gpt-verdict}.md`. **New foot-gun filed**: `blit copy src_large dst` with an existing local dir named without `./` parses as an mDNS discovery endpoint and errors "remote source must include a module or root" (blit-app endpoints.rs) — STATE open question. Benchmark payload cleaned off the pool; binaries + bench.toml left at /mnt/generic-pool/video/blit-bin/ for future sessions. **Owner calls now pending**: ue-1 declaration (evidence says band holds), ue-2 declaration, zero-copy revisit verdict, REV4 → Shipped flip, and the GitHub push (w9-3 Windows CI check still queued behind it).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2023:**2026-07-04 22:09:54Z** - **OWNER Q&A + PUSH (benchmark host plan, claude)**: Owner pushed `master` → `github` at `10d89e0` (first push carrying the w9-1/w9-4 ungated suites since the Windows triage — windows-latest CI on it is the "meaningfully green" check; `origin` gitea mirror not pushed, lags). Benchmark host question settled in Q&A: the 10 GbE **sign-off pair is TrueNAS (skippy) ↔ Arch client, all-Linux** — the zero-copy/splice revisit gate (D-2026-06-12-1) needs a Linux consumer, and ue-1's parity band should measure the engine without Windows I/O confounders (win_fs, TCP autotuning, filter drivers). Key facts recorded: the client box **dual-boots Win 11/Arch on identical hardware** (so a later TrueNAS→Win 11 bare-metal pull in the same hardware window gives a clean platform delta as a deployment-parity datapoint, not a gate), and a **Windows VM on the Arch install** exists for Windows-specific functional checks — explicitly never for perf numbers (virtio/NAT skews throughput). iperf3 baseline per pair before any Blit numbers. Plan recorded in STATE.md Queue item 2. Handoff commit follows; push of the handoff itself pending the owner's ref-listing go per push policy.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2039:**2026-07-04 13:53:22Z** - **DECISIONS (owner Q&A, claude)**: The owner asked for the four standing questions "one at a time, no idea what these refer to" — each was presented in plain English with options and answered: (1) **commit erratum → leave as-is** (D-2026-07-04-2; mirrors the D-2026-06-07-1 no-rewrite calculus — two bisect-skippable commits beat force-pushing shared history); (2) **10 GbE session → "soon, but keep coding first"** (STATE Blocked reworded: not a daily blocker; owner will call "benchmark"); (3) **D-2026-06-20-1 stale warmup/size-gate wording → "follow the existing pattern"** — the ledger's own precedent (D-2026-06-20-2's veto annotation, D-2026-06-20-6's struck scope clause) IS edit-in-place-with-annotation, so the superseded framings are struck with pointers to -2 q1 and REV4/-5 (bounded-unilateral untouched — still true), and -5's "remains an open question" note resolved; (4) **supports_cancellation → flip it** (D-2026-07-04-3): CancelJob + TUI F2 will work on attached Push/PullSync transfers; policy-only after w4-3's race wiring; contract change (exit 2→0) recorded; implementation queued as **w4-5-supports-cancellation-flip**, now the topmost open REVIEW.md row. Batch `2a21d6f` through the codex loop per D-2026-07-04-1: **NEEDS FIXES (1 Medium + 1 Low, both STATE.md coherence)** — the Now bullet still called the erratum an open owner call, and the queue rewrite dropped the coder's-pick clause (design-3-vs-w2-2 ordering contradiction); both accepted, fixed `a928193`. The decision content itself passed all cross-checks (ledger consistency, w4-3 scope-note agreement, strike precision). check-docs.sh green. All on master, unpushed.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2105:**2026-05-13 18:30:00Z** - **FEAT + SCOPE**: §3.1 / §3.3 / §5.2 / §5.4 closed (D5 modified, D6 default taken, BlitAuth + AI telemetry removed). Three owner decisions landed in one commit set: **(1) §3.1 D5 modified.** Rather than ship `--metrics` as dormant scaffolding, the daemon now emits a one-line stderr summary at end of each push/pull/pull_sync RPC when `--metrics` is on. Format: `[metrics] push ok in 1.23s (push_ops=N pull_ops=N purge_ops=N active=N errors=N)` — rsync/rclone-style operator visibility under systemd / foreground without needing the TUI. `crates/blit-daemon/src/metrics.rs` gains `log_completion(op_kind, duration, ok)` (no-op when disabled — atomics never loaded) and `format_completion_line` (extracted so the format contract is unit-testable without stderr capture). 3 new unit tests in `metrics::tests` pin the line format and error tagging. Counters themselves stay in place and feed future TUI work; the change just gives `--metrics` a real consumer for 0.1.0. **(2) §3.3 D6 default taken.** Phase 4.8 daemon FS-capability work (4.8.2 startup/idle daemon probes + 4.8.3 `blit diagnostics profile` capability probes) explicitly deferred to 0.2.0. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.8 now carries a scope-note callout marking 4.8.2 and 4.8.3 as deferred; 4.8.1 client-side `fs_capability` cache ships in 0.1.0 as today. **(3) §5.2 BlitAuth removed.** `service BlitAuth { Authenticate(AuthRequest) returns (AuthResponse) }` plus the two messages stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` (forward-compat auth passthrough hook for delegated pull) also removed — field 10 reserved against future reuse. `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` no longer sets the field. `DAEMON_CONFIG.md` Trust Model section rewritten to say "no auth, intentional, out of scope — terminate auth at the network edge" (was "no auth in 0.1.0; passthrough field exists for forward-compat"). `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` got a top-of-file auth note flagging body references to BlitAuth/`delegated_credential` as obsolete (body retained as historical design record). **(4) §5.4 AI telemetry removed.** `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` deleted entirely. References in `docs/plan/README.md`, `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` dropped. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.9 marked as removed from scope. Predictor (§2.8) is unaffected — performance history is still collected; no "analyze my history" feature is planned. **Release-plan tracker:** §3.1 / §3.2 / §3.3 all ✅ closed. §5.2 / §5.4 changed from "deferred" to "removed." `--metrics` decision (D5) modified from default; all other D-decisions taken at their plan defaults. **Net release-blocker count:** §2.6 (hardware-bound 10 GbE benchmark capture) is the last open P0. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+3 metrics tests); `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check` clean.

.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2145:**2026-05-03 01:25:00Z** - **DOCS/TOOLS**: Completed Phase 3 cleanup/harness items from `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` except live benchmark execution. .review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2205:**2026-04-14 20:00:00Z** - **ACTION**: Completed unified-pipeline remediation across all transfer paths. Previously only local→local and gRPC-fallback push routed through `execute_sink_pipeline`; multi-stream TCP push and both daemon pull paths had bespoke dispatch code. Added `execute_sink_pipeline_streaming(source, sinks, payload_rx, prefetch, progress)` in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/pipeline.rs` and made the one-shot form a thin wrapper. Rewrote `MultiStreamSender` in `crates/blit-core/src/remote/push/client/mod.rs` as a lightweight `payload_tx` + `JoinHandle` shell — deleted `data_plane_sink_worker`, batch-splitting helpers, and `StreamStats` (~130 lines). Converted both daemon pull paths in `crates/blit-daemon/src/service/pull.rs` to use the pipeline; deleted `handle_pull_stream` and `handle_pull_stream_streaming` (~125 lines). Deleted dead modules: `transfer_engine.rs` (WorkerFactory trait + task-channel infra), `transfer_facade/` (TaskAggregator, PlannerEvent, build_local_plan, stream_local_plan), and `RetryableTask` from `transfer_plan.rs` — 1139 lines total. Updated `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` with the unified-pipeline diagram and source/sink matrix. Added `blit(1)` command-summary hints and expanded `TransferArgs` doc-comments for `--help`. **Tests**: 152 passed (87 core + 65 integration), clippy clean. **Remaining**: end-to-end 10 GbE benchmark runs.

.review/results/otp-2w.codex.md:14:Review commits 0c43d2a and ceea6ed together (run: git show 0c43d2a; git show ceea6ed). They implement otp-2w — the OLD-path baseline on the owner-designated Mac<->Windows pair, completing ONE_TRANSFER_PATH slice otp-2 (the owner adjudicated the prior open question by designating this closer-spec pair for the acceptance bar's cross-direction half; docs/STATE.md records the resolution). Deliverables: scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh (zoey methodology with the daemon-host half in PowerShell over SSH), scripts/windows/purge-standby.ps1 (standby-list purge via NtSetSystemInformation), evidence in docs/bench/otp2w-baseline-2026-07-10/ (README, summary.csv, runs.csv, drain-outcomes.txt), finding doc .review/findings/otp-2w-windows-baseline.md, STATE/DEVLOG updates. Check: (1) the bash+PowerShell harness's correctness — quoting across bash->ssh->PowerShell->WMI->cmd layers, the WMI daemon launch (session-survival rationale), drain/purge/durability ordering, whether push and pull windows pay equivalent durability costs on this platform pair (Write-VolumeCache vs per-file fsync); (2) the purge-standby.ps1 P/Invoke correctness (privilege enabling, SystemMemoryListInformation command 4, error handling); (3) evidence coherence — README medians vs runs.csv/summary.csv, the stability and reading claims vs data (spread numbers, the x1.8-x2.7 push-pull ratios, carrier-insensitivity on large pushes); (4) whether the owner adjudication is recorded faithfully (STATE open question -> RESOLVED, nothing silently over-claimed about symmetry — the README calls it close-spec, not symmetric); (5) methodology parity with the zoey harness where it matters and honest disclosure where the platforms differ (standby purge vs drop_caches, Defender note); (6) no production code touched; no contradiction with docs/DECISIONS.md. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.
.review/results/otp-2w.codex.md:86:- [scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:137](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:137) — **Medium** — Remote PowerShell retains non-terminating error behavior. Failed `Write-VolumeCache`/`Get-Counter` calls can leave `$w = $null`; `$null -lt 1048576` is true, allowing a false `drained 6s` result and exit 0.
.review/results/otp-2w.codex.md:88:- [scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:119](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:119) — **Medium** — WMI’s returned PID is discarded, readiness accepts any `blit-daemon`, and cleanup kills every process with that name. A surviving stale daemon can mask a new bind failure and cause benchmarking of the wrong build/config; unrelated instances are also terminated.
.review/results/otp-2w.codex.md:90:- [scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:205](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:205) — **Medium** — Push and [pull durability costs](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:225) are systematically unequal: SSH/PowerShell plus a whole-volume cache flush versus Python plus per-file `fsync`. Both reach broadly comparable cache-to-disk depth, but the fixture-dependent overhead biases cross-direction ratios and is not disclosed in the README’s OS-versus-code attribution. [`Write-VolumeCache` operates on the selected volume cache.](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/storage/write-volumecache?view=windowsserver2025-ps)
.review/results/otp-2w.codex.md:103:- [scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:137](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:137) — **Medium** — Remote PowerShell retains non-terminating error behavior. Failed `Write-VolumeCache`/`Get-Counter` calls can leave `$w = $null`; `$null -lt 1048576` is true, allowing a false `drained 6s` result and exit 0.
.review/results/otp-2w.codex.md:105:- [scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:119](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:119) — **Medium** — WMI’s returned PID is discarded, readiness accepts any `blit-daemon`, and cleanup kills every process with that name. .review/results/otp-2w.codex.md:107:- [scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:205](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:205) — **Medium** — Push and [pull durability costs](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp2w_baseline.sh:225) are systematically unequal: SSH/PowerShell plus a whole-volume cache flush versus Python plus per-file `fsync`.
