.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit 06e5413 (run: git show 06e5413). It is a PLAN change (docs only, no code): docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md (new Draft) + D-2026-07-05-1 in docs/DECISIONS.md + STATE/DEVLOG updates. The plan responds to an owner directive (quoted verbatim in the doc): ONE block of transfer code — direction/initiator/CLI-verb select roles, never code paths; the per-direction drivers (client push driver, daemon push-receive, client pull driver, daemon pull-send, delegated-pull driver, separate local orchestration) and the Push/PullSync RPCs are DELETED at cutover; benchmark verdict cells must be symmetric-fs disk-to-disk. Check: internal coherence of the plan (goal/non-goals/constraints/acceptance/slices consistent); no contradiction with docs/DECISIONS.md (esp. D-2026-07-04-4 ceiling principle, D-2026-07-04-1 codex loop, D-2026-06-20-1/-2 dial contract, D-2026-06-12-1 zero-copy) or with the Active REV4 plan; whether the one-choreography design (source streams manifest, destination diffs, sf-2 shape-corrected dial as the only stream policy, fallback as byte-carrier, delegated = daemon-initiated session, local via in-process transport) has gaps or unstated risks the plan should name (e.g. resume protocol ordering, mirror delete authority, NAT/dialer asymmetry, jobs/progress integration, read-only enforcement); whether the slices are review-loop sized and correctly ordered with the tree green throughout; whether the acceptance criteria are observable and actually pin the owner's invariant (initiator/verb invariance within noise on symmetric endpoints; deletion proof; capability parity; test-count floor); and whether anything in the plan quietly re-litigates decisions the owner already settled. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file/section, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:30:    converge-up constraint; symmetric-fs benchmark verdict cells. No code
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:58:    converge-up constraint; symmetric-fs benchmark verdict cells. No code
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:77:+**2026-07-05 07:30:00Z** - **DIRECTIVE + PLAN (ONE_TRANSFER_PATH draft, D-2026-07-05-1, claude)**: Owner rejected the push/pull disparity, the mixed-fs benchmark methodology ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"), and the explanation that direction symmetry lives in four separate driver loops — then issued the directive now recorded verbatim in `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md`: **"ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"**; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did"; identical whether initiated as push from skippy or pull from this machine. Scope/wire/process explicitly delegated to the agent. **Plan drafted through the plan procedure**: one `TransferSession` (roles SOURCE/DESTINATION selected by initiator/verb, never code paths), one bidi `Transfer` RPC replacing `Push`+`PullSync` (no back-compat, lockstep upgrade — repo precedent PullSyncHeader), one choreography (source streams manifest immediately; destination diffs incrementally; sf-2 shape-corrected dial as the only stream policy — absorbs the pull 1s-start residue), gRPC fallback demoted to a byte-carrier option, delegated pull = daemon-initiated session, local transfers on an in-process transport in the final phase; slices otp-1..12 ending in a **deletion slice** (the four drivers + both RPCs removed, file-by-file proof) and a **symmetric-rig acceptance matrix** (same-fs disk-to-disk verdict cells, cold caches, tmpfs as labeled wire-reference only; initiator/verb A/B within ±10%). Converge-up constraint: per cell the unified path must match the better of today's directions, not the average. **D-2026-07-05-1 recorded**: directive + SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused at sf-2 (sf-3a+ blocked; principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands) + design queue paused (w7-1 likely absorbed by otp-5). STATE rewritten around the new queue. Next: codex plan review of this commit, adjudicate, fix, then STOP for the owner's Active flip — no implementation anywhere until then.

.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:715:- Why: the dead draft busy-waits on EAGAIN (would be rewritten, not revived); wiring needs a raw-fd special case beside a permanent buffered fallback; the CPU saving is a fraction of one core, Linux-only, and unmeasured. Revisit gate: 10 GbE benchmarks showing receive-side CPU saturation — design notes preserved in the eval doc.
.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:725:- Decision: `docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE.md` is **Active**. Owner approved with four parameters that bind the design: (q1) **no probe-then-go phase** — the engine starts moving within ~1s at conservative defaults bounded by the receiver ceiling and the tuner adjusts dials live from the first byte; the "small-transfer threshold" is obviated (no probe to skip), and the **planner** carries the workload-shape judgment (file count vs bytes) that the old size gate proxied. (q2) the receiver advertises a **rich capacity profile** (CPU cores, disk class, load, max streams, drain estimate) — "more data serves the ubergoal"; do not minimize the negotiation payload. (q3) engine type **deferred to the agent**, who recommends a new src/dst-agnostic `TransferEngine` + a local adapter over renaming `TransferOrchestrator` in place — ratified at `ue-1c`. (q4) `ue-2` (mid-transfer stream add/drop via PR3's resize proto) is **in scope at Active**, sequenced last; 11 months of owner benchmarking is the justification, the 10 GbE rig is sign-off not a gate.

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:52: **2026-07-05 07:30:00Z** - **DIRECTIVE + PLAN (ONE_TRANSFER_PATH draft, D-2026-07-05-1, claude)**: Owner rejected the push/pull disparity, the mixed-fs benchmark methodology ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"), and the explanation that direction symmetry lives in four separate driver loops — then issued the directive now recorded verbatim in `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md`: **"ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"**; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did"; identical whether initiated as push from skippy or pull from this machine. Scope/wire/process explicitly delegated to the agent. **Plan drafted through the plan procedure**: one `TransferSession` (roles SOURCE/DESTINATION selected by initiator/verb, never code paths), one bidi `Transfer` RPC replacing `Push`+`PullSync` (no back-compat, lockstep upgrade — repo precedent PullSyncHeader), one choreography (source streams manifest immediately; destination diffs incrementally; sf-2 shape-corrected dial as the only stream policy — absorbs the pull 1s-start residue), gRPC fallback demoted to a byte-carrier option, delegated pull = daemon-initiated session, local transfers on an in-process transport in the final phase; slices otp-1..12 ending in a **deletion slice** (the four drivers + both RPCs removed, file-by-file proof) and a **symmetric-rig acceptance matrix** (same-fs disk-to-disk verdict cells, cold caches, tmpfs as labeled wire-reference only; initiator/verb A/B within ±10%). Converge-up constraint: per cell the unified path must match the better of today's directions, not the average. **D-2026-07-05-1 recorded**: directive + SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused at sf-2 (sf-3a+ blocked; principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands) + design queue paused (w7-1 likely absorbed by otp-5). STATE rewritten around the new queue. Next: codex plan review of this commit, adjudicate, fix, then STOP for the owner's Active flip — no implementation anywhere until then.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:63:+- Decision: The D-2026-06-12-1 revisit gate ("receive-side CPU saturation") is **declared met by the owner** (2026-07-05): a UniFi UNAS 8 Pro daemon target whose CPU cannot saturate 10 GbE even from SSD cache. Zero-copy receive is unparked as sanctioned FAST work. Two clarifications: (a) the dead `zero_copy.rs` module still gets deleted as ratified — its EAGAIN busy-wait draft is a rewrite, not a revival (eval doc); (b) the capability returns the one-path way (owner exchange 2026-07-05): a **runtime-selected write strategy inside the unified receive sink** — the eval doc's revisit design (`AsyncFd`-readiness splice loop beside the buffered relay, selected when the reader is a raw TcpStream and the payload is a file record, buffered relay as universal fallback), capability-gated by kernel/fs support, identical in both roles — never a side path. Sequenced after ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover (otp-10) as its own slice set; the UNAS is the measurement rig and the symmetric-endpoint benchmark rule (D-2026-07-05-2 era methodology) applies to its cells.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:65:+- Supersedes: the STATE Blocked "zero-copy option a/b/c" question and the "zero-copy revisit verdict" item among the four 10 GbE owner declarations (both resolved by this entry); amends D-2026-06-12-1's revisit-gate framing from "10 GbE benchmarks showing receive-side CPU saturation" to "a CPU-bound receiver exists" (annotated in the eval doc). D-2026-06-12-1's deletion of the dead module stands.

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1118:DEVLOG.md:12:**2026-07-05 07:30:00Z** - **DIRECTIVE + PLAN (ONE_TRANSFER_PATH draft, D-2026-07-05-1, claude)**: Owner rejected the push/pull disparity, the mixed-fs benchmark methodology ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"), and the explanation that direction symmetry lives in four separate driver loops — then issued the directive now recorded verbatim in `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md`: **"ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"**; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did"; identical whether initiated as push from skippy or pull from this machine. Scope/wire/process explicitly delegated to the agent. **Plan drafted through the plan procedure**: one `TransferSession` (roles SOURCE/DESTINATION selected by initiator/verb, never code paths), one bidi `Transfer` RPC replacing `Push`+`PullSync` (no back-compat, lockstep upgrade — repo precedent PullSyncHeader), one choreography (source streams manifest immediately; destination diffs incrementally; sf-2 shape-corrected dial as the only stream policy — absorbs the pull 1s-start residue), gRPC fallback demoted to a byte-carrier option, delegated pull = daemon-initiated session, local transfers on an in-process transport in the final phase; slices otp-1..12 ending in a **deletion slice** (the four drivers + both RPCs removed, file-by-file proof) and a **symmetric-rig acceptance matrix** (same-fs disk-to-disk verdict cells, cold caches, tmpfs as labeled wire-reference only; initiator/verb A/B within ±10%). Converge-up constraint: per cell the unified path must match the better of today's directions, not the average. **D-2026-07-05-1 recorded**: directive + SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused at sf-2 (sf-3a+ blocked; principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands) + design queue paused (w7-1 likely absorbed by otp-5). STATE rewritten around the new queue. Next: codex plan review of this commit, adjudicate, fix, then STOP for the owner's Active flip — no implementation anywhere until then.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1121:DEVLOG.md:22:**2026-07-05 00:34:30Z** - **BENCHMARK SESSION (10 GbE, TrueNAS↔Arch, claude + owner)**: The owner-called REV4 sign-off session ran end-to-end on the recorded pair (skippy = TrueNAS SCALE, glibc 2.36, binaries exec'd from the pool because /tmp and /home are noexec; client = netwatch-01 Arch, enp6s0 10 GbE). Env: **MTU 9000 both ends** (client NM profile was silently 1500 — set to 9000 via profile edit + full `connection up` reactivation after `device reapply` refused on immutable fields; 8972-byte DF probe green), **iperf3 baseline 9.88 Gbit/s single-stream / 9.91 both directions** — the parity reference (~1.24 GB/s). **Results (validated matrix, avg of 3 true cold runs each)**: TCP push 1 GiB **907 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** (first payload 14.5 ms), TCP pull 1 GiB **904 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** — both at the wire ceiling; gRPC-fallback push 1 GiB 1070 ms ≈ 8.0 Gbit/s (first payload 1.0–2.2 s scaling with manifest size — the design-4 no-mid-manifest-negotiation property, deliberate); gRPC pull ≈ TCP pull on large (880 vs 904 ms — 1 MiB-chunked fallback is wire-competitive on clean LAN), TCP 31% faster on 10k-small pulls; push 10k×4 KiB 2.29 s (~4.4k files/s) vs pull 433 ms (~23k files/s — push side per-file daemon work is the gap, w4-4/w9 territory); local blit beats rsync 2.3× on 1 GiB (157 vs 359 ms), no-op mirror 3 ms vs rsync 42 ms. **ue-1 loopback parity band: HOLDS** — local/push/pull per workload: large 195/178/162 ms, small 83/147/116, mixed 119/163/104; worst spread 1.8×, no 10×/2× gap (record: logs/bench_10gbe_20260704T201804/loopback_parity_band.csv). **Reverse direction validated** (daemon on client, skippy as pusher/puller through ufw): push 7.25 Gbit/s data-plane (first payload 3.9 ms), pull 9.75 Gbit/s. **ue-2 observation**: no organic mid-transfer resize triggered anywhere — a single stream saturates 10 GbE (daemon log: 1 GiB stream at 9.69 Gbit/s; negotiated extra streams close cleanly at 0 bytes), including under deliberate 2-concurrent-push contention (8.30+3.21 Gbit/s, clean completion); organic resize needs a constrained/variable wire — owner call whether the ue-r2-2 deterministic suite + this no-wedge evidence satisfies the gate. **Zero-copy datapoint** (D-2026-06-12-1 revisit): every transfer reported `zero-copy 0 bytes` yet the wire is saturated — splice wiring buys nothing at 10 GbE; CPU is not the bottleneck. **Methodology recorded**: engine-vs-wire isolation by design — tmpfs local ends, ZFS absorbing writes async, ARC-warm re-reads, no sync/ARC-eviction between runs (owner asked; disk-path variants — post-push `zpool sync` timing, cold-ARC pulls via `primarycache` — deferred as owner-gated follow-ups). **Session findings fixed en route**: bench_10gbe.sh had never survived past its local phase — stale endpoint grammar (missing load-bearing trailing slash) + daemon-only `--force-grpc-data` flag on the client, both silent aborts under run_timed's `2>/dev/null` + `set -e` (`b9befb8`); codex review of that fix returned 2 High (run_timed re-runs measure no-ops; shared module root lets gRPC pushes no-op and de-isolates pull labels) — both accepted, fixed `92d6326` (run_timed_fresh / per-run push subdirs / per-workload subpath pulls), validated by a full re-run (avg≈best every row). Verdicts: `.review/results/bench-script-fix.{codex,gpt-verdict}.md`. **New foot-gun filed**: `blit copy src_large dst` with an existing local dir named without `./` parses as an mDNS discovery endpoint and errors "remote source must include a module or root" (blit-app endpoints.rs) — STATE open question. Benchmark payload cleaned off the pool; binaries + bench.toml left at /mnt/generic-pool/video/blit-bin/ for future sessions. **Owner calls now pending**: ue-1 declaration (evidence says band holds), ue-2 declaration, zero-copy revisit verdict, REV4 → Shipped flip, and the GitHub push (w9-3 Windows CI check still queued behind it).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1123:DEVLOG.md:26:**2026-07-04 22:09:54Z** - **OWNER Q&A + PUSH (benchmark host plan, claude)**: Owner pushed `master` → `github` at `10d89e0` (first push carrying the w9-1/w9-4 ungated suites since the Windows triage — windows-latest CI on it is the "meaningfully green" check; `origin` gitea mirror not pushed, lags). Benchmark host question settled in Q&A: the 10 GbE **sign-off pair is TrueNAS (skippy) ↔ Arch client, all-Linux** — the zero-copy/splice revisit gate (D-2026-06-12-1) needs a Linux consumer, and ue-1's parity band should measure the engine without Windows I/O confounders (win_fs, TCP autotuning, filter drivers). Key facts recorded: the client box **dual-boots Win 11/Arch on identical hardware** (so a later TrueNAS→Win 11 bare-metal pull in the same hardware window gives a clean platform delta as a deployment-parity datapoint, not a gate), and a **Windows VM on the Arch install** exists for Windows-specific functional checks — explicitly never for perf numbers (virtio/NAT skews throughput). iperf3 baseline per pair before any Blit numbers. Plan recorded in STATE.md Queue item 2. Handoff commit follows; push of the handoff itself pending the owner's ref-listing go per push policy.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1131:DEVLOG.md:42:**2026-07-04 13:53:22Z** - **DECISIONS (owner Q&A, claude)**: The owner asked for the four standing questions "one at a time, no idea what these refer to" — each was presented in plain English with options and answered: (1) **commit erratum → leave as-is** (D-2026-07-04-2; mirrors the D-2026-06-07-1 no-rewrite calculus — two bisect-skippable commits beat force-pushing shared history); (2) **10 GbE session → "soon, but keep coding first"** (STATE Blocked reworded: not a daily blocker; owner will call "benchmark"); (3) **D-2026-06-20-1 stale warmup/size-gate wording → "follow the existing pattern"** — the ledger's own precedent (D-2026-06-20-2's veto annotation, D-2026-06-20-6's struck scope clause) IS edit-in-place-with-annotation, so the superseded framings are struck with pointers to -2 q1 and REV4/-5 (bounded-unilateral untouched — still true), and -5's "remains an open question" note resolved; (4) **supports_cancellation → flip it** (D-2026-07-04-3): CancelJob + TUI F2 will work on attached Push/PullSync transfers; policy-only after w4-3's race wiring; contract change (exit 2→0) recorded; implementation queued as **w4-5-supports-cancellation-flip**, now the topmost open REVIEW.md row. Batch `2a21d6f` through the codex loop per D-2026-07-04-1: **NEEDS FIXES (1 Medium + 1 Low, both STATE.md coherence)** — the Now bullet still called the erratum an open owner call, and the queue rewrite dropped the coder's-pick clause (design-3-vs-w2-2 ordering contradiction); both accepted, fixed `a928193`. The decision content itself passed all cross-checks (ledger consistency, w4-3 scope-note agreement, strike precision). check-docs.sh green. All on master, unpushed.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1148:DEVLOG.md:108:**2026-05-13 18:30:00Z** - **FEAT + SCOPE**: §3.1 / §3.3 / §5.2 / §5.4 closed (D5 modified, D6 default taken, BlitAuth + AI telemetry removed). Three owner decisions landed in one commit set: **(1) §3.1 D5 modified.** Rather than ship `--metrics` as dormant scaffolding, the daemon now emits a one-line stderr summary at end of each push/pull/pull_sync RPC when `--metrics` is on. Format: `[metrics] push ok in 1.23s (push_ops=N pull_ops=N purge_ops=N active=N errors=N)` — rsync/rclone-style operator visibility under systemd / foreground without needing the TUI. `crates/blit-daemon/src/metrics.rs` gains `log_completion(op_kind, duration, ok)` (no-op when disabled — atomics never loaded) and `format_completion_line` (extracted so the format contract is unit-testable without stderr capture). 3 new unit tests in `metrics::tests` pin the line format and error tagging. Counters themselves stay in place and feed future TUI work; the change just gives `--metrics` a real consumer for 0.1.0. **(2) §3.3 D6 default taken.** Phase 4.8 daemon FS-capability work (4.8.2 startup/idle daemon probes + 4.8.3 `blit diagnostics profile` capability probes) explicitly deferred to 0.2.0. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.8 now carries a scope-note callout marking 4.8.2 and 4.8.3 as deferred; 4.8.1 client-side `fs_capability` cache ships in 0.1.0 as today. **(3) §5.2 BlitAuth removed.** `service BlitAuth { Authenticate(AuthRequest) returns (AuthResponse) }` plus the two messages stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` (forward-compat auth passthrough hook for delegated pull) also removed — field 10 reserved against future reuse. `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` no longer sets the field. `DAEMON_CONFIG.md` Trust Model section rewritten to say "no auth, intentional, out of scope — terminate auth at the network edge" (was "no auth in 0.1.0; passthrough field exists for forward-compat"). `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` got a top-of-file auth note flagging body references to BlitAuth/`delegated_credential` as obsolete (body retained as historical design record). **(4) §5.4 AI telemetry removed.** `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` deleted entirely. References in `docs/plan/README.md`, `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` dropped. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.9 marked as removed from scope. Predictor (§2.8) is unaffected — performance history is still collected; no "analyze my history" feature is planned. **Release-plan tracker:** §3.1 / §3.2 / §3.3 all ✅ closed. §5.2 / §5.4 changed from "deferred" to "removed." `--metrics` decision (D5) modified from default; all other D-decisions taken at their plan defaults. **Net release-blocker count:** §2.6 (hardware-bound 10 GbE benchmark capture) is the last open P0. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+3 metrics tests); `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check` clean.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1150:DEVLOG.md:116:**2026-05-07 14:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup #2 catching review of `8d43e4d` — the RELEASE_PLAN_v2 itself was still presenting closed P0s as open work, which was a serious risk because `docs/plan/README.md` points new agents at it as the live source of truth for what remains before shipping. **Medium**: added a top-of-file "Closure tracker (last updated 2026-05-07)" block with a one-row-per-section table showing closing commits for §2.1/§2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.7/§2.8 and §4, plus pending markers for §2.6 (hardware-bound benchmark — last open P0) and §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 (P1, doc-only). Each P0 section now has its own per-section `**Status (2026-05-07):** ✅ Closed <commit>` block at the top so a reader landing on a specific section sees current state without scrolling back to the top. §2.5 line 265 also still showed the old `blit completions remote <prefix>` syntax in the recommendation paragraph — corrected to the actual `<REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` form, matching the existing line-21 manpage. §6 commit-sequence rewritten as a status table with closing commits per row (P0 done except step 2 §2.6; P1 deferrable). §7 decisions table flipped from "Default recommendation" / "Owner" columns to "Default" / "Outcome" with closing commits where taken — D1/D2/D3/D7/D8/D9 ✅ taken, D4/D5/D6 still open. Test totals updated from the 2026-05-04 baseline (383) to current (407, after R41-R45 review-fix coverage). Footer "Last updated" + "Next review" rewritten. **Low**: `docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md:83` Success Criteria still said `blit-cli mirror -p -v local remote`; updated to `blit mirror`. Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1224:docs/DECISIONS.md:140:- Decision: The D-2026-06-12-1 revisit gate ("receive-side CPU saturation") is **declared met by the owner** (2026-07-05): a UniFi UNAS 8 Pro daemon target whose CPU cannot saturate 10 GbE even from SSD cache. Zero-copy receive is unparked as sanctioned FAST work. Two clarifications: (a) the dead `zero_copy.rs` module still gets deleted as ratified — its EAGAIN busy-wait draft is a rewrite, not a revival (eval doc); (b) the capability returns the one-path way (owner exchange 2026-07-05): a **runtime-selected write strategy inside the unified receive sink** — the eval doc's revisit design (`AsyncFd`-readiness splice loop beside the buffered relay, selected when the reader is a raw TcpStream and the payload is a file record, buffered relay as universal fallback), capability-gated by kernel/fs support, identical in both roles — never a side path. Sequenced after ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover (otp-10) as its own slice set; the UNAS is the measurement rig and the symmetric-endpoint benchmark rule (D-2026-07-05-2 era methodology) applies to its cells.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1226:docs/DECISIONS.md:142:- Supersedes: the STATE Blocked "zero-copy option a/b/c" question and the "zero-copy revisit verdict" item among the four 10 GbE owner declarations (both resolved by this entry); amends D-2026-06-12-1's revisit-gate framing from "10 GbE benchmarks showing receive-side CPU saturation" to "a CPU-bound receiver exists" (annotated in the eval doc). D-2026-06-12-1's deletion of the dead module stands.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1364:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:770:- `retry/wait CLI defaults` = retry=0, wait=5s, fixed spacing (run_with_retries, blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55) (crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:278) — SIMPLE: an explicit user knob in a zero-tuning tool (owner-approved robocopy parity); no backoff — fixed 5s spacing is a frozen recovery policy.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1367:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:810:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55 — run_with_retries — retry count/wait come from CLI flags (default retry=0, wait=5s per blit-cli/src/cli.rs:633-639); fixed (non-backoff) spacing — User-tunable knob (mild SIMPLE tension, owner-approved robocopy parity); constant wait rather than exponential backoff.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1557:docs/audit/inventory/plan-phases.md:410:> "Blit v2 is feature-complete for a 0.1.0 release. All phases through Phase 4 (Production Hardening) are substantially done. The remaining open items are benchmarking tasks that require dedicated hardware (10+ GbE network) and post-release investigations (RDMA, ReFS privilege)."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1657:docs/audit/2026-05-04_roadmap_audit.md:63:| Phase 3 Remote Ops done | PARTIAL | Hybrid TCP done (`remote/transfer/data_plane.rs`), gRPC fallback (`remote/pull.rs`, `service/pull.rs`), all admin RPCs (`Find`, `DiskUsage`, `FilesystemStats`, `CompletePath`, `Purge`, `ListModules` — `proto/blit.proto:24-36`). Remote→remote delegation Phases 1+2 shipped (`service/delegated_pull.rs`, `transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs`); CLI relay primitive retained as `--relay-via-cli`. | Phase 3 *still has open TODO items* for "Capture remote benchmark runs (TCP vs forced gRPC fallback)" (`TODO.md:228`) and "Benchmark remote fallback + data-plane streaming…" (`TODO.md:190`). |
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1658:docs/audit/2026-05-04_roadmap_audit.md:78:| Windows CopyFileExW, USN Change Journal, ReFS block clone | SHIPPING (block clone gated on privilege) | `copy/windows.rs`; `change_journal/snapshot.rs::windows`; `fs_capability/windows.rs:54+` `probe_block_clone_support`. `win_fs.rs:134+` documents `SeManageVolumePrivilege` requirement. | ReFS benchmark in `WORKFLOW_PHASE_2.5.md:72` shows blit 0.59s vs robocopy 0.165s — block clone path operates but ~3.5× slower than robocopy. Privilege investigation is `TODO.md:259` open item. |
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1659:docs/audit/2026-05-04_roadmap_audit.md:87:| ReFS block clone privilege investigation | UNVERIFIED / open | `TODO.md:259` open: "Investigate SeManageVolumePrivilege requirement". `win_fs.rs:134+` has the privilege-enable scaffold but `WORKFLOW_PHASE_2.5.md:72` records the fallback path is ~3.5× slower than robocopy. | Confirmed open. Listed in PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md "Post-Release". |
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1661:docs/audit/2026-05-04_roadmap_audit.md:132:| 5 | Re-evaluate remote→remote (CLI relay → daemon-A→daemon-B direct) | SHIPPING (Phases 1+2 of REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN), benchmark NOT-STARTED | Phase 1: `15991ed`. Phase 2: `0c00b4b`. Live bench: open. |
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1662:docs/audit/2026-05-04_roadmap_audit.md:174:| Integration/perf tests proving push/pull parity | SHIPPING (functional) / NOT-STARTED (perf) | `crates/blit-cli/tests/remote_parity.rs` covers TCP + forced gRPC for both directions. Live perf parity validation = the open benchmark items. |
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:2017:docs/plan/RELEASE_PLAN_v2_2026-05-04.md:712:| 2 | §2.6 — Run remote benchmark | ⏳ **Pending** (last open P0; hardware-bound) |
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:2031:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:52: **2026-07-05 07:30:00Z** - **DIRECTIVE + PLAN (ONE_TRANSFER_PATH draft, D-2026-07-05-1, claude)**: Owner rejected the push/pull disparity, the mixed-fs benchmark methodology ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"), and the explanation that direction symmetry lives in four separate driver loops — then issued the directive now recorded verbatim in `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md`: **"ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"**; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did"; identical whether initiated as push from skippy or pull from this machine. Scope/wire/process explicitly delegated to the agent. **Plan drafted through the plan procedure**: one `TransferSession` (roles SOURCE/DESTINATION selected by initiator/verb, never code paths), one bidi `Transfer` RPC replacing `Push`+`PullSync` (no back-compat, lockstep upgrade — repo precedent PullSyncHeader), one choreography (source streams manifest immediately; destination diffs incrementally; sf-2 shape-corrected dial as the only stream policy — absorbs the pull 1s-start residue), gRPC fallback demoted to a byte-carrier option, delegated pull = daemon-initiated session, local transfers on an in-process transport in the final phase; slices otp-1..12 ending in a **deletion slice** (the four drivers + both RPCs removed, file-by-file proof) and a **symmetric-rig acceptance matrix** (same-fs disk-to-disk verdict cells, cold caches, tmpfs as labeled wire-reference only; initiator/verb A/B within ±10%). Converge-up constraint: per cell the unified path must match the better of today's directions, not the average. **D-2026-07-05-1 recorded**: directive + SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused at sf-2 (sf-3a+ blocked; principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands) + design queue paused (w7-1 likely absorbed by otp-5). STATE rewritten around the new queue. Next: codex plan review of this commit, adjudicate, fix, then STOP for the owner's Active flip — no implementation anywhere until then.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:2036:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:63:+- Decision: The D-2026-06-12-1 revisit gate ("receive-side CPU saturation") is **declared met by the owner** (2026-07-05): a UniFi UNAS 8 Pro daemon target whose CPU cannot saturate 10 GbE even from SSD cache. Zero-copy receive is unparked as sanctioned FAST work. Two clarifications: (a) the dead `zero_copy.rs` module still gets deleted as ratified — its EAGAIN busy-wait draft is a rewrite, not a revival (eval doc); (b) the capability returns the one-path way (owner exchange 2026-07-05): a **runtime-selected write strategy inside the unified receive sink** — the eval doc's revisit design (`AsyncFd`-readiness splice loop beside the buffered relay, selected when the reader is a raw TcpStream and the payload is a file record, buffered relay as universal fallback), capability-gated by kernel/fs support, identical in both roles — never a side path. Sequenced after ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover (otp-10) as its own slice set; the UNAS is the measurement rig and the symmetric-endpoint benchmark rule (D-2026-07-05-2 era methodology) applies to its cells.

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:2250:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:248: **2026-07-05 07:30:00Z** - **DIRECTIVE + PLAN (ONE_TRANSFER_PATH draft, D-2026-07-05-1, claude)**: Owner rejected the push/pull disparity, the mixed-fs benchmark methodology ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"), and the explanation that direction symmetry lives in four separate driver loops — then issued the directive now recorded verbatim in `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md`: **"ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"**; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did"; identical whether initiated as push from skippy or pull from this machine. Scope/wire/process explicitly delegated to the agent. **Plan drafted through the plan procedure**: one `TransferSession` (roles SOURCE/DESTINATION selected by initiator/verb, never code paths), one bidi `Transfer` RPC replacing `Push`+`PullSync` (no back-compat, lockstep upgrade — repo precedent PullSyncHeader), one choreography (source streams manifest immediately; destination diffs incrementally; sf-2 shape-corrected dial as the only stream policy — absorbs the pull 1s-start residue), gRPC fallback demoted to a byte-carrier option, delegated pull = daemon-initiated session, local transfers on an in-process transport in the final phase; slices otp-1..12 ending in a **deletion slice** (the four drivers + both RPCs removed, file-by-file proof) and a **symmetric-rig acceptance matrix** (same-fs disk-to-disk verdict cells, cold caches, tmpfs as labeled wire-reference only; initiator/verb A/B within ±10%). Converge-up constraint: per cell the unified path must match the better of today's directions, not the average. **D-2026-07-05-1 recorded**: directive + SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused at sf-2 (sf-3a+ blocked; principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands) + design queue paused (w7-1 likely absorbed by otp-5). STATE rewritten around the new queue. Next: codex plan review of this commit, adjudicate, fix, then STOP for the owner's Active flip — no implementation anywhere until then.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:2253:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:258: **2026-07-05 00:34:30Z** - **BENCHMARK SESSION (10 GbE, TrueNAS↔Arch, claude + owner)**: The owner-called REV4 sign-off session ran end-to-end on the recorded pair (skippy = TrueNAS SCALE, glibc 2.36, binaries exec'd from the pool because /tmp and /home are noexec; client = netwatch-01 Arch, enp6s0 10 GbE). Env: **MTU 9000 both ends** (client NM profile was silently 1500 — set to 9000 via profile edit + full `connection up` reactivation after `device reapply` refused on immutable fields; 8972-byte DF probe green), **iperf3 baseline 9.88 Gbit/s single-stream / 9.91 both directions** — the parity reference (~1.24 GB/s). **Results (validated matrix, avg of 3 true cold runs each)**: TCP push 1 GiB **907 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** (first payload 14.5 ms), TCP pull 1 GiB **904 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** — both at the wire ceiling; gRPC-fallback push 1 GiB 1070 ms ≈ 8.0 Gbit/s (first payload 1.0–2.2 s scaling with manifest size — the design-4 no-mid-manifest-negotiation property, deliberate); gRPC pull ≈ TCP pull on large (880 vs 904 ms — 1 MiB-chunked fallback is wire-competitive on clean LAN), TCP 31% faster on 10k-small pulls; push 10k×4 KiB 2.29 s (~4.4k files/s) vs pull 433 ms (~23k files/s — push side per-file daemon work is the gap, w4-4/w9 territory); local blit beats rsync 2.3× on 1 GiB (157 vs 359 ms), no-op mirror 3 ms vs rsync 42 ms. **ue-1 loopback parity band: HOLDS** — local/push/pull per workload: large 195/178/162 ms, small 83/147/116, mixed 119/163/104; worst spread 1.8×, no 10×/2× gap (record: logs/bench_10gbe_20260704T201804/loopback_parity_band.csv). **Reverse direction validated** (daemon on client, skippy as pusher/puller through ufw): push 7.25 Gbit/s data-plane (first payload 3.9 ms), pull 9.75 Gbit/s. **ue-2 observation**: no organic mid-transfer resize triggered anywhere — a single stream saturates 10 GbE (daemon log: 1 GiB stream at 9.69 Gbit/s; negotiated extra streams close cleanly at 0 bytes), including under deliberate 2-concurrent-push contention (8.30+3.21 Gbit/s, clean completion); organic resize needs a constrained/variable wire — owner call whether the ue-r2-2 deterministic suite + this no-wedge evidence satisfies the gate. **Zero-copy datapoint** (D-2026-06-12-1 revisit): every transfer reported `zero-copy 0 bytes` yet the wire is saturated — splice wiring buys nothing at 10 GbE; CPU is not the bottleneck. **Methodology recorded**: engine-vs-wire isolation by design — tmpfs local ends, ZFS absorbing writes async, ARC-warm re-reads, no sync/ARC-eviction between runs (owner asked; disk-path variants — post-push `zpool sync` timing, cold-ARC pulls via `primarycache` — deferred as owner-gated follow-ups). **Session findings fixed en route**: bench_10gbe.sh had never survived past its local phase — stale endpoint grammar (missing load-bearing trailing slash) + daemon-only `--force-grpc-data` flag on the client, both silent aborts under run_timed's `2>/dev/null` + `set -e` (`b9befb8`); codex review of that fix returned 2 High (run_timed re-runs measure no-ops; shared module root lets gRPC pushes no-op and de-isolates pull labels) — both accepted, fixed `92d6326` (run_timed_fresh / per-run push subdirs / per-workload subpath pulls), validated by a full re-run (avg≈best every row). Verdicts: `.review/results/bench-script-fix.{codex,gpt-verdict}.md`. **New foot-gun filed**: `blit copy src_large dst` with an existing local dir named without `./` parses as an mDNS discovery endpoint and errors "remote source must include a module or root" (blit-app endpoints.rs) — STATE open question. Benchmark payload cleaned off the pool; binaries + bench.toml left at /mnt/generic-pool/video/blit-bin/ for future sessions. **Owner calls now pending**: ue-1 declaration (evidence says band holds), ue-2 declaration, zero-copy revisit verdict, REV4 → Shipped flip, and the GitHub push (w9-3 Windows CI check still queued behind it).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:2255:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:262: **2026-07-04 22:09:54Z** - **OWNER Q&A + PUSH (benchmark host plan, claude)**: Owner pushed `master` → `github` at `10d89e0` (first push carrying the w9-1/w9-4 ungated suites since the Windows triage — windows-latest CI on it is the "meaningfully green" check; `origin` gitea mirror not pushed, lags). Benchmark host question settled in Q&A: the 10 GbE **sign-off pair is TrueNAS (skippy) ↔ Arch client, all-Linux** — the zero-copy/splice revisit gate (D-2026-06-12-1) needs a Linux consumer, and ue-1's parity band should measure the engine without Windows I/O confounders (win_fs, TCP autotuning, filter drivers). Key facts recorded: the client box **dual-boots Win 11/Arch on identical hardware** (so a later TrueNAS→Win 11 bare-metal pull in the same hardware window gives a clean platform delta as a deployment-parity datapoint, not a gate), and a **Windows VM on the Arch install** exists for Windows-specific functional checks — explicitly never for perf numbers (virtio/NAT skews throughput). iperf3 baseline per pair before any Blit numbers. Plan recorded in STATE.md Queue item 2. Handoff commit follows; push of the handoff itself pending the owner's ref-listing go per push policy.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:2263:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:278: **2026-07-04 13:53:22Z** - **DECISIONS (owner Q&A, claude)**: The owner asked for the four standing questions "one at a time, no idea what these refer to" — each was presented in plain English with options and answered: (1) **commit erratum → leave as-is** (D-2026-07-04-2; mirrors the D-2026-06-07-1 no-rewrite calculus — two bisect-skippable commits beat force-pushing shared history); (2) **10 GbE session → "soon, but keep coding first"** (STATE Blocked reworded: not a daily blocker; owner will call "benchmark"); (3) **D-2026-06-20-1 stale warmup/size-gate wording → "follow the existing pattern"** — the ledger's own precedent (D-2026-06-20-2's veto annotation, D-2026-06-20-6's struck scope clause) IS edit-in-place-with-annotation, so the superseded framings are struck with pointers to -2 q1 and REV4/-5 (bounded-unilateral untouched — still true), and -5's "remains an open question" note resolved; (4) **supports_cancellation → flip it** (D-2026-07-04-3): CancelJob + TUI F2 will work on attached Push/PullSync transfers; policy-only after w4-3's race wiring; contract change (exit 2→0) recorded; implementation queued as **w4-5-supports-cancellation-flip**, now the topmost open REVIEW.md row. Batch `2a21d6f` through the codex loop per D-2026-07-04-1: **NEEDS FIXES (1 Medium + 1 Low, both STATE.md coherence)** — the Now bullet still called the erratum an open owner call, and the queue rewrite dropped the coder's-pick clause (design-3-vs-w2-2 ordering contradiction); both accepted, fixed `a928193`. The decision content itself passed all cross-checks (ledger consistency, w4-3 scope-note agreement, strike precision). check-docs.sh green. All on master, unpushed.

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:2592:.review/results/one-transfer-path-plan.codex.md:553:/usr/bin/zsh -lc 'rg -n "Status|sf-|ceiling|competitor|FAST|SIMPLE|RELIABLE|zero-copy|wire|Active|fallback|symmetric|benchmark|tripwire" docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md' in /home/michael/dev/Blit
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:2831:.review/results/sf-2-shape-correction-resize.codex.md:5092:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1817:**2026-07-04 13:53:22Z** - **DECISIONS (owner Q&A, claude)**: The owner asked for the four standing questions "one at a time, no idea what these refer to" — each was presented in plain English with options and answered: (1) **commit erratum → leave as-is** (D-2026-07-04-2; mirrors the D-2026-06-07-1 no-rewrite calculus — two bisect-skippable commits beat force-pushing shared history); (2) **10 GbE session → "soon, but keep coding first"** (STATE Blocked reworded: not a daily blocker; owner will call "benchmark"); (3) **D-2026-06-20-1 stale warmup/size-gate wording → "follow the existing pattern"** — the ledger's own precedent (D-2026-06-20-2's veto annotation, D-2026-06-20-6's struck scope clause) IS edit-in-place-with-annotation, so the superseded framings are struck with pointers to -2 q1 and REV4/-5 (bounded-unilateral untouched — still true), and -5's "remains an open question" note resolved; (4) **supports_cancellation → flip it** (D-2026-07-04-3): CancelJob + TUI F2 will work on attached Push/PullSync transfers; policy-only after w4-3's race wiring; contract change (exit 2→0) recorded; implementation queued as **w4-5-supports-cancellation-flip**, now the topmost open REVIEW.md row. Batch `2a21d6f` through the codex loop per D-2026-07-04-1: **NEEDS FIXES (1 Medium + 1 Low, both STATE.md coherence)** — the Now bullet still called the erratum an open owner call, and the queue rewrite dropped the coder's-pick clause (design-3-vs-w2-2 ordering contradiction); both accepted, fixed `a928193`. .review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:2957:.review/results/sf-1-tripwire-harness.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit 7202c1a (run: git show 7202c1a). It implements sf-1 of docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md (Active): a tripwire + stream-scaling benchmark harness, scripts/bench_tripwires.sh — no production code. Spec: full matrix (blit, rsyncd spun on the daemon host over ssh, rsync-over-ssh, rclone best-config, cp -a for local cells), fresh never-seen targets every timed run, CSV schema identical to docs/bench/10gbe-2026-07-05/tool_comparison.csv, one-command run against any daemon host, plus a stream-scaling probe mode (files/s vs actual negotiated stream count). Check: shell correctness (quoting, set -euo pipefail interactions, trap/teardown safety — it must never rm anything it did not create), fairness of the tool comparison methodology (fresh targets, shared data root, warm-read parity, rclone fairness flags per DIAGNOSIS.md), the tripwire/exit-code contract, the baseline-diff awk, the stream-count measurement validity, and FAST/SIMPLE/RELIABLE fit. Also check the .review/findings/sf-1-tripwire-harness.md record and the REVIEW.md row for accuracy. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3010:.review/results/sf-1-tripwire-harness.codex.md:2328:**2026-07-05 00:34:30Z** - **BENCHMARK SESSION (10 GbE, TrueNAS↔Arch, claude + owner)**: The owner-called REV4 sign-off session ran end-to-end on the recorded pair (skippy = TrueNAS SCALE, glibc 2.36, binaries exec'd from the pool because /tmp and /home are noexec; client = netwatch-01 Arch, enp6s0 10 GbE). Env: **MTU 9000 both ends** (client NM profile was silently 1500 — set to 9000 via profile edit + full `connection up` reactivation after `device reapply` refused on immutable fields; 8972-byte DF probe green), **iperf3 baseline 9.88 Gbit/s single-stream / 9.91 both directions** — the parity reference (~1.24 GB/s). **Results (validated matrix, avg of 3 true cold runs each)**: TCP push 1 GiB **907 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** (first payload 14.5 ms), TCP pull 1 GiB **904 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** — both at the wire ceiling; gRPC-fallback push 1 GiB 1070 ms ≈ 8.0 Gbit/s (first payload 1.0–2.2 s scaling with manifest size — the design-4 no-mid-manifest-negotiation property, deliberate); gRPC pull ≈ TCP pull on large (880 vs 904 ms — 1 MiB-chunked fallback is wire-competitive on clean LAN), TCP 31% faster on 10k-small pulls; push 10k×4 KiB 2.29 s (~4.4k files/s) vs pull 433 ms (~23k files/s — push side per-file daemon work is the gap, w4-4/w9 territory); local blit beats rsync 2.3× on 1 GiB (157 vs 359 ms), no-op mirror 3 ms vs rsync 42 ms. **ue-1 loopback parity band: HOLDS** — local/push/pull per workload: large 195/178/162 ms, small 83/147/116, mixed 119/163/104; worst spread 1.8×, no 10×/2× gap (record: logs/bench_10gbe_20260704T201804/loopback_parity_band.csv). **Reverse direction validated** (daemon on client, skippy as pusher/puller through ufw): push 7.25 Gbit/s data-plane (first payload 3.9 ms), pull 9.75 Gbit/s. **ue-2 observation**: no organic mid-transfer resize triggered anywhere — a single stream saturates 10 GbE (daemon log: 1 GiB stream at 9.69 Gbit/s; negotiated extra streams close cleanly at 0 bytes), including under deliberate 2-concurrent-push contention (8.30+3.21 Gbit/s, clean completion); organic resize needs a constrained/variable wire — owner call whether the ue-r2-2 deterministic suite + this no-wedge evidence satisfies the gate. **Zero-copy datapoint** (D-2026-06-12-1 revisit): every transfer reported `zero-copy 0 bytes` yet the wire is saturated — splice wiring buys nothing at 10 GbE; CPU is not the bottleneck. **Methodology recorded**: engine-vs-wire isolation by design — tmpfs local ends, ZFS absorbing writes async, ARC-warm re-reads, no sync/ARC-eviction between runs (owner asked; disk-path variants — post-push `zpool sync` timing, cold-ARC pulls via `primarycache` — deferred as owner-gated follow-ups). **Session findings fixed en route**: bench_10gbe.sh had never survived past its local phase — stale endpoint grammar (missing load-bearing trailing slash) + daemon-only `--force-grpc-data` flag on the client, both silent aborts under run_timed's `2>/dev/null` + `set -e` (`b9befb8`); codex review of that fix returned 2 High (run_timed re-runs measure no-ops; shared module root lets gRPC pushes no-op and de-isolates pull labels) — both accepted, fixed `92d6326` (run_timed_fresh / per-run push subdirs / per-workload subpath pulls), validated by a full re-run (avg≈best every row). Verdicts: `.review/results/bench-script-fix.{codex,gpt-verdict}.md`. **New foot-gun filed**: `blit copy src_large dst` with an existing local dir named without `./` parses as an mDNS discovery endpoint and errors "remote source must include a module or root" (blit-app endpoints.rs) — STATE open question. Benchmark payload cleaned off the pool; binaries + bench.toml left at /mnt/generic-pool/video/blit-bin/ for future sessions. **Owner calls now pending**: ue-1 declaration (evidence says band holds), ue-2 declaration, zero-copy revisit verdict, REV4 → Shipped flip, and the GitHub push (w9-3 Windows CI check still queued behind it).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3012:.review/results/sf-1-tripwire-harness.codex.md:2332:**2026-07-04 22:09:54Z** - **OWNER Q&A + PUSH (benchmark host plan, claude)**: Owner pushed `master` → `github` at `10d89e0` (first push carrying the w9-1/w9-4 ungated suites since the Windows triage — windows-latest CI on it is the "meaningfully green" check; `origin` gitea mirror not pushed, lags). Benchmark host question settled in Q&A: the 10 GbE **sign-off pair is TrueNAS (skippy) ↔ Arch client, all-Linux** — the zero-copy/splice revisit gate (D-2026-06-12-1) needs a Linux consumer, and ue-1's parity band should measure the engine without Windows I/O confounders (win_fs, TCP autotuning, filter drivers). Key facts recorded: the client box **dual-boots Win 11/Arch on identical hardware** (so a later TrueNAS→Win 11 bare-metal pull in the same hardware window gives a clean platform delta as a deployment-parity datapoint, not a gate), and a **Windows VM on the Arch install** exists for Windows-specific functional checks — explicitly never for perf numbers (virtio/NAT skews throughput). iperf3 baseline per pair before any Blit numbers. Plan recorded in STATE.md Queue item 2. Handoff commit follows; push of the handoff itself pending the owner's ref-listing go per push policy.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3075:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit 78eabfd (run: git show 78eabfd). It adds docs/plan/SMALL_FILE_CEILING.md (Status: Draft, plan-doc only, no code) plus a STATE.md queue entry. This is a PLAN review per docs/agent/GPT_REVIEW_LOOP.md: check internal coherence (goal vs acceptance criteria vs slices vs constraints), no contradiction with docs/DECISIONS.md (esp. D-2026-06-20-1 bounded-unilateral dial contract, D-2026-07-04-1 review loop, D-2026-06-12-1 zero-copy) or docs/plan/UNIFIED_TRANSFER_ENGINE_REV4.md, and that the measured claims match the repo's benchmark records (DEVLOG 2026-07-05 entries; logs/ CSVs are gitignored — the DEVLOG digest is the in-repo record). Key principle the doc must consistently embody (owner, 2026-07-05): goals are hardware-ceiling-driven, never competitor-relative; other tools are tripwires only. Flag any residual competitor-relative framing, any slice not sized for one review-loop pass, any acceptance criterion that is not observable/testable, and any invariant risk (SIMPLE: dial stays single tuning owner; RELIABLE: REV4 invariants; wire-compat gate on sf-6). Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3200:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1883:**2026-05-13 18:30:00Z** - **FEAT + SCOPE**: §3.1 / §3.3 / §5.2 / §5.4 closed (D5 modified, D6 default taken, BlitAuth + AI telemetry removed). Three owner decisions landed in one commit set: **(1) §3.1 D5 modified.** Rather than ship `--metrics` as dormant scaffolding, the daemon now emits a one-line stderr summary at end of each push/pull/pull_sync RPC when `--metrics` is on. Format: `[metrics] push ok in 1.23s (push_ops=N pull_ops=N purge_ops=N active=N errors=N)` — rsync/rclone-style operator visibility under systemd / foreground without needing the TUI. `crates/blit-daemon/src/metrics.rs` gains `log_completion(op_kind, duration, ok)` (no-op when disabled — atomics never loaded) and `format_completion_line` (extracted so the format contract is unit-testable without stderr capture). 3 new unit tests in `metrics::tests` pin the line format and error tagging. Counters themselves stay in place and feed future TUI work; the change just gives `--metrics` a real consumer for 0.1.0. **(2) §3.3 D6 default taken.** Phase 4.8 daemon FS-capability work (4.8.2 startup/idle daemon probes + 4.8.3 `blit diagnostics profile` capability probes) explicitly deferred to 0.2.0. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.8 now carries a scope-note callout marking 4.8.2 and 4.8.3 as deferred; 4.8.1 client-side `fs_capability` cache ships in 0.1.0 as today. **(3) §5.2 BlitAuth removed.** `service BlitAuth { Authenticate(AuthRequest) returns (AuthResponse) }` plus the two messages stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` (forward-compat auth passthrough hook for delegated pull) also removed — field 10 reserved against future reuse. `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` no longer sets the field. `DAEMON_CONFIG.md` Trust Model section rewritten to say "no auth, intentional, out of scope — terminate auth at the network edge" (was "no auth in 0.1.0; passthrough field exists for forward-compat"). `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` got a top-of-file auth note flagging body references to BlitAuth/`delegated_credential` as obsolete (body retained as historical design record). **(4) §5.4 AI telemetry removed.** `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` deleted entirely. References in `docs/plan/README.md`, `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` dropped. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.9 marked as removed from scope. Predictor (§2.8) is unaffected — performance history is still collected; no "analyze my history" feature is planned. **Release-plan tracker:** §3.1 / §3.2 / §3.3 all ✅ closed. §5.2 / §5.4 changed from "deferred" to "removed." `--metrics` decision (D5) modified from default; all other D-decisions taken at their plan defaults. **Net release-blocker count:** §2.6 (hardware-bound 10 GbE benchmark capture) is the last open P0. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+3 metrics tests); `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check` clean.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3202:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1891:**2026-05-07 14:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup #2 catching review of `8d43e4d` — the RELEASE_PLAN_v2 itself was still presenting closed P0s as open work, which was a serious risk because `docs/plan/README.md` points new agents at it as the live source of truth for what remains before shipping. **Medium**: added a top-of-file "Closure tracker (last updated 2026-05-07)" block with a one-row-per-section table showing closing commits for §2.1/§2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.7/§2.8 and §4, plus pending markers for §2.6 (hardware-bound benchmark — last open P0) and §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 (P1, doc-only). Each P0 section now has its own per-section `**Status (2026-05-07):** ✅ Closed <commit>` block at the top so a reader landing on a specific section sees current state without scrolling back to the top. §2.5 line 265 also still showed the old `blit completions remote <prefix>` syntax in the recommendation paragraph — corrected to the actual `<REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` form, matching the existing line-21 manpage. §6 commit-sequence rewritten as a status table with closing commits per row (P0 done except step 2 §2.6; P1 deferrable). §7 decisions table flipped from "Default recommendation" / "Owner" columns to "Default" / "Outcome" with closing commits where taken — D1/D2/D3/D7/D8/D9 ✅ taken, D4/D5/D6 still open. Test totals updated from the 2026-05-04 baseline (383) to current (407, after R41-R45 review-fix coverage). Footer "Last updated" + "Next review" rewritten. **Low**: `docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md:83` Success Criteria still said `blit-cli mirror -p -v local remote`; updated to `blit mirror`. Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3210:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2019:**2026-07-05 00:34:30Z** - **BENCHMARK SESSION (10 GbE, TrueNAS↔Arch, claude + owner)**: The owner-called REV4 sign-off session ran end-to-end on the recorded pair (skippy = TrueNAS SCALE, glibc 2.36, binaries exec'd from the pool because /tmp and /home are noexec; client = netwatch-01 Arch, enp6s0 10 GbE). Env: **MTU 9000 both ends** (client NM profile was silently 1500 — set to 9000 via profile edit + full `connection up` reactivation after `device reapply` refused on immutable fields; 8972-byte DF probe green), **iperf3 baseline 9.88 Gbit/s single-stream / 9.91 both directions** — the parity reference (~1.24 GB/s). **Results (validated matrix, avg of 3 true cold runs each)**: TCP push 1 GiB **907 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** (first payload 14.5 ms), TCP pull 1 GiB **904 ms ≈ 9.5 Gbit/s** — both at the wire ceiling; gRPC-fallback push 1 GiB 1070 ms ≈ 8.0 Gbit/s (first payload 1.0–2.2 s scaling with manifest size — the design-4 no-mid-manifest-negotiation property, deliberate); gRPC pull ≈ TCP pull on large (880 vs 904 ms — 1 MiB-chunked fallback is wire-competitive on clean LAN), TCP 31% faster on 10k-small pulls; push 10k×4 KiB 2.29 s (~4.4k files/s) vs pull 433 ms (~23k files/s — push side per-file daemon work is the gap, w4-4/w9 territory); local blit beats rsync 2.3× on 1 GiB (157 vs 359 ms), no-op mirror 3 ms vs rsync 42 ms. **ue-1 loopback parity band: HOLDS** — local/push/pull per workload: large 195/178/162 ms, small 83/147/116, mixed 119/163/104; worst spread 1.8×, no 10×/2× gap (record: logs/bench_10gbe_20260704T201804/loopback_parity_band.csv). **Reverse direction validated** (daemon on client, skippy as pusher/puller through ufw): push 7.25 Gbit/s data-plane (first payload 3.9 ms), pull 9.75 Gbit/s. **ue-2 observation**: no organic mid-transfer resize triggered anywhere — a single stream saturates 10 GbE (daemon log: 1 GiB stream at 9.69 Gbit/s; negotiated extra streams close cleanly at 0 bytes), including under deliberate 2-concurrent-push contention (8.30+3.21 Gbit/s, clean completion); organic resize needs a constrained/variable wire — owner call whether the ue-r2-2 deterministic suite + this no-wedge evidence satisfies the gate. **Zero-copy datapoint** (D-2026-06-12-1 revisit): every transfer reported `zero-copy 0 bytes` yet the wire is saturated — splice wiring buys nothing at 10 GbE; CPU is not the bottleneck. **Methodology recorded**: engine-vs-wire isolation by design — tmpfs local ends, ZFS absorbing writes async, ARC-warm re-reads, no sync/ARC-eviction between runs (owner asked; disk-path variants — post-push `zpool sync` timing, cold-ARC pulls via `primarycache` — deferred as owner-gated follow-ups). **Session findings fixed en route**: bench_10gbe.sh had never survived past its local phase — stale endpoint grammar (missing load-bearing trailing slash) + daemon-only `--force-grpc-data` flag on the client, both silent aborts under run_timed's `2>/dev/null` + `set -e` (`b9befb8`); codex review of that fix returned 2 High (run_timed re-runs measure no-ops; shared module root lets gRPC pushes no-op and de-isolates pull labels) — both accepted, fixed `92d6326` (run_timed_fresh / per-run push subdirs / per-workload subpath pulls), validated by a full re-run (avg≈best every row). Verdicts: `.review/results/bench-script-fix.{codex,gpt-verdict}.md`. **New foot-gun filed**: `blit copy src_large dst` with an existing local dir named without `./` parses as an mDNS discovery endpoint and errors "remote source must include a module or root" (blit-app endpoints.rs) — STATE open question. Benchmark payload cleaned off the pool; binaries + bench.toml left at /mnt/generic-pool/video/blit-bin/ for future sessions. **Owner calls now pending**: ue-1 declaration (evidence says band holds), ue-2 declaration, zero-copy revisit verdict, REV4 → Shipped flip, and the GitHub push (w9-3 Windows CI check still queued behind it).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3212:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2023:**2026-07-04 22:09:54Z** - **OWNER Q&A + PUSH (benchmark host plan, claude)**: Owner pushed `master` → `github` at `10d89e0` (first push carrying the w9-1/w9-4 ungated suites since the Windows triage — windows-latest CI on it is the "meaningfully green" check; `origin` gitea mirror not pushed, lags). Benchmark host question settled in Q&A: the 10 GbE **sign-off pair is TrueNAS (skippy) ↔ Arch client, all-Linux** — the zero-copy/splice revisit gate (D-2026-06-12-1) needs a Linux consumer, and ue-1's parity band should measure the engine without Windows I/O confounders (win_fs, TCP autotuning, filter drivers). Key facts recorded: the client box **dual-boots Win 11/Arch on identical hardware** (so a later TrueNAS→Win 11 bare-metal pull in the same hardware window gives a clean platform delta as a deployment-parity datapoint, not a gate), and a **Windows VM on the Arch install** exists for Windows-specific functional checks — explicitly never for perf numbers (virtio/NAT skews throughput). iperf3 baseline per pair before any Blit numbers. Plan recorded in STATE.md Queue item 2. Handoff commit follows; push of the handoff itself pending the owner's ref-listing go per push policy.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3220:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2039:**2026-07-04 13:53:22Z** - **DECISIONS (owner Q&A, claude)**: The owner asked for the four standing questions "one at a time, no idea what these refer to" — each was presented in plain English with options and answered: (1) **commit erratum → leave as-is** (D-2026-07-04-2; mirrors the D-2026-06-07-1 no-rewrite calculus — two bisect-skippable commits beat force-pushing shared history); (2) **10 GbE session → "soon, but keep coding first"** (STATE Blocked reworded: not a daily blocker; owner will call "benchmark"); (3) **D-2026-06-20-1 stale warmup/size-gate wording → "follow the existing pattern"** — the ledger's own precedent (D-2026-06-20-2's veto annotation, D-2026-06-20-6's struck scope clause) IS edit-in-place-with-annotation, so the superseded framings are struck with pointers to -2 q1 and REV4/-5 (bounded-unilateral untouched — still true), and -5's "remains an open question" note resolved; (4) **supports_cancellation → flip it** (D-2026-07-04-3): CancelJob + TUI F2 will work on attached Push/PullSync transfers; policy-only after w4-3's race wiring; contract change (exit 2→0) recorded; implementation queued as **w4-5-supports-cancellation-flip**, now the topmost open REVIEW.md row. Batch `2a21d6f` through the codex loop per D-2026-07-04-1: **NEEDS FIXES (1 Medium + 1 Low, both STATE.md coherence)** — the Now bullet still called the erratum an open owner call, and the queue rewrite dropped the coder's-pick clause (design-3-vs-w2-2 ordering contradiction); both accepted, fixed `a928193`. The decision content itself passed all cross-checks (ledger consistency, w4-3 scope-note agreement, strike precision). check-docs.sh green. All on master, unpushed.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3237:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2105:**2026-05-13 18:30:00Z** - **FEAT + SCOPE**: §3.1 / §3.3 / §5.2 / §5.4 closed (D5 modified, D6 default taken, BlitAuth + AI telemetry removed). Three owner decisions landed in one commit set: **(1) §3.1 D5 modified.** Rather than ship `--metrics` as dormant scaffolding, the daemon now emits a one-line stderr summary at end of each push/pull/pull_sync RPC when `--metrics` is on. Format: `[metrics] push ok in 1.23s (push_ops=N pull_ops=N purge_ops=N active=N errors=N)` — rsync/rclone-style operator visibility under systemd / foreground without needing the TUI. `crates/blit-daemon/src/metrics.rs` gains `log_completion(op_kind, duration, ok)` (no-op when disabled — atomics never loaded) and `format_completion_line` (extracted so the format contract is unit-testable without stderr capture). 3 new unit tests in `metrics::tests` pin the line format and error tagging. Counters themselves stay in place and feed future TUI work; the change just gives `--metrics` a real consumer for 0.1.0. **(2) §3.3 D6 default taken.** Phase 4.8 daemon FS-capability work (4.8.2 startup/idle daemon probes + 4.8.3 `blit diagnostics profile` capability probes) explicitly deferred to 0.2.0. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.8 now carries a scope-note callout marking 4.8.2 and 4.8.3 as deferred; 4.8.1 client-side `fs_capability` cache ships in 0.1.0 as today. **(3) §5.2 BlitAuth removed.** `service BlitAuth { Authenticate(AuthRequest) returns (AuthResponse) }` plus the two messages stripped from `proto/blit.proto`. `RemoteSourceLocator.delegated_credential` (forward-compat auth passthrough hook for delegated pull) also removed — field 10 reserved against future reuse. `crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/remote_remote_direct.rs` no longer sets the field. `DAEMON_CONFIG.md` Trust Model section rewritten to say "no auth, intentional, out of scope — terminate auth at the network edge" (was "no auth in 0.1.0; passthrough field exists for forward-compat"). `docs/plan/REMOTE_REMOTE_DELEGATION_PLAN.md` got a top-of-file auth note flagging body references to BlitAuth/`delegated_credential` as obsolete (body retained as historical design record). **(4) §5.4 AI telemetry removed.** `docs/plan/AI_TELEMETRY_ANALYSIS.md` deleted entirely. References in `docs/plan/README.md`, `docs/plan/PROJECT_STATE_ASSESSMENT.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` dropped. `WORKFLOW_PHASE_4.md` §4.9 marked as removed from scope. Predictor (§2.8) is unaffected — performance history is still collected; no "analyze my history" feature is planned. **Release-plan tracker:** §3.1 / §3.2 / §3.3 all ✅ closed. §5.2 / §5.4 changed from "deferred" to "removed." `--metrics` decision (D5) modified from default; all other D-decisions taken at their plan defaults. **Net release-blocker count:** §2.6 (hardware-bound 10 GbE benchmark capture) is the last open P0. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` all pass (+3 metrics tests); `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check` clean.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3239:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2113:**2026-05-07 14:00:00Z** - **DOCS**: Followup #2 catching review of `8d43e4d` — the RELEASE_PLAN_v2 itself was still presenting closed P0s as open work, which was a serious risk because `docs/plan/README.md` points new agents at it as the live source of truth for what remains before shipping. **Medium**: added a top-of-file "Closure tracker (last updated 2026-05-07)" block with a one-row-per-section table showing closing commits for §2.1/§2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.7/§2.8 and §4, plus pending markers for §2.6 (hardware-bound benchmark — last open P0) and §3.1/§3.2/§3.3 (P1, doc-only). Each P0 section now has its own per-section `**Status (2026-05-07):** ✅ Closed <commit>` block at the top so a reader landing on a specific section sees current state without scrolling back to the top. §2.5 line 265 also still showed the old `blit completions remote <prefix>` syntax in the recommendation paragraph — corrected to the actual `<REMOTE> [--prefix <STR>] [--files] [--dirs]` form, matching the existing line-21 manpage. §6 commit-sequence rewritten as a status table with closing commits per row (P0 done except step 2 §2.6; P1 deferrable). §7 decisions table flipped from "Default recommendation" / "Owner" columns to "Default" / "Outcome" with closing commits where taken — D1/D2/D3/D7/D8/D9 ✅ taken, D4/D5/D6 still open. Test totals updated from the 2026-05-04 baseline (383) to current (407, after R41-R45 review-fix coverage). Footer "Last updated" + "Next review" rewritten. **Low**: `docs/plan/REMOTE_TRANSFER_PARITY.md:83` Success Criteria still said `blit-cli mirror -p -v local remote`; updated to `blit mirror`. Unlike POST_REVIEW_FIXES.md, this file is listed as still-applicable design in `docs/plan/README.md`, so a stale binary name there would mislead. **Tests**: `cargo test --workspace` 407 pass / 0 fail (no code touched).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3744:.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:10254:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:770:- `retry/wait CLI defaults` = retry=0, wait=5s, fixed spacing (run_with_retries, blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55) (crates/blit-cli/src/cli.rs:278) — SIMPLE: an explicit user knob in a zero-tuning tool (owner-approved robocopy parity); no backoff — fixed 5s spacing is a frozen recovery policy.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3745:.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:10259:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:810:- /home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-app/src/transfers/retry.rs:55 — run_with_retries — retry count/wait come from CLI flags (default retry=0, wait=5s per blit-cli/src/cli.rs:633-639); fixed (non-backoff) spacing — User-tunable knob (mild SIMPLE tension, owner-approved robocopy parity); constant wait rather than exponential backoff.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3808:.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:11033:docs/audit/2026-05-04_roadmap_audit.md:78:| Windows CopyFileExW, USN Change Journal, ReFS block clone | SHIPPING (block clone gated on privilege) | `copy/windows.rs`; `change_journal/snapshot.rs::windows`; `fs_capability/windows.rs:54+` `probe_block_clone_support`. `win_fs.rs:134+` documents `SeManageVolumePrivilege` requirement. | ReFS benchmark in `WORKFLOW_PHASE_2.5.md:72` shows blit 0.59s vs robocopy 0.165s — block clone path operates but ~3.5× slower than robocopy. Privilege investigation is `TODO.md:259` open item. |
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3985:   140	- Decision: The D-2026-06-12-1 revisit gate ("receive-side CPU saturation") is **declared met by the owner** (2026-07-05): a UniFi UNAS 8 Pro daemon target whose CPU cannot saturate 10 GbE even from SSD cache. Zero-copy receive is unparked as sanctioned FAST work. Two clarifications: (a) the dead `zero_copy.rs` module still gets deleted as ratified — its EAGAIN busy-wait draft is a rewrite, not a revival (eval doc); (b) the capability returns the one-path way (owner exchange 2026-07-05): a **runtime-selected write strategy inside the unified receive sink** — the eval doc's revisit design (`AsyncFd`-readiness splice loop beside the buffered relay, selected when the reader is a raw TcpStream and the payload is a file record, buffered relay as universal fallback), capability-gated by kernel/fs support, identical in both roles — never a side path. Sequenced after ONE_TRANSFER_PATH's cutover (otp-10) as its own slice set; the UNAS is the measurement rig and the symmetric-endpoint benchmark rule (D-2026-07-05-2 era methodology) applies to its cells.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:3987:   142	- Supersedes: the STATE Blocked "zero-copy option a/b/c" question and the "zero-copy revisit verdict" item among the four 10 GbE owner declarations (both resolved by this entry); amends D-2026-06-12-1's revisit-gate framing from "10 GbE benchmarks showing receive-side CPU saturation" to "a CPU-bound receiver exists" (annotated in the eval doc). D-2026-06-12-1's deletion of the dead module stands.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:4018:    76	- **Converge up, not down**: per benchmark cell, the unified session
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:4138:docs/DECISIONS.md:140:- Decision: The D-2026-06-12-1 revisit gate ("receive-side CPU saturation") is **declared met by the owner** (2026-07-05): a UniFi UNAS 8 Pro daemon target whose CPU cannot saturate 10 GbE even from SSD cache. Zero-copy receive is unparked as sanctioned FAST work. D-2026-06-12-1's deletion of the dead module stands.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:4169:DEVLOG.md:12:**2026-07-05 07:30:00Z** - **DIRECTIVE + PLAN (ONE_TRANSFER_PATH draft, D-2026-07-05-1, claude)**: Owner rejected the push/pull disparity, the mixed-fs benchmark methodology ("tmp on one side, spinning rust on the other is not a valid test"), and the explanation that direction symmetry lives in four separate driver loops — then issued the directive now recorded verbatim in `docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md`: **"ONE BLOCK OF CODE that does the transfer. no POSSIBILITY OF ANYTHING EVER using anything else because anything else does not exist"**; "I NEVER see a situation where pull is faster than push or vice versa... because of something blit did"; identical whether initiated as push from skippy or pull from this machine. Scope/wire/process explicitly delegated to the agent. **Plan drafted through the plan procedure**: one `TransferSession` (roles SOURCE/DESTINATION selected by initiator/verb, never code paths), one bidi `Transfer` RPC replacing `Push`+`PullSync` (no back-compat, lockstep upgrade — repo precedent PullSyncHeader), one choreography (source streams manifest immediately; destination diffs incrementally; sf-2 shape-corrected dial as the only stream policy — absorbs the pull 1s-start residue), gRPC fallback demoted to a byte-carrier option, delegated pull = daemon-initiated session, local transfers on an in-process transport in the final phase; slices otp-1..12 ending in a **deletion slice** (the four drivers + both RPCs removed, file-by-file proof) and a **symmetric-rig acceptance matrix** (same-fs disk-to-disk verdict cells, cold caches, tmpfs as labeled wire-reference only; initiator/verb A/B within ±10%). Converge-up constraint: per cell the unified path must match the better of today's directions, not the average. **D-2026-07-05-1 recorded**: directive + SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused at sf-2 (sf-3a+ blocked; principle D-2026-07-04-4 stands) + design queue paused (w7-1 likely absorbed by otp-5). STATE rewritten around the new queue. Next: codex plan review of this commit, adjudicate, fix, then STOP for the owner's Active flip — no implementation anywhere until then.
