/bin/zsh -lc 'nl -ba scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1' in /Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# local-ab-run.ps1 — ONE timed local D:->E: run on netwatch-01 (blit vs robocopy).
     2	#
     3	# Runs entirely on Windows so the ssh round trip stays OUTSIDE the timed window
     4	# (the otp-2w F3 rule). Methodology matches the blit rig harnesses exactly —
     5	# anything less is not comparable to the otp-12 numbers:
     6	#   * cold caches before every run (standby-list purge);
     7	#   * writeback DRAINED before the window opens (a dirty queue from the previous
     8	#     run would otherwise be charged to this one);
     9	#   * destination container precreated OUTSIDE the window on BOTH arms, so
    10	#     neither tool pays a mkdir the other does not (otp-12b F5);
    11	#   * durability keyed by the DESTINATION volume, never by the tool — the bug
    12	#     that invalidated the first Linux session (docs/bench/otp12-perf README);
    13	#   * the landed file count is returned so a tool that "succeeds" while writing
    14	#     nothing is caught instead of scoring a fast time.
    15	#
    16	# Prints exactly one sentinel-framed record:  R:<ms>,<flush_ms>,<rc>,<files>,<drain>:R
    17	# Anything else (a crash, pwsh noise) fails to parse and VOIDS the run — nothing
    18	# can masquerade as a time.
    19	
    20	param(
    21	    [Parameter(Mandatory)][ValidateSet('blit', 'robocopy')][string]$Tool,
    22	    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Src,        # D:\...\src_mixed   (NO trailing slash)
    23	    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$DestRoot,   # E:\blit-local-bench\<tag>
    24	    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$BlitExe,
    25	    [string]$DestDrive = 'E',
    26	    [string]$PurgeScript = 'D:\blit-test\purge-standby.ps1',
    27	    # Concurrency knobs — the two tools must be compared at EQUAL concurrency
    28	    # or the result conflates "blit's per-file path is slow" with "blit ships
    29	    # one worker". robocopy /MT:8 vs blit's default single apply worker is NOT
    30	    # a like-for-like comparison (owner, 2026-07-13).
    31	    #   RoboThreads : robocopy /MT:N. NOTE robocopy with NO /MT is
    32	    #                 single-threaded; /MT:8 is what we passed originally.
    33	    #   BlitWorkers : 0 = ship default (ONE apply worker —
    34	    #                 transfer_session/local.rs:602). N>0 passes `--workers N`,
    35	    #                 which sets debug_mode and widens sink_workers to N. That
    36	    #                 flag's ONLY effect on the local session is the worker
    37	    #                 count (local.rs:328-332), so it is a clean lever.
    38	    [int]$RoboThreads = 8,
    39	    [int]$BlitWorkers = 0
    40	)
    41	
    42	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
    43	
    44	# --- cold caches (untimed) --------------------------------------------------
    45	& $PurgeScript | Out-Null
    46	
    47	# --- drain writeback (untimed): quiet = <1 MB/s for 3 consecutive 2s samples -
    48	Write-VolumeCache -DriveLetter $DestDrive
    49	$drain = 'DRAIN-TIMEOUT'
    50	$quiet = 0
    51	for ($i = 0; $i -lt 60; $i++) {
    52	    $w = (Get-Counter "\PhysicalDisk(_Total)\Disk Write Bytes/sec" -SampleInterval 2 -MaxSamples 1).CounterSamples[0].CookedValue
    53	    if ($null -ne $w -and [double]$w -lt 1048576) { $quiet++ } else { $quiet = 0 }
    54	    if ($quiet -ge 3) { $drain = "drained_$((($i + 1) * 2))s"; break }
    55	}
    56	
    57	# --- destination container, precreated OUTSIDE the window (both arms) --------
    58	# blit nests a no-trailing-slash source under the destination, so `blit copy
    59	# <Src> <DestRoot>` lands <DestRoot>\<leaf>. robocopy copies CONTENTS, so it is
    60	# pointed at <DestRoot>\<leaf> directly. Both arms therefore land an identical
    61	# tree and neither pays for the container.
    62	$leaf = Split-Path $Src -Leaf
    63	$dest = Join-Path $DestRoot $leaf
    64	New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dest | Out-Null
    65	
    66	# --- the timed window -------------------------------------------------------
    67	$sw = [Diagnostics.Stopwatch]::StartNew()
    68	if ($Tool -eq 'blit') {
    69	    if ($BlitWorkers -gt 0) {
    70	        & $BlitExe copy $Src $DestRoot --yes --workers $BlitWorkers > $null 2>&1
    71	    }
    72	    else {
    73	        & $BlitExe copy $Src $DestRoot --yes > $null 2>&1
    74	    }
    75	    $rc = $LASTEXITCODE
    76	}
    77	else {
    78	    # Retries bounded so a transient error cannot hang the run for hours on
    79	    # robocopy's default /R:1000000.
    80	    robocopy $Src $dest /E /MT:$RoboThreads /R:2 /W:2 /NFL /NDL /NJH /NJS > $null 2>&1
    81	    $rc = $LASTEXITCODE
    82	}
    83	$sw.Stop()
    84	$ms = [int]$sw.Elapsed.TotalMilliseconds
    85	
    86	# --- durability, self-timed, keyed by the DESTINATION volume ----------------
    87	$flush = -1
    88	try {
    89	    $a = [DateTimeOffset]::UtcNow.ToUnixTimeMilliseconds()
    90	    Write-VolumeCache -DriveLetter $DestDrive -ErrorAction Stop
    91	    $b = [DateTimeOffset]::UtcNow.ToUnixTimeMilliseconds()
    92	    $flush = [int]($b - $a)
    93	}
    94	catch { $flush = -1 }   # caller VOIDS the run: a failed flush is not a fast run
    95	
    96	# --- did the bytes actually land? -------------------------------------------
    97	$files = (Get-ChildItem $dest -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object).Count
    98	
    99	Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $DestRoot -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   100	
   101	# ${drain} braces are LOAD-BEARING: PowerShell parses a bare `$drain:R` as a
   102	# SCOPE-qualified variable (like $env:PATH), so the closing sentinel silently
   103	# vanishes and every run parses as a void. Same trap as bench_otp12_win.sh:520
   104	# — documented there, and reproduced here anyway on the first run.
   105	"R:$ms,$flush,$rc,$files,${drain}:R"

codex
Two facts are already important: the harness’s current “warm-up fixes teardown” comment is an asserted diagnosis, not something the counters prove; and the drain samples `_Total`, not specifically E:. I’m separating those claims from what the code actually guarantees and checking the recorded run order.

   145	# --- N-arm interleaved cell (the cross-session bi-stability fix) -------------
   146	# WHY (found live 2026-07-13): blit's DEFAULT config measured 1388ms for
   147	# `small` in one session and 2225ms in another — a 60% swing on the same
   148	# binary, same flags, flat within each session — while robocopy /MT:8 measured
   149	# 697ms in BOTH. The rig is stable for robocopy and bi-stable for blit, cause

   151	# Fix: put EVERY arm in ONE session, rotating the start arm per slot so no arm

   154	#
   155	# ARMS entries: "<label>:<tool>:<n>"  — n = --workers for blit (0 = the SHIPPED
   156	# default, no flag), /MT:n for robocopy.
   157	ARMS_SPEC="${ARMS_SPEC:-blit_ship:blit:0,blit_w8:blit:8,robo_mt1:robocopy:1,robo_mt8:robocopy:8}"
   158	
   159	run_cell_multi() {   # fixture — all arms interleaved, slot-voided
   160	    local w="$1" slot=1 attempts=0 valid=0 max=$(( 2 * RUNS ))
   161	    local -a arms=(); IFS=, read -r -a arms <<< "$ARMS_SPEC"
   162	    local n_arms=${#arms[@]}
   163	    log "=== $w ($n_arms arms interleaved, rotating start, $RUNS slots) ==="
   164	    log "    arms: ${arms[*]}"
   165	    # One untimed warm-up (absorbs the previous cell's teardown still settling).
   166	    ARM_TOOL=blit; ARM_N=0
   167	    one_run "$w" blit "${SESSION}_${w}_warmup" || true
   168	    log "  $w/warmup (untimed, discarded): ${RUN_MS}ms ($RUN_DRAIN)"
   169	    while (( valid < RUNS && attempts < max )); do
   170	        attempts=$(( attempts + 1 ))
   171	        local slot_valid=yes i idx spec label tool n
   172	        local -a rows=()
   173	        for (( i = 0; i < n_arms; i++ )); do
   174	            idx=$(( (i + slot - 1) % n_arms ))          # rotate the start arm
   175	            spec="${arms[$idx]}"
   176	            label="${spec%%:*}"; tool="$(echo "$spec" | cut -d: -f2)"; n="${spec##*:}"
   177	            ARM_TOOL="$tool"; ARM_N="$n"
   178	            one_run "$w" "$tool" "${SESSION}_${w}_${label}_s${slot}a${attempts}"
   179	            [[ "$RUN_VALID" == yes ]] || slot_valid=no
   180	            rows+=("$w,$label,$slot,$attempts,$RUN_MS,$RUN_FLUSH,$RUN_EXIT,$RUN_FILES,$RUN_DRAIN")
   181	            log "  $w/$label slot $slot: ${RUN_MS}ms (flush ${RUN_FLUSH}ms, exit $RUN_EXIT, $RUN_FILES files, $RUN_DRAIN)"
   182	        done
   183	        local r
   184	        for r in "${rows[@]}"; do echo "$r,$slot_valid" >> "$CSV"; done
   185	        if [[ "$slot_valid" == yes ]]; then
   186	            valid=$(( valid + 1 )); slot=$(( slot + 1 ))
   187	        else
   188	            log "  $w: slot $slot VOIDED — re-running the whole slot"
   189	        fi
   190	    done
   191	    (( valid >= RUNS )) || log "  $w INCOMPLETE: $valid/$RUNS valid slots after $attempts attempts"
   192	}
   193	
   194	run_cell() {   # fixture — ABBA over blit/robocopy, pair-void, 2xRUNS cap
   195	    local w="$1" slot=1 attempts=0 valid=0 max=$(( 2 * RUNS ))
   196	    log "=== $w (blit vs robocopy, ABBA, $RUNS pairs) ==="
   197	    # WARM-UP, DISCARDED (found live 2026-07-13): the cell's FIRST timed run
   198	    # was systematically slow (small: 2256ms vs 1396-1402ms; mixed: 1339ms vs
   199	    # 935-946ms) while `large` — whose predecessor cell leaves almost no
   200	    # cleanup behind — showed none. It is the PREVIOUS cell's teardown (a
   201	    # 10k-file Remove-Item) still settling past the drain, not a property of
   202	    # the tool. ABBA fixes `blit` as slot 1's first arm, so that bill landed on
   203	    # blit in EVERY cell — a systematic bias against whichever tool goes first.
   204	    # One untimed run per cell absorbs it; its result is thrown away.
   205	    one_run "$w" blit "${SESSION}_${w}_warmup"
   206	    log "  $w/warmup (untimed, discarded): ${RUN_MS}ms ($RUN_DRAIN)"
   207	    while (( valid < RUNS && attempts < max )); do
   208	        attempts=$(( attempts + 1 ))
   209	        local order pair_valid=yes tool rowA="" rowB=""
   210	        if (( slot % 2 )); then order="blit robocopy"; else order="robocopy blit"; fi
   211	        for tool in $order; do
   212	            one_run "$w" "$tool" "${SESSION}_${w}_${tool}_s${slot}a${attempts}"
   213	            [[ "$RUN_VALID" == yes ]] || pair_valid=no
   214	            local row="$w,$tool,$slot,$attempts,$RUN_MS,$RUN_FLUSH,$RUN_EXIT,$RUN_FILES,$RUN_DRAIN"
   215	            if [[ "$tool" == blit ]]; then rowA="$row"; else rowB="$row"; fi
   216	            log "  $w/$tool slot $slot (attempt $attempts): ${RUN_MS}ms (flush ${RUN_FLUSH}ms, exit $RUN_EXIT, $RUN_FILES files, $RUN_DRAIN)"
   217	        done
   218	        echo "$rowA,$pair_valid" >> "$CSV"
   219	        echo "$rowB,$pair_valid" >> "$CSV"
   220	        if [[ "$pair_valid" == yes ]]; then
   221	            valid=$(( valid + 1 )); slot=$(( slot + 1 ))
   222	        else
   223	            log "  $w: pair at slot $slot VOIDED — re-running the slot"
   224	        fi
   225	    done
   226	    (( valid >= RUNS )) || log "  $w INCOMPLETE: $valid/$RUNS valid pairs after $attempts attempts"
   227	}
   228	
   229	summarize() {
   230	    python3 - "$CSV" "$OUT_DIR/summary.csv" <<'PYEOF'
   231	import csv, sys, statistics as st
   232	runs_p, summary_p = sys.argv[1:3]
   233	rows = [r for r in csv.DictReader(open(runs_p)) if r["valid"] == "yes"]
   234	by = {}
   235	for r in rows:
   236	    by.setdefault((r["fixture"], r["tool"]), []).append(int(r["ms"]))
   237	
   238	with open(summary_p, "w") as f:
   239	    f.write("fixture,arm,median_ms,best_ms,spread_pct,n\n")
   240	    for k in sorted(by):
   241	        v = sorted(by[k])
   242	        spread = round(100.0 * (max(v) - min(v)) / max(min(v), 1), 1)
   243	        f.write(f"{k[0]},{k[1]},{int(st.median(v))},{min(v)},{spread},{len(v)}\n")
   244	
   245	def med(w, arm):
   246	    v = by.get((w, arm))
   247	    return st.median(v) if v else None
   248	
   249	fixtures = [w for w in ("large", "small", "mixed") if any(k[0] == w for k in by)]
   250	arms = sorted({k[1] for k in by})
   251	
   252	print(f"\n{'fixture':8} " + " ".join(f"{a:>10}" for a in arms))
   253	print("-" * (9 + 11 * len(arms)))
   254	for w in fixtures:
   255	    cells = []
   256	    for a in arms:
   257	        m = med(w, a)
   258	        cells.append(f"{m:9.0f}ms" if m else f"{'-':>11}")
   259	    print(f"{w:8} " + " ".join(cells))
   260	
   261	# The comparisons that matter, each INSIDE one session (no cross-session math).
   262	print("\n=== EQUAL-CONCURRENCY comparisons (the only fair cross-tool ones) ===")
   263	for w in fixtures:
   264	    for bl, ro, tag in (("blit_ship", "robo_mt1", "1 thread "),
   265	                        ("blit_w8", "robo_mt8", "8 threads")):
   266	        b, r = med(w, bl), med(w, ro)
   267	        if b and r:
   268	            print(f"  {w:6} @{tag}: blit {b:6.0f}ms  robocopy {r:6.0f}ms  "
   269	                  f"ratio {b/r:5.3f}  {'blit WINS' if b < r else 'blit LOSES'}")
   270	
   271	print("\n=== SCALING: what does 8x the workers/threads actually buy each tool? ===")
   272	for w in fixtures:
   273	    b1, b8 = med(w, "blit_ship"), med(w, "blit_w8")
   274	    r1, r8 = med(w, "robo_mt1"), med(w, "robo_mt8")
   275	    if b1 and b8:
   276	        print(f"  {w:6} blit     1 -> 8 workers: {b1:6.0f} -> {b8:6.0f}ms  = {b1/b8:4.2f}x speedup")
   277	    if r1 and r8:
   278	        print(f"  {w:6} robocopy 1 -> 8 threads: {r1:6.0f} -> {r8:6.0f}ms  = {r1/r8:4.2f}x speedup")
   279	
   280	print("\nCross-tool wall clock, NOT a controlled protocol comparison.")
   281	print("This says NOTHING about P1 — there is no initiator axis in a local copy.")
   282	print("blit_ship = the SHIPPED default (one apply worker, no --workers flag).")
   283	PYEOF
   284	}
   285	
   286	main() {
   287	    preflight
   288	    [[ "$PREFLIGHT_ONLY" == 1 ]] && { log "PREFLIGHT_ONLY: nothing timed"; exit 0; }
   289	    stage_runner
   290	    local w
   291	    for w in ${FIXTURES//,/ }; do
   292	        if [[ "${MULTI:-0}" == 1 ]]; then run_cell_multi "$w"; else run_cell "$w"; fi
   293	    done
   294	    wssh "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force '$DEST_BASE' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" || true
   295	    summarize | tee -a "$OUT_DIR/bench.log"
   296	    log "runs: $CSV"
   297	}
   298	
   299	main "$@"

.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:30:5. The recorded cold-allocation outlier: blit's warm-up (2253ms) AND first timed run (2175ms) in the `small` cell are slow, then it settles at 1386-1430; robocopy shows the same far more weakly (742 -> ~690). The README claims blit is "disproportionately sensitive to cold NTFS destination-allocation state". Is that supported, or is there a harness explanation?
.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:133:- **High — The README under-reports major execution asymmetries.** Default blit uses size+mtime, with BLAKE3 only when `--checksum` is selected ([local.rs:175](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/local.rs:175), [mod.rs:1331](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:1331)). Nevertheless, every file is statted/opened for readability, cloned into a mutex-protected manifest map and sent over an in-process channel, canonical-containment-checked/statted at the destination, then reopened for availability ([source.rs:137](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/source.rs:137), [mod.rs:1349](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:1349), [mod.rs:3541](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:3541), [source.rs:236](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/source.rs:236)). This is substantial bookkeeping/safety work, but not post-copy verification.

.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:22:  Session 2 (18:38, RUNS=8): blit small = 1388ms median (runs: 2175, 1386, 1386, 1402, 1390, 1387, 1430, 1383)

.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:25:Flat within each session; no drift; tight spreads. Meanwhile robocopy /MT:8 measured 697ms in BOTH session 2 and a later session B — rock steady. So the rig is STABLE for robocopy and BI-STABLE for blit.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:28:  - Sessions 1 & 2 interleaved blit against `robocopy /MT:8`  (an 8-THREAD neighbour). blit read ~1390ms.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:29:  - Session A interleaved blit against `robocopy /MT:1`       (a 1-THREAD neighbour). blit read ~2225ms.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:30:  - Session B: blit --workers 8 (1331ms) interleaved with robocopy /MT:8 (697ms).
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:31:  - Also: sessions 1 & 2 ran fixtures in the order large,small,mixed (so `small` ran SECOND, after a 1 GiB copy). Session A ran small,mixed (so `small` ran FIRST). Note in sessions 1 & 2 the FIRST timed small run was ~2175-2256ms — i.e. matching session A's plateau — and then it dropped to ~1390 and stayed there.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:33:MY HYPOTHESIS (test it, do not accept it): Windows CPU frequency boost / core parking residency. An 8-thread neighbour (robocopy /MT:8, or blit --workers 8) unparks cores and raises boost clocks; blit's next run — single-worker, latency-bound, syscall-heavy — inherits a hot CPU. robocopy /MT:8 generates its own load so it is immune. If true, blit's HONEST single-worker cost on an idle machine is ~2225ms and the ~1390ms figure is an artifact of what it was benchmarked NEXT TO.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:37:2. Enumerate COMPETING explanations for a 60% bi-stability that affects a single-threaded workload but not an 8-threaded one on the same machine, same disks, same files. Consider at least: NTFS free-space/MFT fragmentation accumulating across sessions; the T705 NVMe SLC write cache and thermal throttling; Windows standby-purge behaviour; Defender / real-time AV scanning (does it scan more when files arrive slowly?); the file-count-verify and Remove-Item teardown of 10k files not fully settling past the drain; and the fixture ORDER difference (small ran first vs second). For each: how would I tell it apart from the CPU-boost story, with a concrete measurement?

.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1114:.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:25:Flat within each session; no drift; tight spreads. Meanwhile robocopy /MT:8 measured 697ms in BOTH session 2 and a later session B — rock steady. So the rig is STABLE for robocopy and BI-STABLE for blit.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1115:.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:28:  - Sessions 1 & 2 interleaved blit against `robocopy /MT:8`  (an 8-THREAD neighbour). blit read ~1390ms.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1116:.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:29:  - Session A interleaved blit against `robocopy /MT:1`       (a 1-THREAD neighbour). blit read ~2225ms.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1117:.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:30:  - Session B: blit --workers 8 (1331ms) interleaved with robocopy /MT:8 (697ms).
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1118:.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:33:MY HYPOTHESIS (test it, do not accept it): Windows CPU frequency boost / core parking residency. An 8-thread neighbour (robocopy /MT:8, or blit --workers 8) unparks cores and raises boost clocks; blit's next run — single-worker, latency-bound, syscall-heavy — inherits a hot CPU. robocopy /MT:8 generates its own load so it is immune. If true, blit's HONEST single-worker cost on an idle machine is ~2225ms and the ~1390ms figure is an artifact of what it was benchmarked NEXT TO.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1119:.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:37:2. Enumerate COMPETING explanations for a 60% bi-stability that affects a single-threaded workload but not an 8-threaded one on the same machine, same disks, same files. Consider at least: NTFS free-space/MFT fragmentation accumulating across sessions; the T705 NVMe SLC write cache and thermal throttling; Windows standby-purge behaviour; Defender / real-time AV scanning (does it scan more when files arrive slowly?); the file-count-verify and Remove-Item teardown of 10k files not fully settling past the drain; and the fixture ORDER difference (small ran first vs second). For each: how would I tell it apart from the CPU-boost story, with a concrete measurement?
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1136:.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:19:  small  10,000 x 4 KiB      blit 1388ms  robocopy  697ms  ratio 1.991
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1141:.review/results/win-local-ab.codex.md:30:5. The recorded cold-allocation outlier: blit's warm-up (2253ms) AND first timed run (2175ms) in the `small` cell are slow, then it settles at 1386-1430; robocopy shows the same far more weakly (742 -> ~690). The README claims blit is "disproportionately sensitive to cold NTFS destination-allocation state". Is that supported, or is there a harness explanation?
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1245:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1388:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:447:     3	Last updated: 2026-07-05 (**the 10 GbE benchmark session ran** —

.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1779:  Session 1 (18:32, RUNS=4): blit small = 1400ms median (runs: 2256, 1402, 1396, 1399)
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1780:  Session 2 (18:38, RUNS=8): blit small = 1388ms median (runs: 2175, 1386, 1386, 1402, 1390, 1387, 1430, 1383)
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1781:  Session A (18:58, RUNS=6): blit small = 2225ms median (runs: 2222, 2176, 2181, 2245, 2228, 2258)  <-- 60% SLOWER
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1783:Flat within each session; no drift; tight spreads. Meanwhile robocopy /MT:8 measured 697ms in BOTH session 2 and a later session B — rock steady. So the rig is STABLE for robocopy and BI-STABLE for blit.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1786:  - Sessions 1 & 2 interleaved blit against `robocopy /MT:8`  (an 8-THREAD neighbour). blit read ~1390ms.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1787:  - Session A interleaved blit against `robocopy /MT:1`       (a 1-THREAD neighbour). blit read ~2225ms.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1788:  - Session B: blit --workers 8 (1331ms) interleaved with robocopy /MT:8 (697ms).
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1789:  - Also: sessions 1 & 2 ran fixtures in the order large,small,mixed (so `small` ran SECOND, after a 1 GiB copy). Session A ran small,mixed (so `small` ran FIRST). Note in sessions 1 & 2 the FIRST timed small run was ~2175-2256ms — i.e. matching session A's plateau — and then it dropped to ~1390 and stayed there.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1791:MY HYPOTHESIS (test it, do not accept it): Windows CPU frequency boost / core parking residency. An 8-thread neighbour (robocopy /MT:8, or blit --workers 8) unparks cores and raises boost clocks; blit's next run — single-worker, latency-bound, syscall-heavy — inherits a hot CPU. robocopy /MT:8 generates its own load so it is immune. If true, blit's HONEST single-worker cost on an idle machine is ~2225ms and the ~1390ms figure is an artifact of what it was benchmarked NEXT TO.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1795:2. Enumerate COMPETING explanations for a 60% bi-stability that affects a single-threaded workload but not an 8-threaded one on the same machine, same disks, same files. Consider at least: NTFS free-space/MFT fragmentation accumulating across sessions; the T705 NVMe SLC write cache and thermal throttling; Windows standby-purge behaviour; Defender / real-time AV scanning (does it scan more when files arrive slowly?); the file-count-verify and Remove-Item teardown of 10k files not fully settling past the drain; and the fixture ORDER difference (small ran first vs second). For each: how would I tell it apart from the CPU-boost story, with a concrete measurement?
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:2099:  small  10,000 x 4 KiB      blit 1388ms  robocopy  697ms  ratio 1.991
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:2110:5. The recorded cold-allocation outlier: blit's warm-up (2253ms) AND first timed run (2175ms) in the `small` cell are slow, then it settles at 1386-1430; robocopy shows the same far more weakly (742 -> ~690). The README claims blit is "disproportionately sensitive to cold NTFS destination-allocation state". Is that supported, or is there a harness explanation?
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:2213:- **High — The README under-reports major execution asymmetries.** Default blit uses size+mtime, with BLAKE3 only when `--checksum` is selected ([local.rs:175](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/local.rs:175), [mod.rs:1331](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:1331)). Nevertheless, every file is statted/opened for readability, cloned into a mutex-protected manifest map and sent over an in-process channel, canonical-containment-checked/statted at the destination, then reopened for availability ([source.rs:137](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/source.rs:137), [mod.rs:1349](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:1349), [mod.rs:3541](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:3541), [source.rs:236](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/remote/transfer/source.rs:236)). This is substantial bookkeeping/safety work, but not post-copy verification.
.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:2236:- **High — The README under-reports major execution asymmetries.** Default blit uses size+mtime, with BLAKE3 only when `--checksum` is selected ([local.rs:175](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-cli/src/transfers/local.rs:175), [mod.rs:1331](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:1331)).  succeeded in 0ms:

     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	# =============================================================================
     3	# bench_win_local_ab.sh — blit vs robocopy, LOCAL D: -> E: on netwatch-01.
     4	#
     5	# WHY (owner, 2026-07-13): a local-only A/B on the Windows box. It strips the
     6	# network out entirely — no MTU, no MSS, no initiator layout, no daemon, no
     7	# carrier. If blit trails robocopy HERE, the problem was never the wire.
     8	#
     9	# It is also the cleanest hardware we own for the question: D: and E: are two
    10	# SEPARATE, IDENTICAL Crucial T705 4TB NVMe drives (disk#0 and disk#3), so a
    11	# D:->E: copy has no read/write contention on one device and neither side of
    12	# the copy is a bottleneck the other lacks.
    13	#
    14	# WHAT IT CAN AND CANNOT ANSWER (do not conflate — the bench_baseline_tools.sh
    15	# distinction applies here too):
    16	#   * It CANNOT test the initiator axis. Both tools run on Windows against
    17	#     local paths; there is no initiator to vary. It says NOTHING about P1.
    18	#   * It CAN test the SHIPPING BAR locally ("does blit beat robocopy on the
    19	#     same box, same files, same disks") and it CAN expose a blit-side local
    20	#     regression that the network harnesses would confound with the wire.
    21	#   * The comparison is cross-tool, so it is NOT a controlled protocol
    22	#     comparison — robocopy is a plain Win32 copy loop, blit rides the unified
    23	#     transfer_session (local included, since otp-11). Report it as such.
    24	#
    25	# METHODOLOGY: identical to the blit rig harnesses (anything less is not
    26	# comparable) — cold caches every run, writeback drained BEFORE the window,
    27	# fresh never-seen destination per run, destination container precreated
    28	# outside the window on both arms, durability keyed by the DESTINATION volume,
    29	# ABBA interleave, pair-void with a 2xRUNS cap, nonzero exit voids the run.
    30	# The per-run body runs entirely on Windows (scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1)
    31	# so the ssh round trip is outside the timed window.
    32	#
    33	# Build note: bins\f35702a\blit.exe is the SHIPPING transfer code — the only
    34	# delta f35702a..HEAD is bb28ddd (cargo fmt on blit-app/endpoints.rs + a test),
    35	# and otp-11 (local rides the unified session) is already IN f35702a.
    36	#
    37	# Usage:
    38	#   bash scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh
    39	#   RUNS=8 FIXTURES=mixed bash scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh
    40	#   PREFLIGHT_ONLY=1 bash scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh
    41	# =============================================================================
    42	set -euo pipefail
    43	
    44	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    45	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
    46	
    47	WIN_SSH="${WIN_SSH:-michael@netwatch-01}"
    48	RUNS="${RUNS:-4}"
    49	FIXTURES="${FIXTURES:-large,small,mixed}"
    50	BLIT_EXE="${BLIT_EXE:-D:\\blit-test\\bins\\f35702a\\blit.exe}"
    51	BLIT_SHA="${BLIT_SHA:-f35702a}"
    52	WIN_TEST="${WIN_TEST:-D:\\blit-test}"
    53	SRC_ROOT="${SRC_ROOT:-$WIN_TEST\\bench-module}"
    54	DEST_BASE="${DEST_BASE:-E:\\blit-local-bench}"
    55	DEST_DRIVE="${DEST_DRIVE:-E}"
    56	PREFLIGHT_ONLY="${PREFLIGHT_ONLY:-0}"
    57	
    58	SESSION="$(date +%Y%m%dT%H%M%S)"
    59	OUT_DIR="${OUT_DIR:-$REPO_ROOT/logs/win_local_ab_$SESSION}"
    60	mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
    61	CSV="$OUT_DIR/runs.csv"
    62	echo "fixture,tool,slot,attempt,ms,flush_ms,exit,files,drain,valid" > "$CSV"
    63	
    64	SSH_MUX=(-o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=10 -o ControlMaster=auto
    65	         -o "ControlPath=$HOME/.ssh/cm-%r@%h-%p" -o ControlPersist=300)
    66	wssh() { ssh "${SSH_MUX[@]}" "$WIN_SSH" "$@"; }
    67	log() { echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) $*" | tee -a "$OUT_DIR/bench.log"; }
    68	die() { log "FATAL: $*"; exit 1; }
    69	
    70	# Fixture shapes — verified, never assumed (the otp-12a F2 rule).
    71	FIX_FILES_large=1
    72	FIX_FILES_small=10000
    73	FIX_FILES_mixed=5001
    74	
    75	preflight() {
    76	    [[ "$RUNS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$RUNS" -ge 2 ]] || die "RUNS must be >= 2 (got '$RUNS')"
    77	    wssh "exit 0" || die "cannot ssh $WIN_SSH"
    78	    wssh "if (-not (Test-Path '$BLIT_EXE')) { exit 1 }" \
    79	        || die "blit not staged at $BLIT_EXE"
    80	    wssh "if (Select-String -Path '$BLIT_EXE' -SimpleMatch -Quiet -Pattern '+$BLIT_SHA') { exit 0 } else { exit 1 }" \
    81	        || die "$BLIT_EXE does not embed +$BLIT_SHA — restage the build"
    82	    wssh "if (-not (Test-Path '$WIN_TEST\\purge-standby.ps1')) { exit 1 }" \
    83	        || die "purge-standby.ps1 not staged at $WIN_TEST"
    84	    wssh "if ((Get-Volume -DriveLetter $DEST_DRIVE).SizeRemaining -lt 20GB) { exit 1 }" \
    85	        || die "less than 20 GB free on ${DEST_DRIVE}: — refusing to run"
    86	    local w want got
    87	    for w in ${FIXTURES//,/ }; do
    88	        want="$(eval echo "\$FIX_FILES_$w")"
    89	        [[ -n "$want" ]] || die "unknown fixture '$w' (want: large|small|mixed)"
    90	        got="$(wssh "(Get-ChildItem '$SRC_ROOT\\pull_src_$w\\src_$w' -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object).Count" | tr -cd '0-9')"
    91	        [[ "$got" == "$want" ]] \
    92	            || die "fixture src_$w has $got files, want $want — restage it before measuring"
    93	        log "  fixture src_$w verified ($got files)"
    94	    done
    95	    # Stale destination from an interrupted run would be re-used as a warm cache.
    96	    wssh "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force '$DEST_BASE' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" || true
    97	    log "preflight OK  blit=$BLIT_SHA  runs/arm=$RUNS  fixtures=$FIXTURES  D: -> ${DEST_DRIVE}:"
    98	}
    99	
   100	stage_runner() {
   101	    wssh "New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path '$WIN_TEST' | Out-Null"
   102	    scp -q -o BatchMode=yes "$SCRIPT_DIR/windows/local-ab-run.ps1" \
   103	        "$WIN_SSH:$WIN_TEST/local-ab-run.ps1" || die "failed to stage local-ab-run.ps1"
   104	    log "runner staged at $WIN_TEST\\local-ab-run.ps1"
   105	}
   106	
   107	# One timed run. Sets RUN_MS/RUN_FLUSH/RUN_EXIT/RUN_FILES/RUN_DRAIN/RUN_VALID.
   108	RUN_MS=0; RUN_FLUSH=0; RUN_EXIT=0; RUN_FILES=0; RUN_DRAIN=""; RUN_VALID=yes
   109	one_run() {   # fixture tool tag
   110	    local w="$1" tool="$2" tag="$3" out
   111	    out="$(wssh "pwsh -NoProfile -File '$WIN_TEST\\local-ab-run.ps1' -Tool $tool -Src '$SRC_ROOT\\pull_src_$w\\src_$w' -DestRoot '$DEST_BASE\\$tag' -BlitExe '$BLIT_EXE' -DestDrive $DEST_DRIVE" 2>>"$OUT_DIR/err.log" \
   112	        | tr -d '\r' | sed -n 's/.*R:\([0-9-]*,[0-9-]*,[0-9-]*,[0-9]*,[A-Za-z0-9_-]*\):R.*/\1/p' | head -1)"
   113	    if [[ -z "$out" ]]; then
   114	        RUN_MS=0; RUN_FLUSH=0; RUN_EXIT=99; RUN_FILES=0; RUN_DRAIN="PARSE-FAIL"; RUN_VALID=no
   115	        return 0
   116	    fi
   117	    IFS=, read -r RUN_MS RUN_FLUSH RUN_EXIT RUN_FILES RUN_DRAIN <<< "$out"
   118	    RUN_VALID=yes
   119	    # robocopy: exit 0-7 is SUCCESS (8+ is failure). Every other tool: 0 only.
   120	    if [[ "$2" == robocopy ]]; then
   121	        [[ "$RUN_EXIT" -lt 8 ]] || RUN_VALID=no
   122	    else
   123	        [[ "$RUN_EXIT" == 0 ]] || RUN_VALID=no
   124	    fi
   125	    [[ "$RUN_FLUSH" -ge 0 ]] || { RUN_VALID=no; RUN_FLUSH=0; }   # failed flush voids
   126	    [[ "$RUN_DRAIN" == drained* ]] || RUN_VALID=no                # undrained voids
   127	    local want; want="$(eval echo "\$FIX_FILES_$1")"
   128	    [[ "$RUN_FILES" == "$want" ]] || RUN_VALID=no                 # wrong tree voids
   129	    RUN_MS=$(( RUN_MS + RUN_FLUSH ))
   130	}
   131	
   132	run_cell() {   # fixture — ABBA over blit/robocopy, pair-void, 2xRUNS cap
   133	    local w="$1" slot=1 attempts=0 valid=0 max=$(( 2 * RUNS ))
   134	    log "=== $w (blit vs robocopy, ABBA, $RUNS pairs) ==="
   135	    # WARM-UP, DISCARDED (found live 2026-07-13): the cell's FIRST timed run
   136	    # was systematically slow (small: 2256ms vs 1396-1402ms; mixed: 1339ms vs
   137	    # 935-946ms) while `large` — whose predecessor cell leaves almost no
   138	    # cleanup behind — showed none. It is the PREVIOUS cell's teardown (a
   139	    # 10k-file Remove-Item) still settling past the drain, not a property of
   140	    # the tool. ABBA fixes `blit` as slot 1's first arm, so that bill landed on
   141	    # blit in EVERY cell — a systematic bias against whichever tool goes first.
   142	    # One untimed run per cell absorbs it; its result is thrown away.
   143	    one_run "$w" blit "${SESSION}_${w}_warmup"
   144	    log "  $w/warmup (untimed, discarded): ${RUN_MS}ms ($RUN_DRAIN)"
   145	    while (( valid < RUNS && attempts < max )); do
   146	        attempts=$(( attempts + 1 ))
   147	        local order pair_valid=yes tool rowA="" rowB=""
   148	        if (( slot % 2 )); then order="blit robocopy"; else order="robocopy blit"; fi
   149	        for tool in $order; do
   150	            one_run "$w" "$tool" "${SESSION}_${w}_${tool}_s${slot}a${attempts}"
   151	            [[ "$RUN_VALID" == yes ]] || pair_valid=no
   152	            local row="$w,$tool,$slot,$attempts,$RUN_MS,$RUN_FLUSH,$RUN_EXIT,$RUN_FILES,$RUN_DRAIN"
   153	            if [[ "$tool" == blit ]]; then rowA="$row"; else rowB="$row"; fi
   154	            log "  $w/$tool slot $slot (attempt $attempts): ${RUN_MS}ms (flush ${RUN_FLUSH}ms, exit $RUN_EXIT, $RUN_FILES files, $RUN_DRAIN)"
   155	        done
   156	        echo "$rowA,$pair_valid" >> "$CSV"
   157	        echo "$rowB,$pair_valid" >> "$CSV"
   158	        if [[ "$pair_valid" == yes ]]; then
   159	            valid=$(( valid + 1 )); slot=$(( slot + 1 ))
   160	        else
   161	            log "  $w: pair at slot $slot VOIDED — re-running the slot"
   162	        fi
   163	    done
   164	    (( valid >= RUNS )) || log "  $w INCOMPLETE: $valid/$RUNS valid pairs after $attempts attempts"
   165	}
   166	
   167	summarize() {
   168	    python3 - "$CSV" "$OUT_DIR/summary.csv" <<'PYEOF'
   169	import csv, sys, statistics as st
   170	runs_p, summary_p = sys.argv[1:3]
   171	rows = [r for r in csv.DictReader(open(runs_p)) if r["valid"] == "yes"]
   172	by = {}
   173	for r in rows:
   174	    by.setdefault((r["fixture"], r["tool"]), []).append(int(r["ms"]))
   175	
   176	with open(summary_p, "w") as f:
   177	    f.write("fixture,tool,median_ms,best_ms,spread_pct,n\n")
   178	    for k in sorted(by):
   179	        v = sorted(by[k])
   180	        spread = round(100.0 * (max(v) - min(v)) / max(min(v), 1), 1)
   181	        f.write(f"{k[0]},{k[1]},{int(st.median(v))},{min(v)},{spread},{len(v)}\n")
   182	
   183	print(f"\n{'fixture':8} {'blit':>9} {'robocopy':>9} {'ratio':>7}  verdict")
   184	print("-" * 48)
   185	for w in ("large", "small", "mixed"):
   186	    b, r = by.get((w, "blit")), by.get((w, "robocopy"))
   187	    if not b or not r:
   188	        continue
   189	    bm, rm = st.median(b), st.median(r)
   190	    ratio = bm / rm
   191	    verdict = "blit FASTER" if ratio < 1.0 else "blit SLOWER"
   192	    print(f"{w:8} {bm:8.0f}ms {rm:8.0f}ms {ratio:7.3f}  {verdict}")
   193	print("\nratio = blit / robocopy  (<1.00 means blit wins)")
   194	print("Cross-tool wall clock, NOT a controlled protocol comparison.")
   195	print("This says NOTHING about P1 — there is no initiator axis in a local copy.")
   196	PYEOF
   197	}
   198	
   199	main() {
   200	    preflight
   201	    [[ "$PREFLIGHT_ONLY" == 1 ]] && { log "PREFLIGHT_ONLY: nothing timed"; exit 0; }
   202	    stage_runner
   203	    local w
   204	    for w in ${FIXTURES//,/ }; do run_cell "$w"; done
   205	    wssh "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force '$DEST_BASE' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" || true
   206	    summarize | tee -a "$OUT_DIR/bench.log"
   207	    log "runs: $CSV"
   208	}
   209	
   210	main "$@"

git: error: couldn't create cache file '/tmp/xcrun_db-STN7uZyo' (errno=Operation not permitted)
git: warning: confstr() failed with code 5: couldn't get path of DARWIN_USER_TEMP_DIR; using /tmp instead
git: error: couldn't create cache file '/tmp/xcrun_db-tdUKVvxq' (errno=Operation not permitted)
     1	# local-ab-run.ps1 — ONE timed local D:->E: run on netwatch-01 (blit vs robocopy).
     2	#
     3	# Runs entirely on Windows so the ssh round trip stays OUTSIDE the timed window
     4	# (the otp-2w F3 rule). Methodology matches the blit rig harnesses exactly —
     5	# anything less is not comparable to the otp-12 numbers:
     6	#   * cold caches before every run (standby-list purge);
     7	#   * writeback DRAINED before the window opens (a dirty queue from the previous
     8	#     run would otherwise be charged to this one);
     9	#   * destination container precreated OUTSIDE the window on BOTH arms, so
    10	#     neither tool pays a mkdir the other does not (otp-12b F5);
    11	#   * durability keyed by the DESTINATION volume, never by the tool — the bug
    12	#     that invalidated the first Linux session (docs/bench/otp12-perf README);
    13	#   * the landed file count is returned so a tool that "succeeds" while writing
    14	#     nothing is caught instead of scoring a fast time.
    15	#
    16	# Prints exactly one sentinel-framed record:  R:<ms>,<flush_ms>,<rc>,<files>,<drain>:R
    17	# Anything else (a crash, pwsh noise) fails to parse and VOIDS the run — nothing
    18	# can masquerade as a time.
    19	
    20	param(
    21	    [Parameter(Mandatory)][ValidateSet('blit', 'robocopy')][string]$Tool,
    22	    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Src,        # D:\...\src_mixed   (NO trailing slash)
    23	    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$DestRoot,   # E:\blit-local-bench\<tag>
    24	    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$BlitExe,
    25	    [string]$DestDrive = 'E',
    26	    [string]$PurgeScript = 'D:\blit-test\purge-standby.ps1'
    27	)
    28	
    29	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
    30	
    31	# --- cold caches (untimed) --------------------------------------------------
    32	& $PurgeScript | Out-Null
    33	
    34	# --- drain writeback (untimed): quiet = <1 MB/s for 3 consecutive 2s samples -
    35	Write-VolumeCache -DriveLetter $DestDrive
    36	$drain = 'DRAIN-TIMEOUT'
    37	$quiet = 0
    38	for ($i = 0; $i -lt 60; $i++) {
    39	    $w = (Get-Counter "\PhysicalDisk(_Total)\Disk Write Bytes/sec" -SampleInterval 2 -MaxSamples 1).CounterSamples[0].CookedValue
    40	    if ($null -ne $w -and [double]$w -lt 1048576) { $quiet++ } else { $quiet = 0 }
    41	    if ($quiet -ge 3) { $drain = "drained_$((($i + 1) * 2))s"; break }
    42	}
    43	
    44	# --- destination container, precreated OUTSIDE the window (both arms) --------
    45	# blit nests a no-trailing-slash source under the destination, so `blit copy
    46	# <Src> <DestRoot>` lands <DestRoot>\<leaf>. robocopy copies CONTENTS, so it is
    47	# pointed at <DestRoot>\<leaf> directly. Both arms therefore land an identical
    48	# tree and neither pays for the container.
    49	$leaf = Split-Path $Src -Leaf
    50	$dest = Join-Path $DestRoot $leaf
    51	New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dest | Out-Null
    52	
    53	# --- the timed window -------------------------------------------------------
    54	$sw = [Diagnostics.Stopwatch]::StartNew()
    55	if ($Tool -eq 'blit') {
    56	    & $BlitExe copy $Src $DestRoot --yes > $null 2>&1
    57	    $rc = $LASTEXITCODE
    58	}
    59	else {
    60	    # /MT:8 is robocopy's own default thread count. Retries bounded so a
    61	    # transient error cannot hang the run for hours on the default /R:1000000.
    62	    robocopy $Src $dest /E /MT:8 /R:2 /W:2 /NFL /NDL /NJH /NJS > $null 2>&1
    63	    $rc = $LASTEXITCODE
    64	}
    65	$sw.Stop()
    66	$ms = [int]$sw.Elapsed.TotalMilliseconds
    67	
    68	# --- durability, self-timed, keyed by the DESTINATION volume ----------------
    69	$flush = -1
    70	try {
    71	    $a = [DateTimeOffset]::UtcNow.ToUnixTimeMilliseconds()
    72	    Write-VolumeCache -DriveLetter $DestDrive -ErrorAction Stop
    73	    $b = [DateTimeOffset]::UtcNow.ToUnixTimeMilliseconds()
    74	    $flush = [int]($b - $a)
    75	}
    76	catch { $flush = -1 }   # caller VOIDS the run: a failed flush is not a fast run
    77	
    78	# --- did the bytes actually land? -------------------------------------------
    79	$files = (Get-ChildItem $dest -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object).Count
    80	
    81	Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $DestRoot -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    82	
    83	# ${drain} braces are LOAD-BEARING: PowerShell parses a bare `$drain:R` as a
    84	# SCOPE-qualified variable (like $env:PATH), so the closing sentinel silently
    85	# vanishes and every run parses as a void. Same trap as bench_otp12_win.sh:520
    86	# — documented there, and reproduced here anyway on the first run.
    87	"R:$ms,$flush,$rc,$files,${drain}:R"

exec

commit e6845ca407ecced78b356b7923f82f388b3a431e
Author:     Michael Coelho <mcoelho@gmail.com>
AuthorDate: Mon Jul 13 19:22:21 2026 -0400
Commit:     Michael Coelho <mcoelho@gmail.com>
CommitDate: Mon Jul 13 19:22:21 2026 -0400

    local small-file finding: plan (Draft) + D-2026-07-13-2 (queued BEHIND otp-12)
    
    The headline was wrong and the owner caught it: "blit is 2x robocopy" was
    8-THREAD robocopy vs 1-WORKER blit. blit's local apply ships one worker
    (transfer_session/local.rs:602 — --workers is a hidden debug flag). At EQUAL
    concurrency the result inverts:
    
      small @ 1 thread : blit 2225ms  robocopy 2531ms  0.88  blit WINS
      mixed @ 1 thread : blit  942ms  robocopy 1045ms  0.90  blit WINS
      small @ 8 threads: blit 1331ms  robocopy  697ms  1.91  blit loses
      mixed @ 8 threads: blit  790ms  robocopy  502ms  1.57  blit loses
    
    So blit's per-file path is not slow — it WINS at one thread. blit does not
    SCALE: 8x concurrency buys blit 1.67x (small) where robocopy gets 3.63x. L3
    (single worker) becomes the prime suspect; L1 (tar framing) is demoted, since
    it cannot be the dominant cost of a tool that wins at one thread.
    
    D-2026-07-13-2: the fix queues BEHIND OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS (owner: "behind").
    The local cost cannot explain P1 (no initiator axis; a per-file/worker cost
    cancels between two arms of the same code) and is very unlikely to explain P2
    (P2 is NEW; this cost is OLD — otp-11's gate measured old-vs-new local small
    at 1684->1750ms, +3.9%). Fixing it first would touch code shared with the wire
    sink and void otp-12's pre-fix baselines. One cheap check is carried into
    pf-1: if the unified session also pinned the REMOTE receive worker count, that
    WOULD be new, per-file, and a live P2 candidate.
    
    NOT YET TRUSTED, and recorded as such: blit's absolute time is BI-STABLE
    across sessions — identical binary and flags read 1388ms and 2225ms for
    `small`, flat within each session, while robocopy /MT:8 read 697ms in both.
    Working hypothesis: CPU boost/core-parking residency (an 8-thread neighbour
    leaves the CPU hot; blit's single-worker syscall-bound run inherits it;
    robocopy /MT:8 generates its own load and is immune). If true, blit's honest
    single-worker cost is ~2225ms and 1388ms was an artifact of what it was
    benchmarked next to. A 4-arm single-session run is the control; no attribution
    until it lands. Harness gains an N-arm interleaved mode for exactly this.
    
    Also fixed — a false attribution in a durable record: GPT_REVIEW_LOOP.md still
    named `gpt-5.5` as the reviewer, so the pf-0-prereg verdict was signed with it.
    ~/.codex/config.toml is ground truth and says model = "gpt-5.6-sol",
    model_reasoning_effort = "ultra". Both corrected; the loop doc now says to READ
    the config rather than assume the model.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
    Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/session_01F6G7FJ7JkVPqXtQMEai62o

diff --git a/docs/plan/LOCAL_SMALL_FILE_PATH.md b/docs/plan/LOCAL_SMALL_FILE_PATH.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..961ff6f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/plan/LOCAL_SMALL_FILE_PATH.md
@@ -0,0 +1,213 @@
+# LOCAL_SMALL_FILE_PATH — the local carrier frames small files for a wire that isn't there
+
+**Status**: Draft
+**Created**: 2026-07-13
+**Owner decisions required**: see §Decisions (none assumed; no code until Active)
+**Parent**: none. This is **not** P1 and **not** P2
+(`docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md`) — see §Not-that-finding.
+**Contract**: `docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md`. **No wire change is in scope.** The
+tar shard is the *wire* payload format and must remain exactly as it is for
+remote sessions; this plan touches only the **local apply carrier**, which
+crosses no transport. Any slice that would alter a frame stops and amends the
+contract through the loop first.
+
+## The observation
+
+Local `D: -> E:` copy on netwatch-01, two separate identical NVMe drives, no
+network, no daemon, no initiator, no carrier negotiation
+(`docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/`, RUNS=8 medians):
+
+| fixture | blit | robocopy `/MT:8` | ratio |
+|---|---:|---:|---:|
+| `large` 1 × 1 GiB | 539 ms | 541 ms | **0.996 parity** |
+| `mixed` 512 MiB + 5000 files | 934 ms | 501 ms | 1.863 |
+| `small` 10 000 × 4 KiB | 1388 ms | 697 ms | 1.991 |
+
+**⚠ THAT COMPARISON IS NOT CONCURRENCY-FAIR** (owner, 2026-07-13): robocopy
+ran 8 threads; blit's local apply runs **one** worker by default
+(`transfer_session/local.rs:602` — `sink_workers` is 1 unless the hidden
+`--workers` flag sets `debug_mode`). It is also generous to robocopy relative
+to its own default (plain `robocopy /E` with no `/MT` is single-threaded).
+**The equal-concurrency sessions are the ones that attribute anything**, and
+this plan does not proceed past ls-1 without them.
+
+## The mechanism (code-read; execution on the local route confirmed)
+
+`blit copy` → `run_local_session` (`transfer_session/local.rs:511`) →
+`diff_chunk_and_apply_local` (`transfer_session/mod.rs:3362`). The byte path
+**forks on size** (`transfer_plan.rs:72`, `:106`):
+
+- **≥ 1 MiB** → `PreparedPayload::File` → `copy_resolved_file_payload`
+  (`sink.rs:473`) → `windows_copyfile` → **`CopyFileExW`**
+  (`copy/windows.rs:363`) / `clonefile` on APFS (`copy/file_copy/clone.rs:15`).
+  One syscall, kernel-side. **The same call robocopy makes** — hence exact
+  parity on `large`.
+- **< 1 MiB** → **tar shard**. Per file, on a copy where **no bytes cross any
+  transport**:
+  1. `build_tar_shard` (`payload.rs:254-293`) opens the file and streams it
+     into an **in-memory tar `Vec<u8>`** — 512 B header + data + 512 B padding
+     (a 4 KiB file becomes ~5 KiB of framing);
+  2. `safe_extract_tar_shard` (`tar_safety.rs:173-183`) **parses that tar and
+     memcpys every file's bytes into a second `Vec<u8>`**;
+  3. `create_dir_all(parent)` **per file** (`sink.rs:600`), even though all 128
+     files in a shard usually share one parent;
+  4. `fs::write` (`sink.rs:603`), then a **separate destination open** —
+     `set_file_mtime` (`sink.rs:606`) = `CreateFileW` + `SetFileTime` + close —
+     to stamp a timestamp `CopyFileEx` sets inline;
+  5. plus **two probe opens per source file before the real read**: a
+     readability probe in the scan (`remote/transfer/source.rs:142`) and an
+     availability probe at plan time (`source.rs:260`).
+
+So a 4 KiB file costs roughly **3 source opens + 2 destination opens + a
+`create_dir_all` + 2 full memcpys**, against robocopy's **1 `CopyFileEx`**. On
+NTFS `CreateFile` is the dominant per-file cost. Reads are additionally
+**serial**: one pipeline worker, and `prepare_payload` does not overlap
+`write_payload` (`pipeline.rs:196-206`).
+
+**Why the framing exists at all**: the tar shard is the *wire* format for
+small files — it amortizes per-file round trips across a network. otp-11's D1
+adopted it for the local carrier by **behavior preservation**, not by design:
+the plan says the local apply uses "`PreparedPayload::File`/`TarShard`
+**exactly as the old local pipeline**, same parallelism, same zero-copy
+primitives" (`docs/plan/OTP11_LOCAL_SESSION.md` D1). D1's zero-copy analysis
+is entirely about **large** files (protecting clonefile/block-clone). **Nobody
+ever asked whether a local copy should frame small files at all.**
+
+## Not-that-finding (what this is NOT)
+
+- **Not P1.** P1 is an initiator/verb *invariance* failure. A local copy has
+  no initiator axis — there is nothing to vary. This cannot speak to it.
+- **Not P2.** P2 is a *new-vs-old* regression on the TCP push path. This cost
+  is **not** new: otp-11's own local gate measured old-vs-new at `small`
+  1684 ms → 1750 ms, **+3.9% PASS** (`docs/bench/otp11-local-2026-07-11/`), and
+  D1 says the local carrier reproduces the old pipeline's payload shapes. The
+  old local path tar-sharded small files too.
+- **Not a regression at all** — it is a long-standing, unexamined inheritance.
+- **⚠ UNMEASURED**: the old blit *client* was never staged on Windows (only the
+  old daemon), so "old blit is ~2× robocopy **on Windows** too" is an
+  inference from the macOS otp-11 gate. `ls-1` closes that gap or states it.
+
+## Hypotheses (each names the mechanism it accuses)
+
+- **L1 — tar framing on a wireless path.** Encode + parse + 2 memcpys + ~1 KB
+  framing per 4 KiB file, buying nothing locally
+  (`payload.rs:254-293`; `tar_safety.rs:173-183`).
+- **L2 — per-file syscall count.** ~5 handle opens per file vs robocopy's ~1
+  (`source.rs:142`, `:260`; `sink.rs:600`, `:603`, `:606`).
+- **L3 — single apply worker.** `sink_workers = 1` by default
+  (`local.rs:602`); reads serial, no read/write overlap (`pipeline.rs:196-206`).
+  Robocopy was given 8 threads.
+- **L4 — `create_dir_all` per file** rather than once per shard
+  (`sink.rs:600`).
+
+L1/L2/L4 are per-file path costs; **L3 is a concurrency default**. They are
+independent and may all contribute. **L3 is measured first** because if it
+dominates, the others are a much smaller prize than the headline 2× suggests.
+
+## Attribution rule — pre-registered, uniform (no post-hoc bands)
+
+Borrowed deliberately from `OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md` §pf-1 decision rule, so an
+environmental/config cause is held to the same bar as a code cause.
+
+- **`Δ_local(fixture)`** = `median(blit) − median(robocopy)` **at equal
+  concurrency**, per fixture, on this rig. It is NOT the 8-vs-1 number.
+- Every hypothesis needs a **wall-time counterfactual**: a variant that removes
+  exactly the accused mechanism, run interleaved against the unmodified build,
+  same rig, same fixture. A hypothesis with no counterfactual **cannot be
+  confirmed** — it is carried as UNTESTED and this plan does not close.
+- Recovery `r` = share of `Δ_local` removed, graded:
+  - `r ≥ 50%` → **CONFIRMED DOMINANT**
+  - `20% ≤ r < 50%` → **CONFIRMED CONTRIBUTING**
+  - `r < 20%` → **KILLED** as a material cause
+- **Overlapping causes are attributed SEQUENTIALLY, never summed** (L1 and L2
+  both remove handle opens; summing would double-count). Grade each solo, then
+  measure the **incremental** recovery of adding it to the already-applied set.
+- Closes only when confirmed contributions account for **≥ 70% of `Δ_local`**.
+  Otherwise the residue is stated in the probe record — never "several
+  hypotheses were consistent, moving on".
+
+## Fix criteria (pre-registered)
+
+- **Bar**: at **equal concurrency**, blit ≤ robocopy on `small` and `mixed`
+  on this rig. (A separate question — whether blit's *default* worker count
+  should change — is a Decision below, not a bar.)
+- **`large` must stay at parity.** The ≥1 MiB `File` payload path
+  (`CopyFileExW` / `clonefile`) is the thing otp-11 D1 fought to protect. Any
+  slice that costs the zero-copy primitive **fails**, regardless of small-file
+  wins.
+- **No remote regression, and no wire change.** The tar shard remains the wire
+  format. A local-carrier fix must be scoped to the local apply path; if a
+  slice would touch code shared with the wire sink, it must prove the remote
+  cells unmoved.
+- **No suite regressions**; floor = the current count. New pins carry guard
+  proofs (temporary revert) per the loop.
+
+## Slices (each through the codex loop; NO code before ls-1 lands)
+
+- **ls-1 (HARD GATE) — attribution, no behavior change.**
+  (a) the equal-concurrency A/B (`ROBO_MT=1`/`BLIT_WORKERS=0` and
+  `ROBO_MT=8`/`BLIT_WORKERS=8`) — establishes `Δ_local` and grades **L3**;
+  (b) build the `0f922de` client natively on netwatch-01 and A/B old-vs-new
+  locally **on Windows**, closing the UNMEASURED gap above;
+  (c) instrument the local apply (behind the existing debug flag): per-file
+  open counts, tar encode/decode time, `create_dir_all` time, so L1/L2/L4 have
+  numbers before anyone edits them.
+  Probe record committed and codex-reviewed **before any fix slice exists**.
+- **ls-2..n** — one fix slice per CONFIRMED cause, smallest change first,
+  A/B'd against the unmodified build on the same rig.
+- **ls-final** — re-run the full local matrix (`large`/`small`/`mixed`) at both
+  concurrencies, plus the remote no-regression check.
+
+## Decisions
+
+1. **Priority — SETTLED (D-2026-07-13-2): BEHIND.** This plan is sequenced
+   behind the ACTIVE `OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md` (MTU experiment → pf-1 → its fix
+   slices). The finding is recorded now; only the *fix* waits. Rationale in the
+   decision entry: the local cost is very unlikely to explain P1 (no initiator
+   axis; a per-file/worker cost cancels between two arms of the same code) or
+   P2 (P2 is *new*, this cost is *old*), while fixing it first would touch code
+   shared with the wire sink and void otp-12's pre-fix baselines.
+   **Carried into pf-1 as a cheap check** — the one way the two could touch: if
+   the unified session changed the **remote receive** worker count the way the
+   local side sits at one (`local.rs:602`), that WOULD be new, per-file, and a
+   live P2 candidate. Read the executed old path; do not assume.
+2. **OPEN — the core design question.** Should the local carrier skip tar
+   framing for small files and copy each one directly (the same primitive
+   `large` already uses), accepting that local and remote payload shapes then
+   differ for small files? **Do not answer this from the current evidence** —
+   see the hypothesis re-ranking below; L1 may not be worth touching at all.
+3. **OPEN — the default worker count.** Local apply ships at one worker
+   (`--workers` is a hidden debug flag). Should the shipped default change?
+
+## ⚠ HYPOTHESIS RE-RANKING (2026-07-13, after the equal-concurrency runs)
+
+The headline "blit is 2× robocopy" was **8-thread robocopy vs 1-worker blit**
+(owner caught it). At **equal concurrency** the picture inverts:
+
+| fixture | blit | robocopy | ratio |
+|---|---:|---:|---:|
+| `small` @ 1 thread | 2225 | 2531 | **0.88 — blit WINS** |
+| `mixed` @ 1 thread | 942 | 1045 | **0.90 — blit WINS** |
+| `small` @ 8 threads | 1331 | 697 | 1.91 — blit loses |
+| `mixed` @ 8 threads | 790 | 502 | 1.57 — blit loses |
+
+Scaling from 8× the concurrency: **blit 1.67×** (small) / 1.19× (mixed);
+**robocopy 3.63×** / 2.08×.
+
+- **L3 (single worker / no scaling) is now the PRIME suspect.** blit does not
+  convert concurrency into throughput.
+- **L1 (tar framing) is DEMOTED.** It cannot be the dominant cost of a tool
+  that *wins* at one thread. It may still be why blit scales badly (a serial
+  tar-build that cannot overlap writes — `pipeline.rs:196-206`), which is an
+  L1×L3 interaction, not L1 alone. `ls-1` must separate them.
+- **⚠ THESE NUMBERS ARE NOT YET TRUSTED.** blit's absolute time is **bi-stable
+  across sessions** — the identical binary and flags measured 1388 ms and
+  2225 ms for `small` in two sessions, flat within each, while `robocopy /MT:8`
+  read 697 ms in both. Working hypothesis: CPU boost/core-parking residency —
+  an 8-thread neighbour leaves the CPU hot, and blit's single-worker,
+  syscall-bound run inherits it, while robocopy at `/MT:8` generates its own
+  load and is immune. **If true, blit's honest single-worker cost is ~2225 ms
+  and the 1388 ms figure was an artifact of what it was benchmarked next to.**
+  A 4-arm single-session run (every arm sharing identical neighbours) is the
+  control; no attribution is made until it lands. Cross-session comparison of
+  blit arms is INVALID until this is explained.
diff --git a/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh b/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh
index 2c211d9..c85f7f9 100755
--- a/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh
+++ b/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh
@@ -54,6 +54,14 @@ SRC_ROOT="${SRC_ROOT:-$WIN_TEST\\bench-module}"
 DEST_BASE="${DEST_BASE:-E:\\blit-local-bench}"
 DEST_DRIVE="${DEST_DRIVE:-E}"
 PREFLIGHT_ONLY="${PREFLIGHT_ONLY:-0}"
+# Concurrency — compare at EQUAL concurrency or the result conflates "blit's
+# per-file path is slow" with "blit ships one apply worker" (owner, 2026-07-13).
+# ROBO_MT=8 + BLIT_WORKERS=0 (the first session) was 8-thread robocopy vs
+# 1-worker blit. Fair pairs: (ROBO_MT=1, BLIT_WORKERS=0) and (ROBO_MT=8,
+# BLIT_WORKERS=8). ROBO_MT=1 is also robocopy's true default — plain
+# `robocopy /E` with no /MT is single-threaded.
+ROBO_MT="${ROBO_MT:-8}"
+BLIT_WORKERS="${BLIT_WORKERS:-0}"   # 0 = blit's shipped default (one worker)
 
 SESSION="$(date +%Y%m%dT%H%M%S)"
 OUT_DIR="${OUT_DIR:-$REPO_ROOT/logs/win_local_ab_$SESSION}"
@@ -94,7 +102,9 @@ preflight() {
     done
     # Stale destination from an interrupted run would be re-used as a warm cache.
     wssh "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force '$DEST_BASE' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" || true
+    local bw="ship-default(1)"; [[ "$BLIT_WORKERS" -gt 0 ]] && bw="--workers $BLIT_WORKERS"
     log "preflight OK  blit=$BLIT_SHA  runs/arm=$RUNS  fixtures=$FIXTURES  D: -> ${DEST_DRIVE}:"
+    log "  CONCURRENCY: robocopy /MT:$ROBO_MT   vs   blit $bw"
 }
 
 stage_runner() {
@@ -105,10 +115,13 @@ stage_runner() {
 }
 
 # One timed run. Sets RUN_MS/RUN_FLUSH/RUN_EXIT/RUN_FILES/RUN_DRAIN/RUN_VALID.
+# ARM_TOOL/ARM_N select the tool and its concurrency for this run.
 RUN_MS=0; RUN_FLUSH=0; RUN_EXIT=0; RUN_FILES=0; RUN_DRAIN=""; RUN_VALID=yes
-one_run() {   # fixture tool tag
-    local w="$1" tool="$2" tag="$3" out
-    out="$(wssh "pwsh -NoProfile -File '$WIN_TEST\\local-ab-run.ps1' -Tool $tool -Src '$SRC_ROOT\\pull_src_$w\\src_$w' -DestRoot '$DEST_BASE\\$tag' -BlitExe '$BLIT_EXE' -DestDrive $DEST_DRIVE" 2>>"$OUT_DIR/err.log" \
+ARM_TOOL=blit; ARM_N=0
+one_run() {   # fixture tool tag   (concurrency comes from ARM_TOOL/ARM_N)
+    local w="$1" tool="$2" tag="$3" out mt=$ROBO_MT bw=$BLIT_WORKERS
+    if [[ "$tool" == robocopy ]]; then mt="$ARM_N"; else bw="$ARM_N"; fi
+    out="$(wssh "pwsh -NoProfile -File '$WIN_TEST\\local-ab-run.ps1' -Tool $tool -Src '$SRC_ROOT\\pull_src_$w\\src_$w' -DestRoot '$DEST_BASE\\$tag' -BlitExe '$BLIT_EXE' -DestDrive $DEST_DRIVE -RoboThreads $mt -BlitWorkers $bw" 2>>"$OUT_DIR/err.log" \
         | tr -d '\r' | sed -n 's/.*R:\([0-9-]*,[0-9-]*,[0-9-]*,[0-9]*,[A-Za-z0-9_-]*\):R.*/\1/p' | head -1)"
     if [[ -z "$out" ]]; then
         RUN_MS=0; RUN_FLUSH=0; RUN_EXIT=99; RUN_FILES=0; RUN_DRAIN="PARSE-FAIL"; RUN_VALID=no
@@ -129,6 +142,55 @@ one_run() {   # fixture tool tag
     RUN_MS=$(( RUN_MS + RUN_FLUSH ))
 }
 
+# --- N-arm interleaved cell (the cross-session bi-stability fix) -------------
+# WHY (found live 2026-07-13): blit's DEFAULT config measured 1388ms for
+# `small` in one session and 2225ms in another — a 60% swing on the same
+# binary, same flags, flat within each session — while robocopy /MT:8 measured
+# 697ms in BOTH. The rig is stable for robocopy and bi-stable for blit, cause
+# unknown. Cross-session comparison is therefore INVALID for blit arms.
+# Fix: put EVERY arm in ONE session, rotating the start arm per slot so no arm
+# systematically follows the same predecessor. Every comparison is then
+# internally controlled and no conclusion crosses a session boundary.
+#
+# ARMS entries: "<label>:<tool>:<n>"  — n = --workers for blit (0 = the SHIPPED
+# default, no flag), /MT:n for robocopy.
+ARMS_SPEC="${ARMS_SPEC:-blit_ship:blit:0,blit_w8:blit:8,robo_mt1:robocopy:1,robo_mt8:robocopy:8}"
+
+run_cell_multi() {   # fixture — all arms interleaved, slot-voided
+    local w="$1" slot=1 attempts=0 valid=0 max=$(( 2 * RUNS ))
+    local -a arms=(); IFS=, read -r -a arms <<< "$ARMS_SPEC"
+    local n_arms=${#arms[@]}
+    log "=== $w ($n_arms arms interleaved, rotating start, $RUNS slots) ==="
+    log "    arms: ${arms[*]}"
+    # One untimed warm-up (absorbs the previous cell's teardown still settling).
+    ARM_TOOL=blit; ARM_N=0
+    one_run "$w" blit "${SESSION}_${w}_warmup" || true
+    log "  $w/warmup (untimed, discarded): ${RUN_MS}ms ($RUN_DRAIN)"
+    while (( valid < RUNS && attempts < max )); do
+        attempts=$(( attempts + 1 ))
+        local slot_valid=yes i idx spec label tool n
+        local -a rows=()
+        for (( i = 0; i < n_arms; i++ )); do
+            idx=$(( (i + slot - 1) % n_arms ))          # rotate the start arm
+            spec="${arms[$idx]}"
+            label="${spec%%:*}"; tool="$(echo "$spec" | cut -d: -f2)"; n="${spec##*:}"
+            ARM_TOOL="$tool"; ARM_N="$n"
+            one_run "$w" "$tool" "${SESSION}_${w}_${label}_s${slot}a${attempts}"
+            [[ "$RUN_VALID" == yes ]] || slot_valid=no
+            rows+=("$w,$label,$slot,$attempts,$RUN_MS,$RUN_FLUSH,$RUN_EXIT,$RUN_FILES,$RUN_DRAIN")
+            log "  $w/$label slot $slot: ${RUN_MS}ms (flush ${RUN_FLUSH}ms, exit $RUN_EXIT, $RUN_FILES files, $RUN_DRAIN)"
+        done
+        local r
+        for r in "${rows[@]}"; do echo "$r,$slot_valid" >> "$CSV"; done
+        if [[ "$slot_valid" == yes ]]; then
+            valid=$(( valid + 1 )); slot=$(( slot + 1 ))
+        else
+            log "  $w: slot $slot VOIDED — re-running the whole slot"
+        fi
+    done
+    (( valid >= RUNS )) || log "  $w INCOMPLETE: $valid/$RUNS valid slots after $attempts attempts"
+}
+
 run_cell() {   # fixture — ABBA over blit/robocopy, pair-void, 2xRUNS cap
     local w="$1" slot=1 attempts=0 valid=0 max=$(( 2 * RUNS ))
     log "=== $w (blit vs robocopy, ABBA, $RUNS pairs) ==="
@@ -174,25 +236,50 @@ for r in rows:
     by.setdefault((r["fixture"], r["tool"]), []).append(int(r["ms"]))
 
 with open(summary_p, "w") as f:
-    f.write("fixture,tool,median_ms,best_ms,spread_pct,n\n")
+    f.write("fixture,arm,median_ms,best_ms,spread_pct,n\n")
     for k in sorted(by):
         v = sorted(by[k])
         spread = round(100.0 * (max(v) - min(v)) / max(min(v), 1), 1)
         f.write(f"{k[0]},{k[1]},{int(st.median(v))},{min(v)},{spread},{len(v)}\n")
 
-print(f"\n{'fixture':8} {'blit':>9} {'robocopy':>9} {'ratio':>7}  verdict")
-print("-" * 48)
-for w in ("large", "small", "mixed"):
-    b, r = by.get((w, "blit")), by.get((w, "robocopy"))
-    if not b or not r:
-        continue
-    bm, rm = st.median(b), st.median(r)
-    ratio = bm / rm
-    verdict = "blit FASTER" if ratio < 1.0 else "blit SLOWER"
-    print(f"{w:8} {bm:8.0f}ms {rm:8.0f}ms {ratio:7.3f}  {verdict}")
-print("\nratio = blit / robocopy  (<1.00 means blit wins)")
-print("Cross-tool wall clock, NOT a controlled protocol comparison.")
+def med(w, arm):
+    v = by.get((w, arm))
+    return st.median(v) if v else None
+
+fixtures = [w for w in ("large", "small", "mixed") if any(k[0] == w for k in by)]
+arms = sorted({k[1] for k in by})
+
+print(f"\n{'fixture':8} " + " ".join(f"{a:>10}" for a in arms))
+print("-" * (9 + 11 * len(arms)))
+for w in fixtures:
+    cells = []
+    for a in arms:
+        m = med(w, a)
+        cells.append(f"{m:9.0f}ms" if m else f"{'-':>11}")
+    print(f"{w:8} " + " ".join(cells))
+
+# The comparisons that matter, each INSIDE one session (no cross-session math).
+print("\n=== EQUAL-CONCURRENCY comparisons (the only fair cross-tool ones) ===")
+for w in fixtures:
+    for bl, ro, tag in (("blit_ship", "robo_mt1", "1 thread "),
+                        ("blit_w8", "robo_mt8", "8 threads")):
+        b, r = med(w, bl), med(w, ro)
+        if b and r:
+            print(f"  {w:6} @{tag}: blit {b:6.0f}ms  robocopy {r:6.0f}ms  "
+                  f"ratio {b/r:5.3f}  {'blit WINS' if b < r else 'blit LOSES'}")
+
+print("\n=== SCALING: what does 8x the workers/threads actually buy each tool? ===")
+for w in fixtures:
+    b1, b8 = med(w, "blit_ship"), med(w, "blit_w8")
+    r1, r8 = med(w, "robo_mt1"), med(w, "robo_mt8")
+    if b1 and b8:
+        print(f"  {w:6} blit     1 -> 8 workers: {b1:6.0f} -> {b8:6.0f}ms  = {b1/b8:4.2f}x speedup")
+    if r1 and r8:
+        print(f"  {w:6} robocopy 1 -> 8 threads: {r1:6.0f} -> {r8:6.0f}ms  = {r1/r8:4.2f}x speedup")
+
+print("\nCross-tool wall clock, NOT a controlled protocol comparison.")
 print("This says NOTHING about P1 — there is no initiator axis in a local copy.")
+print("blit_ship = the SHIPPED default (one apply worker, no --workers flag).")
 PYEOF
 }
 
@@ -201,7 +288,9 @@ main() {
     [[ "$PREFLIGHT_ONLY" == 1 ]] && { log "PREFLIGHT_ONLY: nothing timed"; exit 0; }
     stage_runner
     local w
-    for w in ${FIXTURES//,/ }; do run_cell "$w"; done
+    for w in ${FIXTURES//,/ }; do
+        if [[ "${MULTI:-0}" == 1 ]]; then run_cell_multi "$w"; else run_cell "$w"; fi
+    done
     wssh "Remove-Item -Recurse -Force '$DEST_BASE' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" || true
     summarize | tee -a "$OUT_DIR/bench.log"
     log "runs: $CSV"
diff --git a/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1 b/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1
index 101982e..7674461 100644
--- a/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1
+++ b/scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1
@@ -23,7 +23,20 @@ param(
     [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$DestRoot,   # E:\blit-local-bench\<tag>
     [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$BlitExe,
     [string]$DestDrive = 'E',
-    [string]$PurgeScript = 'D:\blit-test\purge-standby.ps1'
+    [string]$PurgeScript = 'D:\blit-test\purge-standby.ps1',
+    # Concurrency knobs — the two tools must be compared at EQUAL concurrency
+    # or the result conflates "blit's per-file path is slow" with "blit ships
+    # one worker". robocopy /MT:8 vs blit's default single apply worker is NOT
+    # a like-for-like comparison (owner, 2026-07-13).
+    #   RoboThreads : robocopy /MT:N. NOTE robocopy with NO /MT is
+    #                 single-threaded; /MT:8 is what we passed originally.
+    #   BlitWorkers : 0 = ship default (ONE apply worker —
+    #                 transfer_session/local.rs:602). N>0 passes `--workers N`,
+    #                 which sets debug_mode and widens sink_workers to N. That
+    #                 flag's ONLY effect on the local session is the worker
+    #                 count (local.rs:328-332), so it is a clean lever.
+    [int]$RoboThreads = 8,
+    [int]$BlitWorkers = 0
 )
 
 $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
@@ -53,13 +66,18 @@ New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dest | Out-Null
 # --- the timed window -------------------------------------------------------
 $sw = [Diagnostics.Stopwatch]::StartNew()
 if ($Tool -eq 'blit') {
-    & $BlitExe copy $Src $DestRoot --yes > $null 2>&1
+    if ($BlitWorkers -gt 0) {
+        & $BlitExe copy $Src $DestRoot --yes --workers $BlitWorkers > $null 2>&1
+    }
+    else {
+        & $BlitExe copy $Src $DestRoot --yes > $null 2>&1
+    }
     $rc = $LASTEXITCODE
 }
 else {
-    # /MT:8 is robocopy's own default thread count. Retries bounded so a
-    # transient error cannot hang the run for hours on the default /R:1000000.
-    robocopy $Src $dest /E /MT:8 /R:2 /W:2 /NFL /NDL /NJH /NJS > $null 2>&1
+    # Retries bounded so a transient error cannot hang the run for hours on
+    # robocopy's default /R:1000000.
+    robocopy $Src $dest /E /MT:$RoboThreads /R:2 /W:2 /NFL /NDL /NJH /NJS > $null 2>&1
     $rc = $LASTEXITCODE
 }
 $sw.Stop()

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/plan/LOCAL_SMALL_FILE_PATH.md | sed -n '175,235p'" in /Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2
 succeeded in 0ms:
   175	   framing for small files and copy each one directly (the same primitive
   176	   `large` already uses), accepting that local and remote payload shapes then
   177	   differ for small files? **Do not answer this from the current evidence** —
   178	   see the hypothesis re-ranking below; L1 may not be worth touching at all.
   179	3. **OPEN — the default worker count.** Local apply ships at one worker
   180	   (`--workers` is a hidden debug flag). Should the shipped default change?
   181	
   182	## ⚠ HYPOTHESIS RE-RANKING (2026-07-13, after the equal-concurrency runs)
   183	
   184	The headline "blit is 2× robocopy" was **8-thread robocopy vs 1-worker blit**
   185	(owner caught it). At **equal concurrency** the picture inverts:
   186	
   187	| fixture | blit | robocopy | ratio |
   188	|---|---:|---:|---:|
   189	| `small` @ 1 thread | 2225 | 2531 | **0.88 — blit WINS** |
   190	| `mixed` @ 1 thread | 942 | 1045 | **0.90 — blit WINS** |
   191	| `small` @ 8 threads | 1331 | 697 | 1.91 — blit loses |
   192	| `mixed` @ 8 threads | 790 | 502 | 1.57 — blit loses |
   193	
   194	Scaling from 8× the concurrency: **blit 1.67×** (small) / 1.19× (mixed);
   195	**robocopy 3.63×** / 2.08×.
   196	
   197	- **L3 (single worker / no scaling) is now the PRIME suspect.** blit does not
   198	  convert concurrency into throughput.
   199	- **L1 (tar framing) is DEMOTED.** It cannot be the dominant cost of a tool
   200	  that *wins* at one thread. It may still be why blit scales badly (a serial
   201	  tar-build that cannot overlap writes — `pipeline.rs:196-206`), which is an
   202	  L1×L3 interaction, not L1 alone. `ls-1` must separate them.
   203	- **⚠ THESE NUMBERS ARE NOT YET TRUSTED.** blit's absolute time is **bi-stable
   204	  across sessions** — the identical binary and flags measured 1388 ms and
   205	  2225 ms for `small` in two sessions, flat within each, while `robocopy /MT:8`
   206	  read 697 ms in both. Working hypothesis: CPU boost/core-parking residency —
   207	  an 8-thread neighbour leaves the CPU hot, and blit's single-worker,
   208	  syscall-bound run inherits it, while robocopy at `/MT:8` generates its own
   209	  load and is immune. **If true, blit's honest single-worker cost is ~2225 ms
   210	  and the 1388 ms figure was an artifact of what it was benchmarked next to.**
   211	  A 4-arm single-session run (every arm sharing identical neighbours) is the
   212	  control; no attribution is made until it lands. Cross-session comparison of
   213	  blit arms is INVALID until this is explained.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/bench/win-local-ab-2026-07-13/README.md | sed -n '1,180p'" in /Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# blit vs robocopy — LOCAL D: → E: on netwatch-01 (2026-07-13)
     2	
     3	**Status**: Evidence (recorded). **This README declares nothing** — it records
     4	what was measured. Adjudication belongs to the owner.
     5	
     6	**Why**: owner request — a local-only A/B on the Windows box. It strips the
     7	network out entirely: no MTU, no MSS, no initiator layout, no daemon, no
     8	carrier, no wire. If blit trails robocopy *here*, the problem was never the
     9	wire.
    10	
    11	**Harness**: `scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh` + `scripts/windows/local-ab-run.ps1`.
    12	**blit**: `D:\blit-test\bins\f35702a\blit.exe` (embed-verified `+f35702a`).
    13	`f35702a` is the SHIPPING transfer code — the only delta `f35702a..HEAD` is
    14	`bb28ddd` (cargo fmt on `blit-app/endpoints.rs` + a test), and otp-11 (local
    15	rides the unified session) is already **in** `f35702a`.
    16	
    17	**Rig**: D: = disk#0 and E: = disk#3 — two **separate, identical** Crucial
    18	T705 4 TB NVMe drives. No read/write contention on one device; neither side of
    19	the copy is a bottleneck the other lacks.
    20	
    21	**Method** (identical to the blit rig harnesses; anything less is not
    22	comparable): cold caches every run (standby purge), writeback **drained**
    23	before the window opens, fresh never-seen destination per run, destination
    24	container precreated **outside** the window on both arms, durability keyed by
    25	the **destination volume** (`Write-VolumeCache -DriveLetter E`, self-timed,
    26	added to wall time), ABBA interleave, pair-void, nonzero exit voids the run,
    27	and the landed file count is verified per run (a tool that "succeeds" while
    28	writing nothing cannot score a fast time).
    29	
    30	## Result — two independent sessions agree
    31	
    32	RUNS=8, medians in ms (transfer + destination flush):
    33	
    34	| fixture | shape | blit | robocopy | ratio | reading |
    35	|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|
    36	| `large` | 1 × 1 GiB | **539** | 541 | **0.996** | parity |
    37	| `mixed` | 512 MiB + 5000 × 2 KiB | **934** | 501 | **1.863** | blit ~1.9× slower |
    38	| `small` | 10 000 × 4 KiB | **1388** | 697 | **1.991** | blit ~2× slower |
    39	
    40	Replicated at RUNS=4 in a prior session (`summary-runs4-biased.csv`; see the
    41	ordering-bias note below): 0.991 / 1.877 / 2.024.
    42	
    43	**blit matches robocopy byte-for-byte on bulk throughput and costs ~2× the
    44	moment there are many files.** With no network in the picture, that is a
    45	per-file cost inside blit.
    46	
    47	## What this does NOT say
    48	
    49	- **It says nothing about P1.** A local copy has no initiator axis — there is
    50	  no "who dials" to vary. P1 is an initiator-invariance failure and cannot be
    51	  observed here.
    52	- **It is not an otp-11 regression.** otp-11's own local gate already A/B'd
    53	  old-vs-new blit locally (`docs/bench/otp11-local-2026-07-11/`): `small`
    54	  (10 000 × 4 KiB) went **1684 ms → 1750 ms, +3.9% PASS**. The unified session
    55	  did not introduce this cost; it predates otp-11.
    56	- **It is not P2.** P2 is a *new-vs-old blit* regression on the TCP push path.
    57	  Old blit carries this local cost too (per the otp-11 gate above).
    58	- **It is cross-tool**, so it is NOT a controlled protocol comparison —
    59	  robocopy is a plain Win32 copy loop; blit rides the unified
    60	  `transfer_session` (local included, since otp-11). This is "what a user
    61	  experiences with each tool", which is the SHIPPING bar, not a mechanism
    62	  attribution.
    63	
    64	## Known gaps (stated, not hidden)
    65	
    66	- **The old blit client was never staged on Windows** — only
    67	  `bins\0f922de\blit-daemon.exe` exists (the otp-12 sessions ran the old
    68	  *client* from the Mac). So "old blit is also ~2× robocopy **on Windows**" is
    69	  an **INFERENCE** from the Mac otp-11 gate, **not a measurement on this box**.
    70	  Closing it requires a native `0f922de` client build on netwatch-01.
    71	- **A cold-allocation outlier survives, and it is a lead, not noise.** blit's
    72	  warm-up (2253 ms) *and* first timed run (2175 ms) in the `small` cell are
    73	  slow; runs 2-8 settle hard at 1386-1430. Robocopy shows the same effect far
    74	  more weakly (742 → ~690). **blit is disproportionately sensitive to cold
    75	  NTFS destination-allocation state (+57% vs robocopy's +8%).** The outlier is
    76	  KEPT in `runs.csv` (medians are robust to it; it inflates `small/blit`
    77	  `spread_pct` to 57.3%). It may itself be informative and is recorded rather
    78	  than tuned away.
    79	- **Ordering bias, found and fixed mid-session.** The first harness revision
    80	  had no warm-up, and ABBA fixes `blit` as slot 1's first arm — so the
    81	  previous cell's teardown (a 10k-file delete still settling past the drain)
    82	  was charged to blit in *every* cell. `summary-runs4-biased.csv` is that
    83	  biased run, kept for the record: it inflated `mixed/blit` spread to 43.2%.
    84	  An untimed discarded warm-up run per cell now absorbs it (`mixed` spread
    85	  43.2% → 4.2%). The medians moved <1% and the conclusion was unaffected.
    86	
    87	## Relevance to the ACTIVE plan (`docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md`)
    88	
    89	Not adjudicated here — surfaced for the owner:
    90	
    91	- **H7** accuses HEAD's per-entry bookkeeping (a mutex-protected sent-manifest
    92	  map + a per-need event-channel hop, `transfer_session/mod.rs:1038,:1123,:1350`)
    93	  — **per-file, carrier-independent, shared by both carriers**. Since otp-11,
    94	  local copies ride that same unified session. A network-free local
    95	  reproduction is what H7 predicts.
    96	- pf-1's Method proposes reproducing on "two-daemon in-process rigs on the
    97	  Mac". This local rig is cheaper (~10 min), has no network confounds, and no
    98	  initiator axis to muddy attribution.
    99	- **But note the tension**: the otp-11 gate says old ≈ new locally, while H7
   100	  accuses code that is *new*. If both hold, the local cost is NOT H7 — it is
   101	  older than the unified session, and H7 must be tested against P2's *network*
   102	  gap, not against this. That contradiction is not resolved by this evidence.
   103	
   104	## Files
   105	
   106	`runs.csv` (48 timed runs, RUNS=8), `summary.csv`,
   107	`summary-runs4-biased.csv` (the pre-warm-up session, kept as the record of the
   108	ordering bias).

diff --git a/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh b/scripts/bench_win_local_ab.sh
